Highlights

  • Cognizant and Google Cloud Partner to Bring Agentic AI to Critical Retail Moments (April 22, 2026)

    Cognizant and Google Cloud announced a strategic initiative to deploy agentic AI for retail, powered by the Gemini Enterprise platform. This collaboration is designed to automate and enhance complex customer and operational retail interactions, bringing together Cognizant’s transformation know-how with Gemini’s orchestration of autonomous agents. For Workday clients, this raises the benchmark for expected automation quality and signals intense competition for both horizontal (HR/Finance) and vertical (retail-specific) workflows. Deloitte’s practice must proactively connect Workday with agentic platforms to show future-proofed solutioning. Read more

  • Google Deepens Commitment to the Agentic Enterprise with $750M Investment (April 22, 2026)

    Google’s $750 million investment to expand Gemini Enterprise and the agentic AI ecosystem is a powerful statement about enterprise AI’s future. This infusion will build out technology, enablement, and R&D around Gemini-based agent orchestration. For SaaS leaders like Workday and consulting partners like Deloitte, the bar for agentic interoperability and intelligent automation is rising sharply. Clients will note these moves and expect a similar acceleration in their HR/finance transformation agendas. Explore the details

  • Google Places Gemini Enterprise at the Center of Enterprise Agentic Taskforces (April 22, 2026)

    Positioned during Next '26 announcements, Google Cloud is rearchitecting enterprise process automation around Gemini Enterprise, putting multi-agent 'taskforces' front and center for mission-critical workflows. This push emphasizes orchestration across business domains and promises deeper agent ecosystem integration, including with HR/Finance leaders like Workday. Workday ecosystem partners must rapidly adapt to this new orchestration paradigm to deliver sustained value to clients. See full report

Competitive Watch

  • Google Cloud & Cognizant: Jointly targeting agentic AI scale for retail with Gemini Enterprise—potentially extending benchmark standards to other industries. Workday and Deloitte must respond with rapid enablement, integration, and joint GTM to stay relevant. Details
  • Google Cloud: Massive funding surge ($750M) for Gemini and agentic partner growth increases the pace of innovation—Workday must ensure parity or advantage in agent interoperability and workflow automation. See more
  • Google Cloud: Repositioning Gemini Enterprise as the default backbone for business process 'taskforces'—driving the need for deep Workday integration into agent networks. More info

Enablement

  • Agentic AI Platform Integration: Leverage the new Cognizant–Google agentic blueprint to build integrated Workday–Gemini Enterprise solutions. Resource
  • Competitive Benchmarking: Agentic Ecosystem: Use Google’s $750M ecosystem push to refine Deloitte advisories and client playbooks on agentic integration. Resource
  • Multi-Agent Orchestration for HR/Finance: Adopt Gemini Enterprise taskforce frameworks for scalable HR and finance process automation. Resource

Actions for Next Week

  • Brief clients on Cognizant–Google Cloud developments and Google's ecosystem investment; outline Workday integration opportunities.
  • Accelerate enablement and documentation of Gemini Enterprise integration patterns with Workday modules.
  • Schedule joint design workshops on cross-ecosystem agentic pilot projects for HR/finance.
  • Update Deloitte competitive playbooks to reflect agentic ecosystem advances.

Risks & Mitigations

  • Risk: Agentic AI pace may outstrip Workday’s ecosystem integration; clients may choose non-Workday automation layers.
    Mitigation: Prioritize strategic R&D and partnerships for seamless agent–Workday connectivity.
  • Risk: Multi-agent process automation can create governance/data integrity problems across systems.
    Mitigation: Offer agentic governance and monitoring frameworks as part of Deloitte’s advisory services.

All Sources

  • Cognizant and Google Cloud Bring Agentic AI to Retail's Most Critical Customer Moments with Gemini Enterprise - Apr 22, 2026
  • Google Cloud invests $750M to fuel the agentic enterprise - SiliconANGLE
  • Google puts Gemini Enterprise at the heart of the new agentic taskforce — SiliconANGLE

- TODAY_ET = 2026-04-23

RESEARCH SCOPE
Focus on Workday and its ecosystem — Workday HCM, Workday Extend, Workday Skills Cloud, Workday AI Marketplace, and all agentic or autonomous AI capabilities in SaaS solutions. 
Include:
- Workday product developments, patents, partnerships, and leadership commentary.
- Competitive analysis (SAP, Oracle, ServiceNow, UKG, ADP, Dayforce, etc.).
- GSI & boutique consultancy perspectives (Deloitte, Accenture, EY, KPMG, PwC, IBM, Capgemini, Infosys, TCS, Wipro, Cognizant, Kainos, Invisors, TopBloc, OSV, Alight, Mercer, etc.).
- Ecosystem vendors and hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP, Salesforce).
- Analyst & media insights (Gartner, Forrester, IDC, Reuters, Bloomberg, VentureBeat, etc.).
- Emerging risks, governance, and responsible AI developments affecting SaaS/HR technology.

WRITING EXPECTATIONS
- For each cited source, provide **multi-paragraph summaries (150–250 words)** that explain:
  - What was announced, found, or claimed.
  - Why it matters strategically, technically, and competitively.
  - Implications for Workday clients and Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Incorporate synthesis across related articles (e.g., “Across multiple GSIs…” or “Several sources indicate…”).
- Prioritize insights, nuance, and context over mere headlines.
- Every key claim must cite a valid source URL from the provided SOURCE LIST.
- If no credible items are found in the last 24h, produce an honest “no significant updates” section.

FORMATTING RULES
- Return JSON only (no Markdown or prose outside the object).
- `html_body` must be valid, minimal HTML (<h2>, <h3>, <p>, <ul>, <li>, <a href="...">).
- Section order: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Keep total HTML under 35 KB.
- `plain_text_body` must be readable text with visible URLs.

Return JSON ONLY in this exact shape (no code fences, no extra text):

{
  "type": "daily or weekly",
  "run_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "title": "Short headline",
  "priority_focus": "1–2 sentences on what matters most now",
  "highlights": [
    { "headline": "…", "why_it_matters": "…", "source_url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "competitive_watch": [
    { "competitor": "Name", "move": "…", "implication": "…" }
  ],
  "enablement": [
    { "skill": "Topic", "resource_url": "https://…", "90_day_outcome": "…" }
  ],
  "actions_next_week": ["…"],
  "risks": [
    { "risk": "…", "mitigation": "…" }
  ],
  "sources": [
    { "title": "…", "url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "html_body": "<h2>…</h2> (well-formatted HTML with <a href="https://…">links</a>; no scripts/external CSS)",
  "plain_text_body": "Text-only with visible URLs"
}

Parameters:
- Set "run_date" to TODAY_ET = 2026-04-23.
- For DAILY: set "type":"daily"; target ~1000 words; STRICTLY restrict to the last 24 hours (from 2026-04-22T07:49 to 2026-04-23T07:49); include publication dates in highlight text.
- For WEEKLY: set "type":"weekly"; target 2000–2500 words; restrict to 2026-04-16…2026-04-23; include <h3>What changed this week</h3> and publication dates in highlight text.
- Section order for html_body: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Every item must include at least one absolute URL (https://…).
- Keep total HTML under ~25KB.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
- For every highlight and competitive_watch item, include a 2–3 paragraph detailed summary within the "why_it_matters" text.
- Where relevant, compare perspectives across multiple sources.
- Include a clear "so what" statement for Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Ensure all cited URLs appear in the `sources` array and inside the `html_body` link text.
- Keep factual accuracy and avoid speculation.

{

        "content": "{\n  \"type\": \"daily\",\n  \"run_date\": \"2026-04-23\",\n  \"title\": \"Google and Cognizant Accelerate Agentic AI; Ecosystem Implications for Workday\",\n  \"priority_focus\": \"A major partnership between Cognizant and Google Cloud was announced (April 22, 2026) to drive agentic AI in retail at scale using Gemini Enterprise. This signals intense competitive movement among hyperscalers and GSIs towards AI-powered automation and agentic workflows—strategically relevant for Workday clients investing in HR and finance automation.\",\n  \"highlights\": [\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Cognizant and Google Cloud Partner to Bring Agentic AI to Critical Retail Moments (April 22, 2026)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"Cognizant and Google Cloud jointly announced a strategic partnership to deliver advanced agentic AI solutions for the retail sector, leveraging Google’s Gemini Enterprise platform. The offer focuses on automating customer experience and operational workflows using cutting-edge agentic AI, with Cognizant bringing deep domain expertise and scale in retail transformation. \\n\\nTechnically, the partnership leverages Gemini Enterprise’s native ability to orchestrate multiple agents that communicate contextually and act autonomously across disparate cloud and on-prem systems. This enables retailers to address customer needs more quickly and accurately, supporting inventory management, personalization, and real-time responses. The integration of Gemini Enterprise with Cognizant's global delivery teams creates a competitive force that can quickly shape enterprise adoption patterns.\\n\\nFor Workday clients and Deloitte’s Workday practice, this collaboration exemplifies how top GSIs and hyperscalers are raising the bar for agentic automation—signaling faster deployment, richer integrations, and broader domain reach. Workday users should closely monitor these partnerships, as they shape buyer expectations for intelligent process automation not just in retail, but across all sectors. Partners like Deloitte can differentiate by accelerating integrations between Workday and evolving agent platforms, while shaping responsible adoption strategies.\\n\\n(See: https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise)\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Google Deepens Commitment to the Agentic Enterprise with $750M Investment (April 22, 2026)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"Google Cloud made headlines by dedicating $750 million to fuel agentic enterprise initiatives, prioritizing the expansion of Gemini Enterprise and the partner ecosystem. Announced in tandem with Next ’26, this investment will accelerate R&D, go-to-market efforts, and partner enablement for organizations seeking to leverage AI agents for transformative business outcomes.\\n\\nStrategically, the sheer scale of the investment demonstrates Google's conviction that agentic AI is the central competitive arena for the next wave of enterprise software and platform innovation. It signals that the landscape for SaaS, HCM, and operational automation is shifting; AI agents are now table stakes for digital transformation in HR, finance, and other business functions. Google is betting on wide adoption of agent-based orchestration across industries—a clear opportunity (and challenge) for Workday to ensure its platform, AI Marketplace, and agent integrations remain ahead of the curve.\\n\\nFor Deloitte’s Workday practice, this funding surge intensifies the need to maintain technical fluency with the Gemini agentic stack and deep partnerships within Google’s ecosystem. It may also accelerate client demand for pragmatic blueprints that bring together Workday, Gemini, and industry-specific agents. Competitive pressure is rising: clients will expect AI-driven automation to perform at a higher, more autonomous standard.\\n\\n(See: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext)\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Google Places Gemini Enterprise at the Center of Enterprise Agentic Taskforces (April 22, 2026)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"At Next '26, Google Cloud positioned its Gemini Enterprise as the platform of choice for building and deploying autonomous agent 'taskforces'. The update highlights how Gemini Enterprise now supports complex workflows, multi-tool orchestration, and more seamless integration with major partner solutions, including those needed in HR, finance, and supply chain environments.\\n\\nTechnically, this new positioning accentuates agentic AI’s ability to handle complex, cross-functional business tasks previously managed by teams of specialist workers or traditional automation scripts. Gemini Enterprise now provides richer developer tooling, agent marketplaces, and templates aimed at accelerating agent adoption in core business operations. For SaaS ecosystem players like Workday, this model demonstrates both a challenge and an integration opportunity—there is increased competitive pressure, but also a clear path to joint-value when interfacing with Gemini-driven taskforces.\\n\\nFor Deloitte and its Workday clients, the message is unambiguous: successful automation in HR and finance will increasingly depend on connecting Workday’s platform, data, and processes with these new forms of multi-agent orchestration. Partners should prepare clients for a world where Workday is not just a central system of record, but a node in a larger, agent-powered ecosystem.\\n\\n(See: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation)\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"competitive_watch\": [\n    {\n      \"competitor\": \"Google Cloud & Cognizant\",\n      \"move\": \"Announced a significant partnership targeted at the retail sector to operationalize agentic AI at scale using Gemini Enterprise, putting emphasis on automating customer-critical moments.\",\n      \"implication\": \"By combining Google’s advanced AI and Cognizant’s large-scale delivery capability, the partnership could rapidly set new benchmarks for agentic automation across industries. For Workday and Deloitte, there's an urgent need to amplify ecosystem partnerships and agentic AI capabilities to remain competitive as client expectations evolve toward intelligent, cross-system automation. (https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise)\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"competitor\": \"Google Cloud\",\n      \"move\": \"Invested $750 million into expanding Gemini Enterprise and the agentic enterprise ecosystem, signaling an aggressive acceleration of the agentic future.\",\n      \"implication\": \"This level of investment directly challenges the pace and scale at which incumbent SaaS players, including Workday, must innovate on agentic frameworks and integrations. Deloitte must be ready to guide clients through platform selection, integration blueprints, and rapid adoption models that recognize Google’s rapid march in both AI tooling and go-to-market execution. HR/Finance\",\n      \"resource_url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\",\n      \"90_day_outcome\": \"Build, test, and document HR and finance process automations that utilize Workday as a system of record but operate through task-oriented agentic taskforces for scale and resilience.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"actions_next_week\": [\n    \"Brief clients on the implications of the Cognizant–Google Cloud partnership and Google's $750M investment, focusing on integration roadmaps with Workday.\",\n    \"Accelerate internal upskilling on Gemini Enterprise agentic platform and document joint integration patterns relevant for Workday HCM and Finance.\",\n    \"Schedule workshops with key accounts to co-design cross-ecosystem automation pilots leveraging both Workday and agentic AI.\",\n    \"Refine Deloitte’s competitive playbooks to explicitly address new standards for agentic automation set by hyperscaler-GSI partnerships.\"\n  ],\n  \"risks\": [\n    {\n      \"risk\": \"Rapid agentic AI investment by competitors could outpace Workday platform interoperability if clients pursue non-Workday-native automation paths.\",\n      \"mitigation\": \"Commit to ongoing R&D on seamless API, eventing, and agent orchestration between Workday and leading agent platforms; foster alliances to jointly set integration standards.\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"risk\": \"Rising complexity and fragmented accountability with multi-agent taskforces may expose clients to governance and data integrity challenges.\",\n      \"mitigation\": \"Offer consultative services focused on agentic ecosystem governance, robust monitoring, and best-in-class controls for cross-platform automation involving Workday data.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"sources\": [\n    {\n      \"title\": \"Cognizant and Google Cloud Bring Agentic AI to Retail's Most Critical Customer Moments with Gemini Enterprise - Apr 22, 2026\",\n      \"url\": \"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"title\": \"Google Cloud invests $750M to fuel the agentic enterprise - SiliconANGLE\",\n      \"url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"title\": \"Google puts Gemini Enterprise at the heart of the new agentic taskforce — SiliconANGLE\",\n      \"url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"html_body\": \"<h2>Highlights</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Cognizant and Google Cloud Partner to Bring Agentic AI to Critical Retail Moments (April 22, 2026)</strong><br /><p>Cognizant and Google Cloud announced a strategic initiative to deploy agentic AI for retail, powered by the Gemini Enterprise platform. This collaboration is designed to automate and enhance complex customer and operational retail interactions, bringing together Cognizant’s transformation know-how with Gemini’s orchestration of autonomous agents. For Workday clients, this raises the benchmark for expected automation quality and signals intense competition for both horizontal (HR/Finance) and vertical (retail-specific) workflows. Deloitte’s practice must proactively connect Workday with agentic platforms to show future-proofed solutioning. <a href=\\\"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\\">Read more</a></p></li>\\n<li><strong>Google Deepens Commitment to the Agentic Enterprise with $750M Investment (April 22, 2026)</strong><br /><p>Google’s $750 million investment to expand Gemini Enterprise and the agentic AI ecosystem is a powerful statement about enterprise AI’s future. This infusion will build out technology, enablement, and R&D around Gemini-based agent orchestration. For SaaS leaders like Workday and consulting partners like Deloitte, the bar for agentic interoperability and intelligent automation is rising sharply. Clients will note these moves and expect a similar acceleration in their HR/finance transformation agendas. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\\">Explore the details</a></p></li>\\n<li><strong>Google Places Gemini Enterprise at the Center of Enterprise Agentic Taskforces (April 22, 2026)</strong><br /><p>Positioned during Next '26 announcements, Google Cloud is rearchitecting enterprise process automation around Gemini Enterprise, putting multi-agent 'taskforces' front and center for mission-critical workflows. This push emphasizes orchestration across business domains and promises deeper agent ecosystem integration, including with HR/Finance leaders like Workday. Workday ecosystem partners must rapidly adapt to this new orchestration paradigm to deliver sustained value to clients. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\\">See full report</a></p></li>\\n</ul>\\n<h2>Competitive Watch</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Google Cloud & Cognizant: </strong>Jointly targeting agentic AI scale for retail with Gemini Enterprise—potentially extending benchmark standards to other industries. Workday and Deloitte must respond with rapid enablement, integration, and joint GTM to stay relevant. <a href=\\\"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\\">Details</a></li>\\n<li><strong>Google Cloud: </strong>Massive funding surge ($750M) for Gemini and agentic partner growth increases the pace of innovation—Workday must ensure parity or advantage in agent interoperability and workflow automation. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\\">See more</a></li>\\n<li><strong>Google Cloud: </strong>Repositioning Gemini Enterprise as the default backbone for business process 'taskforces'—driving the need for deep Workday integration into agent networks. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\\">More info</a></li>\\n</ul>\\n<h2>Enablement</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Agentic AI Platform Integration:</strong> Leverage the new Cognizant–Google agentic blueprint to build integrated Workday–Gemini Enterprise solutions. <a href=\\\"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\\">Resource</a></li>\\n<li><strong>Competitive Benchmarking: Agentic Ecosystem:</strong> Use Google’s $750M ecosystem push to refine Deloitte advisories and client playbooks on agentic integration. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\\">Resource</a></li>\\n<li><strong>Multi-Agent Orchestration for HR/Finance:</strong> Adopt Gemini Enterprise taskforce frameworks for scalable HR and finance process automation. <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\\">Resource</a></li>\\n</ul>\\n<h2>Actions for Next Week</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li>Brief clients on Cognizant–Google Cloud developments and Google's ecosystem investment; outline Workday integration opportunities.</li>\\n<li>Accelerate enablement and documentation of Gemini Enterprise integration patterns with Workday modules.</li>\\n<li>Schedule joint design workshops on cross-ecosystem agentic pilot projects for HR/finance.</li>\\n<li>Update Deloitte competitive playbooks to reflect agentic ecosystem advances.</li>\\n</ul>\\n<h2>Risks &amp; Mitigations</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Risk:</strong> Agentic AI pace may outstrip Workday’s ecosystem integration; clients may choose non-Workday automation layers.<br /><strong>Mitigation:</strong> Prioritize strategic R&D and partnerships for seamless agent–Workday connectivity.</li>\\n<li><strong>Risk:</strong> Multi-agent process automation can create governance/data integrity problems across systems.<br /><strong>Mitigation:</strong> Offer agentic governance and monitoring frameworks as part of Deloitte’s advisory services.</li>\\n</ul>\\n<h2>All Sources</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\\">Cognizant and Google Cloud Bring Agentic AI to Retail's Most Critical Customer Moments with Gemini Enterprise - Apr 22, 2026</a></li>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\\">Google Cloud invests $750M to fuel the agentic enterprise - SiliconANGLE</a></li>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\\">Google puts Gemini Enterprise at the heart of the new agentic taskforce — SiliconANGLE</a></li>\\n</ul>\",\n  \"plain_text_body\": \"Highlights\\n- Cognizant and Google Cloud Partner to Bring Agentic AI to Critical Retail Moments (April 22, 2026)\\nCognizant and Google Cloud announced a new partnership to deliver agentic AI solutions at scale for the retail sector using the Gemini Enterprise platform. The partnership automates and orchestrates customer experiences and operations with multi-agent systems. This raises the standard for automation in HR and finance as well, challenging Workday and Deloitte to innovate integration and orchestration solutions. More: https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\n\\n- Google Deepens Commitment to the Agentic Enterprise with $750M Investment (April 22, 2026)\\nGoogle Cloud is investing $750 million to accelerate its Gemini Enterprise agentic AI ecosystem. The scale of this investment shows Google’s belief that agentic AI will dominate enterprise automation and SaaS evolution. For Workday users, it's a signal that agent-based automation is now expected, especially in HR and finance. Deloitte clients will require guidance on integrating Workday with emerging agentic platforms. See: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\n\\n- Google Places Gemini Enterprise at the Center of Enterprise Agentic Taskforces (April 22, 2026)\\nGoogle Cloud, at Next '26, announced Gemini Enterprise as the platform for multi-agent taskforces, enabling complex process automation across business domains. This sets a new competitive bar for SaaS platforms such as Workday and demands that Deloitte help clients connect Workday data and workflows to agentic ecosystems. Info: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\n\\nCompetitive Watch\\n- Google Cloud & Cognizant: Their joint move on agentic automation for retail may set standards for all sectors; Workday and Deloitte must respond with integration and value-add. https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\n- Google Cloud: $750M investment increases pressure on Workday to maintain agentic AI integration. https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext \\n- Google Cloud: Gemini Enterprise as agentic taskforce hub may become industry default for complex automations; Workday integration is a must. https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\n\\nEnablement\\n- Agentic AI Platform Integration: Study Cognizant-Google joint reference and build Workday–Gemini blueprints. https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\n- Competitive Benchmarking: Use Google’s $750M commitment for Deloitte playbook updates. https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\n- Multi-Agent Orchestration for HR/Finance: Test Gemini taskforce automation integrating Workday modules. https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\\n\\nActions for Next Week\\n- Brief clients on latest Cognizant–Google Cloud news and its implications for Workday integration.\\n- Accelerate Gemini Enterprise enablement within Deloitte’s Workday teams.\\n- Workshop cross-ecosystem agentic pilots for HR/finance automation.\\n- Update competitive playbooks with new agentic benchmarks from hyperscaler-GSI partnerships.\\n\\nRisks & Mitigations\\n- Risk: Workday interoperability may fall behind agentic AI advances if not prioritized; clients may use non-Workday options.\\nMitigation: Increase R&D for deep Workday-agentic platform connectivity via APIs and partnerships.\\n- Risk: Multi-agent workflow complexity can cause governance/data quality issues across systems.\\nMitigation: Provide agent governance and monitoring consultancy alongside Workday services.\\n\\nAll Sources\\n- Cognizant and Google Cloud Bring Agentic AI to Retail's Most Critical Customer Moments with Gemini Enterprise - Apr 22, 2026: https://news.cognizant.com/2026-04-22-Cognizant-and-Google-Cloud-Bring-Agentic-AI-to-Retails-Most-Critical-Customer-Moments-with-Gemini-Enterprise\\n- Google Cloud invests $750M to fuel the agentic enterprise - SiliconANGLE: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext\\n- Google puts Gemini Enterprise at the heart of the new agentic taskforce — SiliconANGLE: https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation\"\n}",

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available | Workday Aus & NZ",
      "content": "This is where ASOR comes in. Not only does it let you see all of your enterprise agents in one unified view, it deeply integrates all of them with Workday. With ASOR, agents become part of an organization’s workforce strategy—measured like investments, governed like employees, and improved by training and learning. \n\n“The Workday Agent System of Record gives our customers clarity, so they can manage AI with the same discipline and confidence they manage people, finance, and operations.”\n\n## A Single System for a Blended Workforce\n\nFor decades, a core principle of Workday HCM and Workday Financial Management has been “you can’t manage what you can’t see.” This becomes even more critical when you add AI agents into the mix. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] With a unique understanding of how AI agents interact with business processes, tasks, and reporting data, ASOR brings insights to process efficiency, ROI, and total business impact. This helps leaders measure and assess the value that AI agents provide, so they can refocus employees on the highest value work.\n\n“The real value of Workday’s Agent System of Record is the ability to capture analytics across an ecosystem of agents,” said Claudio Valera, PwC global and U.S. Workday alliance leader. “By bringing insights from thousands of use cases into a single system, you’re able to clearly see how your human-machine teams are performing. This visibility enables teams to innovate more effectively and scale AI with confidence.”\n\n## Built for Accountability and Governance",

Learn More\n\n### Finance\n\nUse AI to optimize transactions and processes, deliver data-driven insights, and generate value across the enterprise.\n\nFinancial Management   Spend Management   Financial Planning   All Finance [...] Seamlessly manage all your HR and finance needs on an open, unified platform with AI at the core. Explore frontline workforce solutions.\n\nLearn More\n\nBetter work days start here.\n\nWorkday Sana is the AI experience platform that lets you harness the knowledge of your entire enterprise.\n\nLearn More\n\nThe all-in-one solution for your midsize business.\n\nDiscover how Workday GO simplifies your operations by bringing HR, finance, and payroll into one integrated platform.\n\nLearn More\n\nPUMA simplified HR operations and enabled smarter decisions with AI-powered tools.       "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection [...] Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation\n\n  # While the EU AI Act Stalls, Workday Leads on Trusted, Compliant AI\n\n  Workday champions a strong, workable EU AI Act while already operating ahead of the curve with independently verified governance that defines the standard for trusted AI.\n\n   \n\n  Rich Sauer\n\n  LATEST\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community   Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners",

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available | Workday Aus & NZ",
      "content": "## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] This is where ASOR comes in. Not only does it let you see all of your enterprise agents in one unified view, it deeply integrates all of them with Workday. With ASOR, agents become part of an organization’s workforce strategy—measured like investments, governed like employees, and improved by training and learning. \n\n“The Workday Agent System of Record gives our customers clarity, so they can manage AI with the same discipline and confidence they manage people, finance, and operations.”\n\n## A Single System for a Blended Workforce\n\nFor decades, a core principle of Workday HCM and Workday Financial Management has been “you can’t manage what you can’t see.” This becomes even more critical when you add AI agents into the mix. [...] “In a world where AI agents are enabling teams to augment daily work and deliver better results, interoperability amongst agents in different ecosystems is paramount,” said Ankur Jain, director of product management, Adobe Experience Cloud Ecosystem. “Through the Workday Agent System of Record, we can help ensure seamless implementation for joint enterprise customers and provide teams greater agility in driving value with agentic AI.”\n\n## Managing What Comes Next\n\nThe shift to a blended workforce is happening now. Business leaders need more than individual tools. They need a system of record that supports agents throughout their entire lifecycle.",
      "score": 0.53590065,
      "raw_content": null

      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] ## Innovation Without Compromise\n\n  In these two episodes of The Innovation Exchange podcast, Workday’s Mark Woollen speaks with leaders from PwC and Relish about how partner-built innovations are helping organizations move faster without sacrificing trust, compliance, or control.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n two-people-smiling-at-laptop-devcon-logo-with-gradient \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## Workday DevCon 2026: Going Agentic, Building the Future\n\n  At Workday DevCon, developers solve real problems and build solutions that matter.       "url": "https://kpmg.com/au/en/why-kpmg/alliances/workday/workday-student.html",
      "title": "Workday Student",
      "content": "Rapid advances in technology are offering universities an ideal opportunity to improve their student experiences and reshape operations. Even as demographics shift, enrolment patterns evolve and legacy systems strain under modern demands, institutions have a powerful chance to reimagine how they support staff and enable students.\n\n## What is Workday Student?\n\nWorkday Student, co created with leading universities, is a modern, mobile first, end to end student administration platform that removes silos by unifying academic, financial, Human Capital Management (HCM) and operational processes into a single cloud native, AI fuelled system.\n\n### KPMG’s trusted approach [...] The digital backpack isn’t just about today; it’s about thriving in the age of disruption. AI-driven insights, predictive analytics, dynamic course management and autonomous workflows will redefine how universities engage students and staff and manage operations. With KPMG and Workday, you can lead this change with confidence.\n\n## Benefits of a digital backpack for universities\n\nWorkday supplies the platform, KPMG makes it work for you.\n\n### A single, connected student operating model [...] Workday services partner logo services partner\nWorkday services partner logo advisory partner\nWorkday services partner logo AMS partner\nWorkday extend partner logo\n\n## How KPMG + Workday Student can help transform the student experience confidently\n\nKPMG and Workday help universities bring HCM, finance and student operations together into one cloud native, AI ready ecosystem. By combining KPMG’s pre-built models, accelerators and sector experience with Workday Student’s intelligent platform, institutions can transform their student experience at the front and back ends while accessing new efficiencies and reduced risk and costs.",

      "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147288BR",
      "title": "Program Manager-Workday HCM-Denmark - Copenhagen Denmark - Careers | Infosys",
      "content": "With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem. [...] Own the end-to-end delivery of Workday HCM implementations, transformations, and optimizations\n Translate HR strategy and workforce objectives into executable HCM roadmaps\n Govern multiple Workday HCM initiatives across regions and business units\n Drive standardization, process optimization, and employee experience improvements\n Ensure compliance with local labor laws, data privacy requirements, and internal controls\n\nYou will operate at both strategic and execution levels, partnering closely with HR leadership while maintaining hands-on oversight of delivery.  \n  \nResponsibilities: [...] Own the lifecycle of Workday HCM programs from initiation through stabilization\n Define program scope, roadmap, success metrics, and benefits realization\n Establish governance models, steering committees, and escalation paths\n Ensure alignment with HR strategy, enterprise architecture, security, and data standards\n\n Oversee design, configuration, testing, data migration, cutover, and deployment\n Manage dependencies across integrations, reporting, security, and HR operations\n Ensure adherence to Workday best practices while balancing business needs\n\n Serve as the primary interface between HR leadership, IT, Workday, and system integrators\n Manage implementation partners, AMS providers, and vendor performance\n Communicate status, risks, and outcomes to executives and HR leadership forums",
      "score": 0.22311495,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147287BR",
      "title": "Principal Consultant-Workday Functional-Denmark - Copenhagen Denmark - Careers | Infosys",
      "content": "Required\n\n 8+ years of hands‑on Workday functional configuration experience\n Workday Certification(Minimum 1 certification) – Core HCM, recruitment, time tracking, compensation\n Proven experience as a Functional Lead on Workday implementations or large enhancements\n Deep knowledge of Workday HCM architecture and data model\n Strong experience with business process configuration and security concepts\n Experience supporting Workday releases and tenant management activities\n\n  \nPreferred\n\n Experience with global or multi‑country deployments\n Prior experience in AMS or post‑production support models\n Basic workday integration knowledge [...] Role – Principal consultant  \nTechnology – Workday Functional HCM  \nLocation – Denmark  \nCompensation – Competitive (including bonus)  \n  \nJob Description:  \nWorkday Functional Lead is responsible for owning the functional design, configuration, and successful delivery of Workday solutions aligned to business requirements. This role acts as the primary functional authority for assigned Workday modules, ensuring best‑practice configuration, data integrity, business process alignment, and user adoption.  \nThe Functional Lead partners closely with HR, IT, Program/Project Managers, integration teams, and external system integrators to translate business needs into scalable Workday solutions across implementations, enhancements, and ongoing optimizations.  \n  \n  \nYour role: [...] Serve as the functional subject-matter expert (SME) for Workday modules – CoreHCM, recruitment, compensation, Benefits, Absence/ Time tracking\n Lead end‑to‑end functional delivery from requirements through post‑go‑live support\n Guide business stakeholders toward standard, scalable Workday solutions\n Ensure configuration aligns with enterprise process, security, and compliance standards\n Mentor functional analysts and ensure delivery quality across workstreams\n\n  \nResponsibilities:",

      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_enterprise/",
      "title": "Google says it has all the answers for AI agent sprawl - The Register",
      "content": "The announcements land in an increasingly competitive market for enterprise AI platforms. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, and Salesforce have all made a push into agent orchestration and management in recent months. Google's approach leans heavily on vertical integration, with the hope that designing chips, models, infrastructure, and application layers together produces better results than assembling components from different vendors.\n\nGoogle also announced a $750 million fund to support its partner ecosystem in building and deploying agentic AI, along with agreements with McKinsey, Deloitte, and other consulting firms that will receive early access to upcoming models from Google DeepMind. ®\n\nImage 19Share\n\n[](\n\n[](\n\n#### More about\n\n   Google AI\n   Google Cloud Next\n\nMore like these\n\n× [...] Within the press material for the show, several large customers provided testimonials about their own Gemini deployments. GE Appliances said it has more than 800 of Google's AI agents running across manufacturing, logistics, and supply chain operations. KPMG reported 90 percent Gemini Enterprise adoption among employees with more than 100 agents deployed in the first month. Tata Steel said it deployed over 300 specialized agents in nine months. Merck announced a partnership valued at up to $1 billion to build an agentic platform across its R&D, manufacturing, and commercial functions.       "url": "https://newsroom.servicenow.com/press-releases/details/2026/ServiceNow-and-Google-Cloud-unite-AI-agents-for-autonomous-enterprise-operations/default.aspx",
      "title": "ServiceNow and Google Cloud unite AI agents for autonomous enterprise operations - ServiceNow Newsroom",
      "content": "# Press Release\n\n### ServiceNow and Google Cloud unite AI agents for autonomous enterprise operations\n\nAI agents across Google Gemini’s Enterprise platform and the ServiceNow AI Platform work as one autonomous chain across 5G networking, retail, and IT systems\n\nLAS VEGAS--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- \nServiceNow (NYSE: NOW) and Google Cloud deepened their strategic partnership today at Google Cloud Next, unveiling new AI solutions and agents that bring autonomous operations to the world's largest enterprises. Spanning 5G networking, retail, and IT systems, the solutions represent a significant step in the two companies' shared vision: a future where AI agents can collaborate across platforms to autonomously detect, diagnose, and resolve problems before they affect customers. [...] ServiceNow is introducing a 5G Autonomous Network Operations solution that augments human oversight to identify and resolve problems before customers notice anything is wrong. Once given defined parameters, ServiceNow AI Agents powered by Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience (CX) handle anomaly detection and connect to the ServiceNow AI Platform for remediation. Together, they replace reactive chaos with a self-healing network.\n\nWhen a 5G performance issue surfaces, ServiceNow AI Agents on Gemini Enterprise for CX analyze network telemetry and confirm the root cause in real time. Via MCP, they pass context directly to applicable ServiceNow AI Agents, map the impact across services and SLAs, select the right fix, deploy the network function via A2A, and validate the resolution. [...] “Real customer value from agentic AI will be unlocked when agents seamlessly interoperate across platforms and systems, with enterprise-grade governance,” said Kevin Ichhpurani, president, Global Partner Ecosystem at Google Cloud. “By uniting Gemini Enterprise with the ServiceNow AI Platform via open protocols like MCP, we’re delivering an interoperable AI workforce that can detect, diagnose, and resolve issues autonomously.\"\n\nAutonomous Network Operations: from reactive chaos to self-healing operations",
      "score": 0.5379402,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/grafana_goes_free_with_ai/",
      "title": "Grafana: Free AI for all – please don't bankrupt us • The Register",
      "content": "But you can't have a keynote these days without mentioning agents: Dutt announced the Grafana Assistant - previously cloud-only - is coming to on-prem and open source users. They'll need to connect to a Grafana Cloud account to handle the LLM connection, though observability data stays on-prem. The assistant can power automations in Slack or Teams, or it can be accessed via API or CLI.\n\nGrafana Cloud also gains AI Observability, promising real-time visibility into AI agent behavior. Principal engineer Mat Ryer described it as a \"10,000-foot view\" of where time, tokens and \"therefore dollars\" are going, with the ability to drill down into agent activity — including spotting policy violations or data exposure. [...] Dutt said: \"I'm honestly really excited [about it] but also quite afraid… that we're offering Grafana assistant to the 99 percent. It's not just going to be limited to the 1 percent of Grafana users [who pay].\"\n\n\"I was talking to our chief financial officer about this, and he was kind of worried. So I'm excited – please use it, but maybe not too much.\"\n\n Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n Headless 360: Salesforce's latest pitch to let AI do the dev work\n If an AI agent screws up while running your business, there's nobody to sue\n AIOps is so powerful, vendors are building tools to clean up after agents break your infrastructure [...] ### Broader topics\n\n Data\n Programming\n Self-driving Car\n\n#### TIP US OFF\n\nSend us news\n\n### Other stories you might like\n\n#### NASA reckons the Artemis II heat shield performed like a champ\n\nGood news for future missions as initial findings agree with agency's design decision\n\nScience22 Apr 2026 | 4\n\n#### Right to repair champ Framework punts modular 13in laptop with Core Ultra Series 3\n\nLatest hardware sports dock for graphic card, power sipping battery\n\nPersonal Tech22 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n\nGoogle Cloud Next As biz agentic bot-wrangling intensifies, company says AI orchestration, security and infrastructure tools on the way\n\nAI + ML22 Apr 2026 | 2\n\n#### How JumpCloud unifies IT management to tame shadow AI",
      "score": 0.5054935,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.zdnet.com/article/google-deploys-ai-agent-cyber-force-as-security-arms-race-escalates/",
      "title": "Google bets $32B on AI agent cyber force as security arms race ...",
      "content": "Also: Will AI make cybersecurity obsolete, or is Silicon Valley confabulating again?\n\nTo defend against larger attack surfaces, faster AI deployment, and adversaries using AI for more sophisticated attacks, the good guys also need AI armies. Human analysts can't process the barrage of bits fast enough.\n\nThat combination of change brings us to Google; the firm is essentially launching a cyberforce of AI agents that can not only operate on the front lines of cyberwar but also supply back-end logistics and intelligence analysis. That approach is at the core of this announcement.\n\n## $32 billion [...] This concept is at the core of threat intelligence. In a cybersecurity context, knowing yourself means knowing your vulnerabilities and being able to track and manage enemy attacks and invasions. If an attacker can sneak into your network and live there for weeks or months, you don't know yourself.\n\nAlso: AI threats will get worse: 6 ways to match the tenacity of your digital adversaries\n\nGoogle is announcing Agentic SecOps (security operations) with three key prongs.\n\nThe tech giant uses Gemini AI to explore the dark web and build \"a nuanced profile of your organization.\" The AI can \"analyze millions of daily external events with 98% accuracy to help elevate only the threats that truly matter to your organization.\" [...] Also: 5 ways to use AI to modernize your legacy systems\n\nInto this space, Google is introducing Google Cloud Fraud Defense. Google describes this as \"The evolution of reCAPTCHA, and provides the intelligence that businesses need to trust their digital interactions and commerce.\"\n\nIt's basically a platform designed to determine whether an accessing entity is a human, a bot, or an agent.\n\n## State the facts, Jack\n\nGoogle included some social proof in its announcement. The company described success stories from a variety of major customers using these new tools.\n\nA few of those firms that have seen performance improvements include:\n\n## Welcome to the 2026 arms race",
      "score": 0.49699774,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.zdnet.com/article/google-cloud-next-enterprise-agent-platform-ai/",
      "title": "How Google just revamped Gemini Enterprise for the agentic era",
      "content": "\"We provide a single control plane for governance in Agent Platform, so every employee can use and share agents with full IT visibility,\" the company added. \"Both no-code and pro-code agents are managed through a consistent model for identity, security, and auditing.\"\n\n## Other announcements\n\nGoogle also announced Agentic Data Cloud, a new data architecture intended to help scale AI agents. Several new features let developers instantly query data without moving it out of AWS or Azure, leverage new data science tools across multiple surfaces, and enrich files with metadata to give agents more semantic context, among other capabilities. [...] On Wednesday at Google Cloud Next, the company's annual enterprise conference, Google released its new Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for developers. Evolved from Vertex AI, Agent Platform \"brings together the model selection, model building, and tuning services of Vertex AI that customers love, along with new features for agent integration, security, DevOps, orchestration, and more,\" CEO Thomas Kurian said in the announcement.\n\nAlso: This powerful Gemini setting made my AI results way more personal and accurate [...] ## All-in-one agent building\n\nIn the platform, according to Google, developers can design an agent's life cycle start to finish, from building the agents themselves to scaling and governing them. MCP support and an upgraded Agent Development Kit help developers maximize reasoning capabilities by structuring agents into sub-networks. That tiered approach should set agents up to handle complex tasks, Google said, adding that other features like faster runtime and Memory Bank help agents delegate to each other more efficiently and operate with more context for longer.\n\n\"Gemini Enterprise is now an end-to-end system for the agentic era, built for agents that can execute complex, multi-step work processes,\" Google said in the announcement.",
      "score": 0.45391592,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_unleashes_even_more_ai/",
      "title": "Google unleashes even more AI security agents to fight the baddies",
      "content": "Google unleashes Gemini AI agents on the dark web\n   Smooth criminals talking their way into cloud environments, Google says\n   Google's got a hot cloud infosec startup, a new unified platform — and its eye on Microsoft's $20B+ security biz\n   I meant to do that! AI vendors shrug off responsibility for vulns\n\nBut wait, there's more! In case you needed another platform to secure and manage agents, Google also announced the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, which it says will \"enable access management and AI governance at scale,\" assigning AI agents unique identities, which should allow them to operate autonomously with specific authentication flows – as opposed to running amok and causing chaos. [...] ## More from The Register\n\n### Special Features\n\n   All Special Features\n   RSA\n   Agentic AI\n   The Future of the Datacenter\n   AWS:Reinvent\n   SC25\n   Supercomputing Month\n   Datacenter Networking Nexus\n   The State of Storage\n\n### Vendor Voice\n\n   All Vendor Voice\n   Infinidat\n   Everpure\n   Rubrik\n   Make it real with Capgemini and AWS\n   Money Movement Hub\n   ZTE\n   Scale Kubernetes. Not Chaos.\n   AWS New Horizon\n\n### Resources\n\n   Whitepapers\n   Webinars & Events\n   Newsletters\n\n#### Security\n\n1Image 9: comment bubble on white\n\n# Google unleashes even more AI security agents to fight the baddies\n\n1Image 10: comment bubble on white\n\n## Along with a bunch of new services to make sure those same agents don't cause chaos\n\nImage 11: iconJessica Lyons\n\n Wed 22 Apr 2026  //  12:01 UTC [...] The second agent is aptly named the Detection Engineering agent. Google says this one helps organizations identify security coverage gaps in IT environments, and then continuously creates new detections and detection rules based on these findings.\n\nFinally, a soon-to-be-released Third-Party Context agent scours existing security workflows and enriches them using third-party data.\n\nAnd, Google's Triage and Investigation agent, announced at last year's conference, is now generally available. During the past 12 months, it processed over five million alerts and reduced a typical 30-minute manual analysis time down to 60 seconds, the company claims.",

      "title": "April 2026 announcements - Partner Center - Microsoft Learn",
      "content": "CSP perpetual licenses remain unchanged and don't gain License Mobility.\n\nNo action is required from partners or customers to activate this benefit. The updated product terms will take effect automatically on April 1, 2026.\n\n\n\nBeginning May 1, 2026, Microsoft 365 E7 will be available to transact in Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) channels on monthly, annual (one-year), and triennial (three-year) terms. Microsoft 365 E7 brings together Microsoft 365 E5 for secure productivity, Microsoft Entra Suite for identity and access control, Microsoft 365 Copilot for AI in the flow of work, and Microsoft Agent 365 as the control plane to govern and scale agents.\n\nTo support these new offerings, the following promotions are planned to be available on May 1, 2026: [...] 11.   Validate your support capabilities and prepare for the future of partner support\n12.   SKU Naming Update: Microsoft Teams Premium and Microsoft Teams Events\n13.   Download targeted offers for your customers using AI assistant in Partner Center\n14.   Announcing reduced Microsoft Dragon Copilot pricing\n15.   License Mobility for Windows Server and SQL Server subscriptions\n16.   SKUs and promotions for CSP transactability May 1\n17.   Global Tax ID modernization launch [...] Existing subscriptions continue without interruption. The updated name appears in Partner Center pricing, billing, and customer workspaces.\n\n direct bill partners and distributors.\n\nWe’ve introduced a new AI assistant capability in Partner Center that helps Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) partners discover and download targeted offers applicable to their customers.\n\nTargeted offers are customer‑specific promotions assigned by Microsoft and aren't available through public promotion listings. With this update, partners can use the AI assistant to automatically evaluate customer eligibility and generate a downloadable report of applicable targeted offers.\n\n in the upper‑right corner.\n   Use prompts such as “Download targeted offers applicable to my customers”.",

Mar 20, 2026 5 mins\n   news ### Flaws in four popular VS Code extensions left 128 million installs open to attack Feb 18, 2026 4 mins [...] Tyagi said the partnership’s roadmap is the next variable to watch. “While the partnership appears directionally logical, key uncertainties remain — specifically whether the roadmap will prioritize Grok, Composer, both, or an entirely new model,” he said.\n\nHe also pointed to an earlier precedent. “Model access restrictions often move faster than innovation,” Tyagi said, citing Anthropic’s decision to restrict Windsurf’s access amid OpenAI acquisition rumours. The current announcement “could backfire for Cursor if major providers like OpenAI or Anthropic limit model access,” he said.\n\nSpaceX and Cursor did not immediately respond to requests for comment.\n\nArtificial IntelligenceDevelopment ToolsSoftware Development\n\nNEWSLETTER\n\nThe latest AI updates, straight to your inbox",

      "title": "Google Cloud Debuts New AI Chips, Tools for Building Agents",
      "content": "The new lineup will come in two versions, the company said Wednesday at its Google Cloud Next event, where it also announced a $750 million fund to help boost corporate AI adoption and showed off tools for building AI agents. The TPU 8t is tailored for creating artificial intelligence software, while the TPU 8i is designed to run AI services after they’ve been created — a stage known as inference.\n\nBefore it's here, it's on the Bloomberg Terminal LEARN MORE\n\n[](\n\nHomeBTV+Market DataOpinionAudioOriginalsMagazineEvents\n\nNews\n\nMarketsEconomicsTechnologyPoliticsGreenCryptoAI\n\nWork & Life\n\nWealthPursuitsBusinessweekCityLabSportsEqualityManagement & Work\n\nMarket Data\n\nStocksCommoditiesRates & BondsCurrenciesFuturesSectorsEconomic Calendar\n\nExplore [...] Asia Pacific+65 6212 1000\n\n   ### Company\n\n       About\n       Careers\n       Inclusion at Bloomberg\n       Tech at Bloomberg\n       Philanthropy\n       Sustainability\n       Bloomberg Beta\n\n### Communications\n\n       Press Announcements\n       Press Contacts\n\n### Follow\n\n       Facebook\n       Instagram\n       LinkedIn\n       YouTube\n\n   ### Products\n\n       Bloomberg Professional Services\n       Bloomberg Terminal\n       Data\n       Trading\n       Risk\n       Compliance\n       Indices\n\n### Industry Products\n\n       Bloomberg Law\n       Bloomberg Tax\n       Bloomberg Government\n       BloombergNEF\n\n   ### Media [...] ### Media\n\n       Bloomberg Markets\n       Bloomberg Technology\n       Bloomberg Pursuits\n       Bloomberg Politics\n       Bloomberg Opinion\n       Bloomberg Businessweek\n       Bloomberg Live Conferences\n       Bloomberg Radio\n       Bloomberg Television\n       News Bureaus\n\n### Media Services\n\n       Bloomberg Media Distribution\n       Advertising\n\n   ### Company\n\n       About\n       Careers\n       Inclusion at Bloomberg\n       Tech at Bloomberg\n       Philanthropy\n       Sustainability\n       Bloomberg Beta\n\n### Communications\n\n       Press Announcements\n       Press Contacts\n\n### Follow\n\n       Facebook\n       Instagram\n       LinkedIn\n       YouTube\n\n   ### Products",

      "url": "https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/microsoft-discovery-advancing-agentic-rd-at-scale/",
      "title": "Microsoft Discovery: Advancing agentic R&D at scale",
      "content": "> _We are entering a new phase of our partnership with Microsoft, focused on scaling AI agents across research, sales and marketing to drive near-term growth. By connecting customer demand to scientific development and back to market execution, agentic AI is enabling faster cycles, sharper prioritization, and tangible impact on revenue growth and business performance.”_\n> \n> \n> —Mike Radossich, Chief Executive Officer (CEO), Syensqo\n\n### GigaTIME [...] ## The era of agentic AI for research and development\n\nAgentic AI opens a new chapter for R&D where autonomous agent teams, guided by human expertise, perform the core research and engineering tasks in a redefined agentic loop. Specialized agents can reason on top of vast amounts of organizational and public-domain knowledge, create hypotheses on an expanded search space, test and validate those hypotheses at scale, analyze the results, and feed conclusions into iterative loops. Empowering science and engineering experts with agentic AI has the potential to reshape the future of science and engineering, enabling organizations to lead boldly in the new Frontier R&D era. [...] Thought leadership\n        Apr 8 \n        5 min read \n\n### Cloud Cost Optimization: How to maximize ROI from AI, manage costs, and unlock real business value\n\n   Image 9: Field engineers discussing on site. \n\n       Announcements\n        Mar 25 \n        5 min read \n\n### AI for nuclear energy: Powering an intelligent, resilient future\n\n## Explore\n\nMicrosoft Foundry\n\nThe future of AI starts here. Envision your next great AI app with the latest technologies. Get started with Azure.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft Foundry\n\nConnect with us on social\n\n   X\n   YouTube\n   LinkedIn\n   Instagram\n\nExplore Azure\n\n   What is Azure?\n   Get started with Azure\n   Global infrastructure\n   Datacenter regions\n   Trust your cloud\n   Azure Essentials\n   Customer stories\n\nProducts and pricing",
      "score": 0.32302815,

    {
      "url": "https://sponsored.bloomberg.com/article/salesforce/as-companies-bet-big-on-agentic-ai-strategies-must-shift-to-drive-results",
      "title": "As Companies Bet Big on Agentic AI, Strategies Must Shift to Drive Results",
      "content": "With the release of Agentforce 360 for AWS, an offering running entirely on AWS infrastructure and powered by Amazon Bedrock models, enterprises have a compliant foundation for AI procurement and agent deployment. The platform addresses common architectural weaknesses that could render adoption insecure, incompatible or inoperable at scale.\n\nThe Salesforce and AWS partnership lays the foundation for scaling agentic AI through four core pillars: unified data; agentic security and interoperability; modernized contact centers; and streamlined procurement through AWS Marketplace. Capabilities include allowing agents to access the right data in real time while preserving security, orchestrating actions across multiple applications and providing visibility into exactly what agents are doing. [...] ### For Customers\n\n### Support\n\nAmericas+1 212 318 2000\n\nEMEA+44 20 7330 7500\n\nAsia Pacific+65 6212 1000\n\nAs Companies Bet Big on Agentic AI, Strategies Must Shift to Drive Results\n\n# As Companies Bet Big on Agentic AI, Strategies Must Shift to Drive Results\n\n## Takeaways\n\nProving ROI is now a top priority for enterprises, which are shifting toward agentic AI as they look toward more advanced, action-oriented systems to deliver measurable business value.\n\nAchieving results requires enterprise-wide integration (unified data, governance, scalable infrastructure), enabling AI agents to operate seamlessly across systems without fragmentation. [...] To solve this, Agentforce 360 prioritizes true interoperability, ensuring that agents aren't just isolated task-performers, but are capable of exchanging data and logic across the entire enterprise ecosystem.\n\nAgentforce built on what worked in Salesforce’s original model: streamlining the patchwork nature of customer relationship management into a single platform. Applying the same approach to training, deploying and monitoring AI agents prioritizes enterprise systems over isolated tools, Ahuja says.\n\nChoosing a side in the build-or-buy debate",
      "score": 0.29331386,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://sponsored.bloomberg.com/quicksight/servicenow/the-ai-governance-paradox-steering-ai-without-hindering-innovation",
      "title": "The AI Governance Paradox: Steering AI Without Hindering Innovation",
      "content": "Across Australian enterprises, AI agents are multiplying.\n\nWhat began as targeted pilots is becoming systemic. Copilots draft reports, autonomous agents resolve IT tickets, and AI systems analyze contracts, trigger approvals and move work across departments. Dozens of AI-enabled workflows are already live in many enterprises. Soon, there will be hundreds more.  The scale is global. Bloomberg Intelligence found 97% of global enterprises reported active AI implementation in 2025, with two-thirds moving beyond evaluating AI models to developing and scaling AI tools.  \n\nYet only 33% of Australian leaders have a clear AI vision. This is the governance paradox: while enterprise AI adoption is surging, confidence and control are lagging. [...] Skip To Content\n\n?Sponsored Content:\n\nThis content is made possible by our sponsor; it is not written by and does not necessarily reflect the views of Bloomberg LP’s editorial staff. Click to see our Advertising Guidelines to learn more.Brought to you by ServiceNow\n\n# The AI Governance Paradox: Steering AI Without Hindering Innovation\n\nData\n\nYoY Australia's Enterprise AI Maturity Index score:\n\n2024: 46/100\n\n2025: 36/100 (down by 10 points)\n\nSource: ServiceNow\n\n## The Challenge\n\nAcross Australian enterprises, AI agents are multiplying. [...] That requires integrating data, workflow and security within a single operational backbone: an AI control tower that provides real-time visibility across agents, automated monitoring and enforcement, and built-in auditability. With this control tower, AI deployment and governance can move at the same speed.\n\n“It's not enough for AI to simply work; it needs to be trustworthy,” says Bryan McGowan, Global Trusted AI Leader and US Trusted AI Leader at KPMG. “By activating KPMG AI Trust with alliance partners like ServiceNow, we are taking a significant step forward in AI governance to set a new standard for AI risk management across all industries.”",
      "score": 0.15730536,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/introducing-the-new-hosted-agents-in-foundry-agent-service-secure-scalable-compute-built-for-agents/",
      "title": "secure, scalable compute built for agents | Microsoft Foundry Blog",
      "content": "Today, it is easy to build agents locally. But how do you take that same power and patterns, and deploy it across an enterprise? How do you make it serve thousands of customer conversations securely? How do you give it an identity, govern its access, and observe what it’s doing at scale?\n\nThat’s what Microsoft Foundry is releasing today. Today we’re announcing hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service, now in public preview. This brings agent-optimized compute and services designed for production-grade enterprise agents. We first previewed hosted agents last year at Microsoft Ignite, but this refresh is a fundamentally different experience: secure per-session sandboxes with filesystem persistence, integrated identity, and scale-to-zero economics.\n\nConvert to GIF project image [...] ### More than compute — a complete agent platform\n\nHosted agents aren’t just infrastructure. They’re integrated with the full Microsoft Foundry platform, including announcements we’re making alongside this refresh:\n\nAnd the enterprise capabilities that make this production-ready:\n\nThis is the combination that only Microsoft Foundry can offer: agent-optimized compute and services, integrated with thousands of tools, any model, any harness — all with secure sandboxing, persistence, and enterprise identity.\n\n### What’s next\n\nThe foundation is in place: agent-optimized compute, secure sandboxes, filesystem persistence, enterprise identity, and deep platform integration. But we’re just getting started. [...] ### Bring any harness, any framework, any environment\n\nRather than locking you into a specific harness or runtime, Foundry lets you bring whatever works best for your agent. Use LangGraph, Microsoft Agent Framework, Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK, the GitHub Copilot SDK — any framework you want. Define the optimal environment for your agent with a Dockerfile, so it has exactly the tools and dependencies it needs to get the job done. Deploy in one command (azd deploy). Microsoft Foundry will take care of running your agent at scale.\n\nThis flexibility is the point. Unlike platforms that force one model and one harness, Foundry is multi-model and multi-harness by design. Run models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, and more. Bring any orchestration framework. No lock-in.",
      "score": 0.13689686,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 2.2,
  "request_id": "11b136a8-96d4-450d-aea4-8f2ee11b1ef0"

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available | Workday Aus & NZ",
      "content": "## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] Today, more than 11,000 customers worldwide trust Workday to manage their people and their money. With ASOR, that same foundation now extends to AI agents. ASOR serves as the single source of truth for all of an enterprise’s AI agents—whether they’re built by Workday, a customer, or a partner. It gives leaders clear visibility into where work shifts from people to AI agents and how that work connects to business outcomes.\n\nThis visibility is essential for managing a blended workforce—humans and agents—bringing clarity, control, and insights.\n\n## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale [...] With a unique understanding of how AI agents interact with business processes, tasks, and reporting data, ASOR brings insights to process efficiency, ROI, and total business impact. This helps leaders measure and assess the value that AI agents provide, so they can refocus employees on the highest value work.\n\n“The real value of Workday’s Agent System of Record is the ability to capture analytics across an ecosystem of agents,” said Claudio Valera, PwC global and U.S. Workday alliance leader. “By bringing insights from thousands of use cases into a single system, you’re able to clearly see how your human-machine teams are performing. This visibility enables teams to innovate more effectively and scale AI with confidence.”\n\n## Built for Accountability and Governance",

      "raw_content": null
    },
