      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next/welcome-to-google-cloud-next26",
      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "Gemini momentum with ISVs: Leading companies like Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds more are deeply integrating Gemini into their products, bringing more AI-powered features and agents to their users.\n More enterprise-grade agents from partners: Already, thousands of agents built by our partners are available in Google Cloud Marketplace. Now, you can access select agents directly through Gemini Enterprise, making it easier for you to find the right agent for your team’s needs from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow and Workday. [...] Agentic Taskforceincludes new AI capabilities across Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience and Google Workspace.\n Customer Success across our entire portfolio. [...] Agentic Defense is delivered via a cybersecurity platform that combines Google’s Threat Intelligence and Security Operations with Wiz’s Cloud and AI Security Platform to detect, prevent, and respond to threats. A number of new agents for threat detection, detection engineering, remediation and more, along with Wiz’s AI Application Protection Platform (AI-APP), provide autonomous protection from code, to cloud, to runtime, across multicloud, hybrid, and AI environments.\n Agentic Taskforce includesnew innovations across our Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience solution, as well as new capabilities in Google Workspace including Workspace Intelligence, which streamlines productivity by providing a unified context layer across Workspace apps.",
      "score": 0.3084719,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/release-notes",
      "title": "Google Cloud release notes",
      "content": "Security Command Center\n\nFeature\n\nAI Protection supports agentic workloads in Preview, including Vertex AI Agent Engine and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. This update includes the following:\n\n   Agent vulnerability scanner: Identifies software vulnerabilities (CVEs) in workloads deployed with Agent Engine.\n   Expanded detection and controls: Includes new threat detection findings and recommended security controls for AI agents and MCP servers.\n   Enhanced inventory and filtering: Provides an updated AI security dashboard view and new filtering options for agentic resources in the console.\n\n## April 15, 2026\n\nAlloyDB for PostgreSQL\n\nFeature [...] Cloud SQL for SQL Server\n\nFeature\n\nYou can use the Database Insights remote MCP server to analyze Cloud SQL for SQL Server's performance and system metrics. This feature is in GA.\n\nCloud Storage\n\nFeature\n\nCloud Storage Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is generally available. You can connect to Cloud Storage from AI applications using the server. It lets AI applications and agents create buckets, retrieve object metadata, read and write object data, and list buckets and objects. For more information, see Use the Cloud Storage MCP server and Cloud Storage MCP reference.\n\nCompute Engine\n\nFeature [...] Collect OpenAI Audit logs\n   Collect OpenTelemetry Netflow Receiver logs\n   Collect Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications logs\n   Collect Oracle NetSuite - NetSuite Applications Suite logs\n   Collect Oracle NetSuite logs\n   Collect Vectra Alerts logs\n   Collect Vectra XDR logs\n   Collect Windows Event logs (XML format)\n   Collect WinSCP logs\n   Collect Workday User Activity logs\n   Collect WP Engine logs\n   Collect XAMS by Xiting logs\n   Collect Yubico OTP logs\n   Collect Zero Networks logs\n   Collect Zix Email Encryption logs\n   Collect Zscaler NSS Feeds for Alerts logs\n   Collect ZyXEL ZyWALL logs",
      "score": 0.108670644,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/introducing-the-google-cloud-knowledge-catalog",
      "title": "Introducing the Google Cloud Knowledge Catalog | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "BigQuery measures (Preview):We are redefining data consistency by embedding programmatic business logic directly into the SQL engine. BigQuery measures ensure every calculation is universally reusable and mathematically accurate. The Knowledge Catalog acts as the ultimate aggregator, pulling BigQuery measures and LookML together into a single, governed semantic foundation.\n\nData products (GA): Data products package data assets and context that grounds agents and makes them reliable in production. These self-contained blocks include built-in intent, SLAs, and governance constraints, providing the essential building blocks to scale complex AI use cases.\n\n### Enrichment: Generating meaning through continuous learning [...] Verified queries and semantic guardrails (Preview): One leading cause of AI failure is hallucinated logic and guessed SQL joins. To prevent this, the catalog provides verified SQL patterns and pre-generated natural language questions.\n\n### Search: Unleashing agents with high-precision, secure retrieval\n\nCreating a massive context layer is great, but in the agentic era, search has evolved to be the new query path. When autonomous agents are working on your behalf, they are iterating incredibly fast. The hardest problems at enterprise scale are speed, relevance, global reach, and security. [...] Enterprise connectivity (Preview): To truly understand your operations, semantic context must cover all the key systems in your enterprise. Using Google Cloud Lakehouse, we are interconnecting these systems with context federation, and Knowledge Catalog gains full and immediate visibility to applications, operating systems, and AI platforms — including Palantir, Salesforce Data360, SAP, ServiceNow, and Workday. For example, your SAP data products are automatically mapped to the Knowledge Catalog.",
      "score": 0.100022875,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations",
      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day. A security escalation surfaces in Slack, context lives in a Google Doc, approvals sit in Salesforce, and stakeholder alignment happens over email. Instead of patching together point solutions, companies can now build an interconnected system of engagement that enables teams to move faster across the business and turn ideas into agentic action, all from the same conversation.\n\nKey Integration Features: [...] ## Explore related content by topic\n\n## Related Articles\n\nView of Agent Fabric on a laptop\n\n## Salesforce Advances Agent Fabric: New Guided Determinism and Governance Controls to Scale Multi-Vendor AI Faster\n\nEngine Powers Seamless Travel Experiences with Agentforce and Slack\n\n## Engine Powers Seamless Travel Experiences with Agentforce and Slack\n\nWhy Orchestration Is the Next Frontier for Agents\n\n## Why Orchestration Is the Next (Wildly Necessary) Frontier for Agents\n\nSalesforce Launches Forward Deployed Engineering Partner Network\n\n## Salesforce Launches the Forward Deployed Engineering Partner Network to Scale Agentforce Success\n\nAstro\n\nGet the latest Salesforce News\n\n## Insight, Opinion, News, and Data\n\nSign up for Salesforce News & Insights, direct to your inbox.\n\n## Share article [...] Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations\n\nLAS VEGAS, Cloud Next ’26 — April 22, 2026 — Google Cloud and Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) today announced an expanded partnership that will enable AI agents to execute end-to-end workflows across both platforms by solving the long-standing challenge of fragmented data and disconnected systems. With new integrations across Salesforce and Google Cloud, customers can deploy AI agents in tools like Slack and Google Workspace, while Agentforce and Gemini Enterprise provide the intelligence and context behind the scenes. This allows agents to work across systems, reduces risky data movement, and eliminates time lost to context switching.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, Slack lflunk data access test: Fivetran • The Register",
      "content": "Enterprise HR and finance SaaS provider Workday ranked worst for performance. FiveTran cites restrictive rate limits (~10 calls/sec, 100 records/response), slow API calls for large data volumes, and a reporting service that \"lacks pagination causing large report failures.\"\n\nThe SaaS vendor also ranked second worst for coverage, with FiveTran claiming \"access is gated behind complex domain security policies and some advanced analytics require Prism Analytics (paid add-on).\"\n\nA spokesperson at Workday told The Register: [...] It's an issue organizations should watch closely as they build AI agent systems, which depend on company data to generate meaningful responses. Most major vendors offer AI agents within their own platforms but getting those agents to work across different applications is another matter.\n\nFiveTran's newly launched benchmark indicates a number of companies ranking poorly on its categories of data coverage, performance, and egress charges.\n\nAmong the worst on egress fees is Xero, an accounting SaaS for SMBs. FiveTran claims there is a \"tiered monthly fees plus explicit API egress allotments and overage pricing\" to get to the data. [...] Science22 Apr 2026 | 12\n\n#### Grafana offers AI assistant for free, warns users not to go mad\n\nObservability biz bets on business analytics wedge as Loki put on long-overdue diet\n\nDevops22 Apr 2026 |\n\n#### Right to repair champ Framework punts modular 13in laptop with Core Ultra Series 3\n\nLatest hardware sports dock for graphic card, power sipping battery\n\nPersonal Tech22 Apr 2026 | 13\n\n#### Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n\nGoogle Cloud Next As biz agentic bot-wrangling intensifies, company says AI orchestration, security and infrastructure tools on the way\n\nAI + ML22 Apr 2026 | 2\n\n#### Google unleashes even more AI security agents to fight the baddies\n\nAlong with a bunch of new services to make sure those same agents don't cause chaos",
      "score": 0.9736447,

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "## An Open Ecosystem, Built on Standards\n\nA system of record is only as strong as the ecosystem it supports.\n\nSince launching the Workday Agent Partner Network, more than 65 global partners are connecting their AI agents to Workday’s ASOR. Nearly 20 Workday Ventures portfolio companies have joined as well, bringing new innovative agent solutions into a governed, enterprise-ready environment available in the Workday Marketplace. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents. [...] Today, more than 11,000 customers worldwide trust Workday to manage their people and their money. With ASOR, that same foundation now extends to AI agents. ASOR serves as the single source of truth for all of an enterprise’s AI agents—whether they’re built by Workday, a customer, or a partner. It gives leaders clear visibility into where work shifts from people to AI agents and how that work connects to business outcomes.\n\nThis visibility is essential for managing a blended workforce—humans and agents—bringing clarity, control, and insights.\n\n## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale",
      "score": 0.7746691,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://go.theregister.com/feed/www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_enterprise/",
      "title": "Google says it has all the answers for AI agent sprawl • The Register",
      "content": "Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is organized around four pillars: build, scale, govern, and optimize. On the build side, Google introduced Agent Studio, a low-code interface for creating agents using natural language, alongside an upgraded Agent Development Kit with a new graph-based framework for orchestrating multiple agents working together, the company said during a media prebriefing.\n\nIt also provides an agent registry that gives organizations a central catalog of each internal agent and tool, the company said. Also inside the new platform is an agent marketplace that offers pre-built agents from partners including Atlassian, Oracle, ServiceNow, and Workday. [...] It comes as the challenge facing businesses has shifted from building individual AI agents to managing hundreds or thousands of them at once - something Workday and others are trying to tackle too.\n\n\"The early versions of AI models were really focused on answering questions that people had and assisting them with creative tasks. Now we’re seeing as the models evolve people wanting to delegate tasks and sequences of tasks to agents,\" Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told reporters during a press briefing. \"And these agents then being able to turn around and use a computer, use all of GCP and Workspace as a tool.\"\n\nTo meet the moment, Google rolled out infrastructure in the form of its eighth generation of TPU chips and security updates through its purchase of Wiz.\n\nboxing [...] The announcements land in an increasingly competitive market for enterprise AI platforms. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, and Salesforce have all made a push into agent orchestration and management in recent months. Google's approach leans heavily on vertical integration, with the hope that designing chips, models, infrastructure, and application layers together produces better results than assembling components from different vendors.\n\nGoogle also announced a $750 million fund to support its partner ecosystem in building and deploying agentic AI, along with agreements with McKinsey, Deloitte, and other consulting firms that will receive early access to upcoming models from Google DeepMind. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n### More about\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n### Broader topics\n\n#### More about",
      "score": 0.58919704,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/three-tiers-of-agentic-ai---and-when-to-use-none-of-them/4510377",
      "title": "Three tiers of Agentic AI - and when to use none of them | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "That gap is not a capability problem. The infrastructure has matured. Tool calling is standard across all major models. Frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Microsoft Agent Framework abstract orchestration logic. Model Context Protocol standardizes how agents access external tools and data sources. Google's Agent-to-Agent protocol now under Linux Foundation governance with over 50 enterprise technology partners including Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, and Workday standardizes how agents coordinate with each other. The protocols are in place. The frameworks are production ready.\n\nThe gap is a selection and governance problem. [...] ### References\n\nOutSystems — State of AI Development Report - \n\nDatabricks — State of AI Agents Report - \n\nGartner — 2025 Microsoft 365 and Copilot Survey -  (Paywalled primary source — publicly reported via techpartner.news: )\n\nAnthropic — Model Context Protocol (MCP) - \n\nGoogle Cloud — Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A) . \n\nNeuBird AI — Production Operations Deployment Announcement NeuBird AI Closes $19.3M Funding Round to Scale Agentic AI Across Enterprise Production Operations\n\nReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models — Yao et al. \n\nEnterprise Integration Patterns — Gregor Hohpe & Bobby Woolf, Addison-Wesley \n\nsgangaramani's avatar\nIcon for Microsoft rank\n\n### Share this page [...] ### Share this page\n\nShare to LinkedIn\nShare to Facebook\nShare to X\nShare to Reddit\nShare to Blue Sky\nSubscribe to RSS\nShare to Email",
      "score": 0.25883895,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.okta.com/company/careers/business-technology/servicenow-itam-developer-7727730/",
      "title": "ServiceNow ITAM Developer | Okta",
      "content": "Lead technical delivery for ServiceNow SAM and HAM, focusing on the Content Library, Normalization engines, and Reconciliation processes.\n Partner with business stakeholders to analyze asset management business processes and implement automated workflows that drive license reclamation and cost-avoidance strategies.\n Oversee and troubleshoot complex integrations with core systems, including Coupa, Okta, JAMF, and Intune, ensuring data flow is consistent and accurate.\n Build, configure, and maintain integrations with SaaS connectors (e.g., Salesforce, Workday, Adobe) and develop custom integrations for unique software vendors using IntegrationHub. [...] This is a highly collaborative and integrated role. You will work closely with our SAM Managers to translate complex asset management business processes into technical solutions that identify and capture cost-saving opportunities. You will manage a sophisticated ecosystem that connects financial data from Workday and NetSuite, procurement data from Coupa, and endpoint data from JAMF and Intune. Your expertise will bridge the gap between technical data modeling and strategic business outcomes.\n\nResponsibilities [...] Build and maintain automated workflows for the hardware lifecycle, following Common Service Data Model (CSDM) best practices to keep the Asset and CI tables in sync.\n Implement HAM Pro features like Hardware Model Normalization, Mobile Asset Tracking, and Asset Inventory Audit workflows.\n Manage Cloud Cost Management (CCM) workflows to provide visibility and optimization for AWS and GCP spend.\n Manage Software Spend Detection by integrating financial data from Workday and NetSuite to identify tail spend and shadow IT.\n Design advanced dashboards and Performance Analytics widgets to report on compliance, cloud savings, and hardware refresh cycles for executive leadership.",
      "score": 0.08368814,

      "url": "https://events.sap.com/gb-hr-connect-london-2026/en_us/home.html",
      "title": "HR Connect London 2026",
      "content": "A digital adoption strategy is critical to realising the full value of your SAP investment and navigating the evolution of agentic AI. WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM empowers employees and managers to complete HR workflows correctly and confidently through personalised guidance, automation, AI assistance and learning in the flow of work. Discover how organisations are increasing ROI and employee satisfaction while reducing support costs by delivering intelligent support at the moment of need.”\n\nHardwick Hub - Migration project's best kept secret: SpinifexIT\n\nMigration can feel complex - especially when the path forward isn’t clear. For many organisations, uncertainty, perceived risk, and mixed experiences make it easy to pause. [...] Join us at HR Connect London on 23rd April, where you’ll engage with a community of HR leaders, peers and experts explore how SAP SuccessFactors AI-enabled suite helps you build a workforce strategy that fuels connection.\n\nAt HR Connect, you’ll learn how SAP SuccessFactors connects your people, your business and your goals through:\n\n- Unified skills intelligence, recruiting, and talent development that that engages employees and fuels performance.\n\n- Integrated AI and data that delivers contextual insights across your business.\n\n- An open platform and expansive partner network designed for limitless connections and continuous innovation\n\nWe’ll show you how to keep your people, processes and progress moving forward together – so you can make every employee a success story. [...] Discover how RS Group created a single, connected view of skills, development and career pathways. Hear how they aligned timing, budget and internal capability to understand the skills they have today, and the skills they will need tomorrow. Learn why scalability, employee experience and smart integration guided their fit‑for‑purpose approach, enabling a practical, joined‑up transformation journey.       "title": "SAP Community Voice: New SAP Champions, Vote Badge... - SAP Community",
      "content": "SAP expert Gautham Krishna introduces key information about the enhanced edition of SAP Integration Suite, highlighting the enabling agentic AI in enterprise environments, shifting the conversation from \"AI that knows\" to \"AI that acts.\" For Gautham, the real challenge is not building AI models, but establishing trust through integration, governance, and secure execution across systems. And with new capabilities like Text-to-Flow, Script Optimization, API Anomaly Detection, and API Traffic Prediction, this enhanced edition helps both accelerate development and strengthen operational reliability. Combined with broader integration services and event-driven architecture, this release positions Integration Suite as the execution layer where AI agents can safely operate, scale, and deliver [...] where AI agents can safely operate, scale, and deliver real business outcomes across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.       "content": "sarina_seth_2-1776860886109.jpeg\n\n- May 12, 10.30-10.50am - Build a transformation capability that delivers measurable value with Sam Masri SAP\n\nSAP Signavio:\n\nsarina_seth_3-1776860886111.jpeg\n\nsarina_seth_3-1776860886111.jpeg\n\n- May 13, 2.00-2.20pm - Unlocking the New Frontier of Agentic Transformation with SAP Signavio with Dr. Gero Decker SAP\n\nAs companies deploy hundreds of AI agents, the real differentiator is no longer automation alone but instead how intelligence is captured and turned into value.       "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/partner-built-agents-available-in-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Partner-built agents available in Gemini Enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "OpenText: OpenText™ Content Aviator™ is a generative AI assistant that enhances content search and productivity. Built on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, it allows users to perform natural language searches and efficiently summarize enterprise documents.\n\nOracle: Oracle AI Database Agent lets you query Oracle data directly from Gemini Enterprise. Available via Google Cloud Marketplace, it delivers fast, enterprise-grade answers without requiring custom chatbots or NL-to-SQL maintenance.\n\nOrion by Gravity: Orion by Gravity is an AI analyst that connects to Looker, BigQuery, and more to proactively hunt for insights. It empowers data teams to identify anomalies and recommend actions that uncover 'unknown-unknowns' in analytics-rich industries. [...] Ascendo: Ascendo AI Agent helps critical infrastructure teams automate workflows, improve diagnostics, and reduce field escalations. By ingesting logs, telemetry, and service manuals, it recommends optimal actions to support faster, accurate decision-making in complex service environments.\n\nAtlassian: Rovo is an AI teammate seamlessly integrated across Jira, Confluence, and your tool stack. Built on Atlassian’s Teamwork Graph, it surfaces knowledge, automates tasks, and takes intelligent actions to help teams pull context, move faster and stay aligned. [...] EPAM: Creative Conductor Marketing Agent turns creative briefs into polished promotional videos. It coordinates specialized sub-agents for visuals, voiceover, and music, assembling assets into a final MP4 to deliver fast, scalable, high-quality marketing content.\n\nExa AI: Exa Agent gives workers a live web view, turning it into a searchable database of code, people, and companies. It searches billions of pages to accelerate tasks like developer tooling, recruiting, and market mapping.\n\nFoxtrot Communications: Forge - AI Agent helps teams eliminate manual ETL pipeline development. Connect any API, and Forge autonomously delivers clean, normalized, analytics-ready datasets, reducing time-to-insight to minutes and freeing engineers to focus on strategic initiatives.",
      "score": 0.24951623,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/partners/how-google-cloud-partner-ecosystem-is-building-the-agentic-enterprise",
      "title": "How Google Cloud partner ecosystem is building the agentic enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Box is launching new Box Agents powered by Gemini 3 Flash and Gemini Enterprise, helping enterprises transform static files into actionable intelligence by natively integrating AI orchestration into their secure content management workflows.\n\nDocuSign is using Gemini to power new features that summarize complex agreements, identify key clauses, and help users understand the implications of their contracts.\n\nOracle is launching the Oracle AI Database Connector for Gemini Enterprise. This new agent enables end users to ask business questions of their Oracle data in natural language in Gemini Enterprise, without needing to write SQL or understand the underlying data model. [...] Today, Agent Gallery provides access to a wide range of third-party agents. These agents have been built on top of our secure, enterprise-grade infrastructure, meaning customers can deploy them within their businesses with the highest levels of governance and confidence. Today, this includes agents built by Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and more.\n\n### Empowering global consulting partners to drive AI transformations\n\nToday, Google Cloud’s global consulting and systems integrator partners offer customers more than 330,000 experts trained on implementing Google AI. At Next, we are expanding our partnerships with every major systems integrator, including: [...] ### Investing to accelerate AI agent development\n\nWe are committed to offering customers the most AI-capable partner ecosystem in the industry. To empower our partners to drive real transformation in the agentic AI era,we are launching a $750 million innovation fund to accelerate agent development and deployment globally, applicable to every business process, function, and industry.\n\nThis funding will support a wide range of activities including:\n\nHands-on support for software companies to build AI agents into their products with the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and bring them to market through our Agent Marketplace and through the new Agent Gallery in Gemini Enterprise.",

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next/welcome-to-google-cloud-next26",
      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "3. Gemini Enterprise, the front door to AI for every employee and customer: Gemini Enterprise enables every customer and employee to build, use, deploy, and manage cutting-edge AI agents. Whether it's bringing together sales and service interactions to improve customer touchpoints, automating complex workflows to improve your supply chain efficiency, or enabling marketers to prototype and create campaigns in record time, Gemini Enterprise is transforming the way companies get work done. [...] Gemini momentum with ISVs: Leading companies like Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds more are deeply integrating Gemini into their products, bringing more AI-powered features and agents to their users.\n More enterprise-grade agents from partners: Already, thousands of agents built by our partners are available in Google Cloud Marketplace. Now, you can access select agents directly through Gemini Enterprise, making it easier for you to find the right agent for your team’s needs from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow and Workday. [...] + Wiz AI Application Protection Platform (AI-APP) secures every layer of AI applications—infrastructure, data, access, models, agents, and applications—from code to runtime, across every environment where you build. AI-APP brings together visibility, risk analysis, and runtime protection into a single, graph-powered platform that helps teams understand and secure AI systems as they operate. Today, we’re taking our commitment further. Wiz now supports Databricks, as well as new agent studios like AWS Agentcore, Gemini Enterprise Agent Builder, Microsoft Azure Copilot Studio, and Salesforce Agentforce so customers gain protection however their teams choose to build. Wiz now also connects to the outer layer of the cloud, including Apigee, Cloudflare, Vercel, and others to add more context",

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/identity-security/next26-redefining-security-for-the-ai-era-with-google-cloud-and-wiz",
      "title": "Next '26: Redefining security for the AI era with Google Cloud and Wiz",
      "content": "Wiz announced its AI-Application Protection Platform (AI-APP) at the RSA Conference, providing deep visibility, risk posture, and runtime analysis for your AI applications. Wiz also announced Wiz Security Agents and Wiz Workflows, helping you identify and respond to risks and threats at machine speed.\n\nToday, we’re taking our commitment to secure customers in any cloud, platform, and AI environment further. Wiz now supports Databricks as well as new agent studios like AWS Agentcore, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, Microsoft Azure Copilot Studio, and Salesforce Agentforce, so customers gain visibility however their teams choose to build. [...] Eliminate shadow AI: Wiz’s dynamic AI-Bill of Materials (AI-BOM) automatically inventories all AI frameworks, models, and IDE extensions across your environment. This provides complete visibility into what is writing code across your stack, allowing you to track sanctioned corporate tools like Gemini Code Assist and GitHub Copilot while simultaneously uncovering unapproved shadow AI plugins.\n\nYou can learn more about the Wiz announcements here.\n\n### Securing your agents and the agentic web\n\nIn addition to securing your cloud and AI workloads, Google Cloud’s secure-by-design foundation can help you innovate at the speed of AI — from agents to fraud defense to the web. [...] Securing and governing agents with the Gemini Enterprise Agent PlatformTo build, orchestrate, govern, and optimize agents, today we are announcing Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform including:\n\n   Agent Identity to enable access management and AI governance at scale. Our new capability provides agents unique identities to operate autonomously with specific authentication flows, and with scoped human delegation.\n\n   Agent Gateway,which enables policy enforcement for all agent-to-agent and agent-to-tool connections. It governs your enterprise agent traffic and understands agent protocols like MCP and Agent2Agent (A2A) to inspect and secure every agent interaction.",

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/the-new-gemini-enterprise-one-platform-for-agent-development",
      "title": "The new Gemini Enterprise: one platform for agent development | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "1. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform isour new developer platform and evolution of Vertex AI. It brings together our full suite of models, development, and tuning services, with new features for businesses to build, scale, govern, and optimize agents that can work autonomously to execute complex business workflows. Learn more here.\n2. Gemini Enterprise app lets teams discover, create, share, and run AI agents in a single, secure environment. The Gemini Enterprise app is built on the Agent Platform, meaning all governance, security, and identity capabilities come standard, and it integrates with your enterprise data – including data in third-party systems–through connectors. [...] For knowledge workers: Gemini Enterprise app is the front door for AI for every employee in your organization. Knowledge workers can build their own agents with our no-code Agent Designer or leverage a wide range of partner-built agents from Google Cloud Marketplace. And these agents can be easily shared across the organization with full governance and compliance controls built-in. Inbox in Gemini Enterprise provides a central location to manage and monitor agents, including long-running agents. [...] For IT teams: We provide a single control plane for governance in Agent Platform, so every employee can use and share agents with full IT visibility. Both no-code and pro-code agents are managed through a consistent model for identity, security, and auditing – preventing AI sprawl without slowing down innovation.",

      "content": "## What's next\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-04-22 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] Agent builders: Agent Platform Agent Builder is a full-stack\nagentic transformation system that helps you create, manage, and deploy\nAI agents. Use the open-source Agent Development Kit\n(ADK) to build and orchestrate\nagents, and then deploy them to the managed, serverless\nAgent Platform Agent Engine\nfor use at scale in production. Each agent is assigned an Agent Identity\n(Identity and Access Management Principal) for security and a clear\naudit trail.\n\nAccess External Information: Enhance model responses by connecting\nto reliable sources with Grounding, interacting with external APIs using\nFunction Calling, and retrieving information from knowledge bases with RAG.\n\nResponsible AI and Safety: Use built-in safety\nfeatures to block harmful content and ensure responsible AI usage. [...] Model customization: Tailor models to your business to create unique AI\nassets. This ranges from Grounding with your enterprise data\nor Google Search to reduce hallucinations, to using Agent Platform\nTraining for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) or Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning\n(PEFT) of models like Gemini. For more information about model\ncustomization, see\nIntroduction to tuning.\n\nGenerative AI Evaluations: Objectively assess and compare model and agent\nperformance with the\nGen AI evaluation service.\nEnsure safety and compliance by deploying runtime defense features like\nModel Armor to\nproactively inspect and protect against emergent threats,\nsuch as prompt injection and data exfiltration.",

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/scale",
      "title": "Scale your agents  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Scale your agents Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\nGemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers the following to help you seamlessly scale your agents\nin production.\n\nBringing AI agents into production requires a high-performance runtime and a\nsystematic approach to continuous improvement. As organizations build complex,\ndynamic applications, Agent Platform provides a fully managed\nenvironment for developers to handle testing, release management, and\nreliability at a global scale. This allows teams to focus entirely on\napplication creation by providing essential services across four critical areas:\n\n## Agent Runtime\n\n### Deploy and manage agents\n\n### Deploy agents [...] ## Code Execution\n\n### Code Execution overview\n\nGet an overview of Code Execution.\n\n### Code Execution quickstart\n\nGet started with Code Execution.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-04-22 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] ### Setp up monitoring\n\nLearn how to use built-in metrics, custom metrics, and alerts to monitor your agents in Agent Platform.\n\n### Use agents\n\n### Use an agent\n\nUse an agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use a custom agent\n\nUse a custom agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use a LangChain agent\n\nUse a LangChain agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use a LangGraph agent\n\nUse a LangGraph agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use a LlamaIndex Query Pipeline agent\n\nUse a LlamaIndex Query Pipeline agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use an Agent2Agent agent\n\nUse an Agent2Agent agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use an Agent Development Kit agent\n\nUse an Agent Development Kit (ADK) agent with Agent Platform Runtime.\n\n### Use an AG2 agent",

      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/compute/cross-cloud-infrastructure-at-next26",
      "title": "Cross-cloud infrastructure at Next ‘26 | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "### Unified data layer\n\nAI agents are only as powerful as the data they can access and the context they’re given. More applications and platforms are using structured and unstructured data, but it can be difficult to catalog, find, and act on that data at scale, leading to less effective agent interactions. To close the gap, your agents need all of your data brought together into a cohesive, queryable knowledge engine, or unified data layer. This way, your agents can identify and access accurate sources. At Next ‘26, we’re enhancing the unified data layer with: [...] Unlocking AI's full potential in the era of agents requires a secure, adaptive foundation. We call it cross-cloud infrastructure for the agentic enterprise – and at Google Cloud Next ‘26, we’re launching a powerful set of new innovations across four areas:\n\nWhat’s new:\n\nLet’s take a closer look at all the news for each of these four areas.\n\n### Fluid compute\n\nAgentic workloads are dynamic and unpredictable, impacting both traditional enterprise applications and the AI agents themselves. Fluid compute is enabled by Google Compute Engine and Google Kubernetes services working together to dynamically adapt and shift weight in real-time, enabling cost-effective, high-speed AI agents and operational enterprise workloads for all customers. [...] Smart Storage: This solution transforms dark data into a powerful knowledge asset for AI agents and training by embedding new semantic intelligence directly into your data objects. With new Google Cloud Storage capabilities like automated annotation, entity extraction, and semantic search, your agents can instantly find and use the specific data they need — whether it's hidden in spreadsheets, PDFs, or other unstructured formats across your entire organization. This significantly speeds up the development and deployment of your AI solutions. Learn more about storage innovations to accelerate your AI workloads.",
      "score": 0.05860943,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/whats-new-in-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "What’s new in Gemini Enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Integrations for Bring Your Own Model Context Protocol (BYO-MCP): In addition to a rich set of rigorously validated, out-of-the-box connectors, we are introducing support for BYO-MCP. This allows admins to securely connect the Gemini Enterprise app directly to your own custom or third-party business tools, internal workflows, and custom-built applications. This capability expands your automation potential by allowing no-code agents to discover and execute tools hosted on your own servers — tailoring the AI experience to your specific enterprise workflows. [...] Companies are ready to build their agentic task force, but this demands doing so within a secure and governed environment. This includes creating and deploying agents with their own identity, registry, and gateway so they can always be traced, monitored, and managed.\n\nToday, we are expanding Gemini Enterprise to meet this demand, transitioning AI from an isolated productivity tool into a secure, collaborative, autonomous engine for your business. Gemini Enterprise is the end-to-end system for the agentic era, including both the Gemini Enterprise app and our newly announced Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.\n\nAt Google Cloud Next, we are announcing several new capabilities in the Gemini Enterprise app, including the ability to: [...] Enhanced Deep Research agent: Execute complex, multi-step research plans that autonomously synthesize information from the open web and internal company data. By combining iterative reasoning with long-running inference, the Deep Research agent can work in the background for hours to deliver comprehensive, citation-backed reports and executive summaries.\n\n### Team productivity: Projects and Canvas\n\nReal work is a team sport. We are moving AI from a solo conversation to a persistent, shared team asset.",
      "score": 0.049575314,
      "raw_content": null
    },

With Vision Warehouse you can send your app\noutput to a warehouse that generalizes your search work and serves\nmultiple data types and use cases.\n\nVision AI graph in console\n\n## A note on Responsible AI [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Agent Platform Vision overview Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\nAgent Platform Vision is an AI-powered platform to ingest, analyze and store video\n data.       "title": "Introduction to Vertex AI Workbench  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "## What's next\n\n Create a Vertex AI Workbench instance.\n Compare Vertex AI's notebook solutions.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-04-15 UTC.\n\n  \n\n,,[\"Last updated 2026-04-15 UTC.\"],[],[]] [...] For more information, see Use reservations.\n\n## Create instances with third party credentials\n\nYou can create and manage Vertex AI Workbench instances with third party credentials provided by Workforce Identity Federation. Workforce Identity Federation uses your external identity provider (IdP) to grant a group of users access to Vertex AI Workbench instances through a proxy.\n\nAccess to a Vertex AI Workbench instance is granted by assigning a workforce pool principal to the Vertex AI Workbench instance's service account.\n\nFor more information, see Create an instance with third party credentials.\n\n## Tags for Vertex AI Workbench instances [...] Using a custom container that isn't derived from the Google-provided base container (`gcr.io/deeplearning-platform-release/workbench-container:latest`) increases the risks of compatibility issues with our services and isn't supported. Instead, modify the base container to create a custom container that meets your needs, and then create an instance using the custom container.",

    {
      "url": "https://ukg.com/company/newsroom/ukg-once-again-named-leader-hcm-gartner",
      "title": "UKG Once Again Named a Leader in HCM by Gartner | UKG",
      "content": "Delivering a comprehensive experience that connects employee outcomes with critical business objectives, the AI-powered UKG Pro suite spans HCM and workforce management, including compliance, HR, HR service delivery (HRSD), payroll, reporting and analytics, scheduling, talent management, time and attendance, and workforce planning.\n\nThe suite is powered by UKG BryteTM, the only GenAI copilot in the world trained on three decades of Great Place To Work data. With a portfolio of specialty solutions designed specifically to solve unique industry challenges, UKG also expands the definition of HCM to include: [...] “We help organizations make the hard stuff simple, delivering a people-centric suite that provides employees, managers, and executives with the tools that are necessary to create a great place to work, no matter the industry or where in the world people work,” said Hugo Sarrazin, president, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “Our suite, powered by AI and GenAI trained on the world’s largest collection of people, work, and culture data, provides the insights, analysis, and recommendations that organizations need to achieve their goals.” [...] ### About UKG\n\nAt UKG, our purpose is people. We are on a mission to inspire every organization to become a great place to work through HCM technology built for all. More than 80,000 organizations across all sizes, industries, and geographies trust UKG HR, payroll, workforce management, and culture cloud solutions to drive great workplace experiences and make better, more confident people and business decisions. With the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data combined with rich experience using artificial intelligence in the service of people, we connect culture insights with business outcomes to show what’s possible when organizations invest in their people. To learn more, visit ukg.com.",
      "score": 0.87113565,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ukg.com/company/newsroom/ukg-once-again-named-leader-hcm-gartner",
      "title": "UKG Once Again Named a Leader in HCM by Gartner | UKG",
      "content": "Delivering a comprehensive experience that connects employee outcomes with critical business objectives, the AI-powered UKG Pro suite spans HCM and workforce management, including compliance, HR, HR service delivery (HRSD), payroll, reporting and analytics, scheduling, talent management, time and attendance, and workforce planning.\n\nThe suite is powered by UKG BryteTM, the only GenAI copilot in the world trained on three decades of Great Place To Work data. With a portfolio of specialty solutions designed specifically to solve unique industry challenges, UKG also expands the definition of HCM to include: [...] “We help organizations make the hard stuff simple, delivering a people-centric suite that provides employees, managers, and executives with the tools that are necessary to create a great place to work, no matter the industry or where in the world people work,” said Hugo Sarrazin, president, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “Our suite, powered by AI and GenAI trained on the world’s largest collection of people, work, and culture data, provides the insights, analysis, and recommendations that organizations need to achieve their goals.” [...] ### About UKG\n\nAt UKG, our purpose is people. We are on a mission to inspire every organization to become a great place to work through HCM technology built for all. More than 80,000 organizations across all sizes, industries, and geographies trust UKG HR, payroll, workforce management, and culture cloud solutions to drive great workplace experiences and make better, more confident people and business decisions. With the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data combined with rich experience using artificial intelligence in the service of people, we connect culture insights with business outcomes to show what’s possible when organizations invest in their people. To learn more, visit ukg.com.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.ukg.com/about-us/newsroom/ukg-once-again-named-leader-hcm-gartner",
      "title": "UKG Once Again Named a Leader in HCM by Gartner | UKG",
      "content": "Delivering a comprehensive experience that connects employee outcomes with critical business objectives, the AI-powered UKG Pro suite spans HCM and workforce management, including compliance, HR, HR service delivery (HRSD), payroll, reporting and analytics, scheduling, talent management, time and attendance, and workforce planning.\n\nThe suite is powered by UKG BryteTM, the only GenAI copilot in the world trained on three decades of Great Place To Work data. With a portfolio of specialty solutions designed specifically to solve unique industry challenges, UKG also expands the definition of HCM to include: [...] “We help organizations make the hard stuff simple, delivering a people-centric suite that provides employees, managers, and executives with the tools that are necessary to create a great place to work, no matter the industry or where in the world people work,” said Hugo Sarrazin, president, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “Our suite, powered by AI and GenAI trained on the world’s largest collection of people, work, and culture data, provides the insights, analysis, and recommendations that organizations need to achieve their goals.” [...] ### About UKG\n\nAt UKG, our purpose is people. We are on a mission to inspire every organization to become a great place to work through HCM technology built for all. More than 80,000 organizations across all sizes, industries, and geographies trust UKG HR, payroll, workforce management, and culture cloud solutions to drive great workplace experiences and make better, more confident people and business decisions. With the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data combined with rich experience using artificial intelligence in the service of people, we connect culture insights with business outcomes to show what’s possible when organizations invest in their people. To learn more, visit ukg.com.",
      "score": 0.87113565,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ukg.com/company/newsroom/ukg-unveils-bryte-ai-agents-and-capabilities-transform-employee-experiences-ukg-pro-hcm-suite",
      "title": "UKG Unveils Bryte AI Agents and Capabilities to Transform Employee Experiences for the UKG Pro HCM Suite | UKG",
      "content": "“With the rapid evolution of artificial intelligence technology, we are extending our UKG Bryte Generative AI capabilities by developing AI agents that can understand and respond to customer needs in real-time, handling a broad array of tasks, from data analysis, to workflow automation, and customer support,” said Hugo Sarrazin, president, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “Our Bryte AI Agents will work alongside employees and managers, particularly those in frontline roles, taking on tasks that are best suited for technology. This allows people to focus on the aspects of their jobs that have the biggest impact on their organizations.” [...] “In HR and operations, the opportunity for agentic AI is everywhere. There are hundreds of time-consuming checklist-driven tasks and processes that are difficult to automate because steps have multiple paths or require independent judgement,” said Josh Bersin, founder, CEO, and global industry analyst at The Josh Bersin Company. “The UKG connected AI agents have the potential to reduce this workload tremendously, liberating HR teams to focus on building a great workplace experience.” [...] With a commitment to responsible and ethical AI utilization, UKG’s connected agents will allow the organization to determine which tasks can be completed autonomously while ensuring there are built-in checkpoints that require people to review and accept. UKG Bryte is trained on the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data, including three decades of proprietary benchmarks and best practices from Great Place To Work, a UKG company.",
      "score": 0.71425,
      "raw_content": null
    },

There are hundreds of time-consuming checklist-driven tasks and processes that are difficult to automate because steps have multiple paths or require independent judgement,” said Josh Bersin, founder, CEO, and global industry analyst at The Josh Bersin Company. “The UKG connected AI agents have the potential to reduce this workload tremendously, liberating HR teams to focus on building a great workplace experience.” [...] With a commitment to responsible and ethical AI utilization, UKG’s connected agents will allow the organization to determine which tasks can be completed autonomously while ensuring there are built-in checkpoints that require people to review and accept. UKG Bryte is trained on the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data, including three decades of proprietary benchmarks and best practices from Great Place To Work, a UKG company.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.ukg.com/about-us/newsroom/ai-powered-ukg-pro-hcm-suite-built-create-great-workplaces",
      "title": "AI-Powered UKG Pro HCM Suite Built to Create Great Workplaces | UKG",
      "content": "The Great Place To Work Hubis an industry-first solution that transforms HR metrics into opportunities for equity, belonging, and creating great leaders. Leveraging generative AI (GenAI) trained on three decades of Trust Index™ benchmarks, insights, and best practices from Great Place To Work available only to UKG, the Great Place To Work Hub provides organizations with actionable insights to become a great workplace, connecting culture to business outcomes. It coaches HR leaders and people managers with AI-powered recommendations and nudges toward specific actions and behaviors, helping them advance along the Great Place To Work “For All” leadership model. [...] ### About UKG\n\nAt UKG, our purpose is people. We are on a mission to inspire every organization to become a great place to work through HCM technology built for all. More than 80,000 customers across all sizes, industries, and geographies trust UKG HR, payroll, and workforce management cloud solutions to drive great workplace experiences and make better, more confident people and business decisions. With the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data combined with rich experience using artificial intelligence in the service of people, we connect culture insights with business outcomes to show what’s possible when organizations invest in their people. To learn more, visit ukg.com.\n\nFollow UKG on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, Twitter, and YouTube. [...] Strategic collaborations with PayPal to help businesses provide more flexibility to employees around payday and Eightfold AI to support AI-powered recruiting.",

      "content": "The Great Place To Work Hubis an industry-first solution that transforms HR metrics into opportunities for equity, belonging, and creating great leaders.     {
      "url": "https://www.ukg.com/company/newsroom/ukg-named-leader-hcm-twelfth-straight-year",
      "title": "UKG Named a Leader in HCM for Twelfth Straight Year | UKG",
      "content": "“We’re using AI and other emerging technologies to help organizations build great workplaces centered on people while giving managers the insights and recommendations required to quickly make informed decisions that support both employees and the business,” said Hugo Sarrazin, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “By redefining what’s possible when you take care of your people, we’re pushing the entire HCM industry forward to improve the employee experience for people all over the world.” [...] Each year, Nucleus Research assesses enterprise HCM vendors based on criteria including product usability, with UKG earning the highest positioning for both categories. With artificial intelligence (AI), generative AI (GenAI), and machine learning (ML) trained on the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data, UKG Pro is praised by Nucleus Research for giving larger organizations the tools, insights, and resources needed to create great workplace experiences for all people, which drives better business results. [...] Home\n\n## Main navigation\n\nMake better workforce decisions with people-first AI, streamlined HR processes, and payroll clarity.\n\nThree people in a roundtable meeting, discussing.\n\nUKG ranks highest in current offering category among human capital management (HCM) solutions in The Forrester Wave™ Q4 2025 report.\n\nEmpower your frontline and optimize every shift with people-first AI and real-time insights. Plan smarter, schedule faster, and lead with confidence.\n\nDelivery person carries box with scanner.\n\nConsistency signals excellence — and UKG Pro Workforce Management™ proves it, with seven consecutive years as a Leader in the Nucleus Research WFM Technology Value Matrix.\n\nBuild trust by paying your people right, every time, everywhere. Stay compliant globally, reduce risk, and cut costs.",
      "score": 0.6307082,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/au/products/hr-service-delivery.html",
      "title": "HR Service Delivery - HR Management - ServiceNow",
      "content": "The world works with ServiceNow™\n\nHow does HRSD work?    ServiceNow AI Agents operate across HR, IT and workplace systems to route requests, trigger workflows and resolve common tasks automatically. By handling routine work in the background, they speed employee resolutions and free managers and HR to focus on strategic, human-centred work. [...] What is an AI Agent?  \n\nAn AI agent isn't just another chatbot or search tool, it acts. ServiceNow AI Agents understand employee requests, apply the right policies and complete tasks, approvals and processes according to your playbooks. It goes beyond answering questions to actually getting work done.\n\nServiceNow AI Agents, powered by the ServiceNow Platform, automate workflows, streamline operations and adapt to diverse business needs across IT, HR, CRM and more. With features like built-in governance, analytics and text-to-action capabilities, they enhance productivity while aiding alignment with organisational goals.\n\n    What is employee development? [...] Benefits of HRSD     Accelerate work as AI takes action    Replace AI summaries with visible progress. Let AI autonomously resolve employee requests and tasks across systems.      Video CTA     Explore AI Experience      Enhance everyday productivity    Reduce manual work as AI agents autonomously handle approvals and admin tasks — freeing leaders to focus on strategic workforce planning.     Video CTA     Read Ebook      Automate hiring through offboarding    Let AI agents take action across the employee lifecycle. From hiring to exit, AI initiates workflows, updates systems and supports task completion autonomously.     Video CTA     Get eBook      Transform with a single platform    The ServiceNow AI Platform unites AI agents, data and workflows on one enterprise-grade platform built",
      "score": 0.878825,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/uk/products/hr-service-delivery.html",
      "title": "HR Service Delivery - HR Management - ServiceNow",
      "content": "Title: HR Service Delivery - HR Management - ServiceNow\nStreamline HR workflows, boost enterprise HR productivity and improve employee satisfaction with ServiceNow HR Service Delivery, our HR management software. Connect and automate workflows across the enterprise with a single AI platform for business transformation. Find components, patterns and resources to help you start designing experiences on the ServiceNow AI Platform. Learn what the ServiceNow Research team is exploring to improve AI-powered experiences. Discover why ServiceNow is the AI platform for business transformation. HR Service Delivery (HRSD)    Improve productivity by making it easy for employees to get the help and guidance they need, all in one place. One platform for enterprise automation    Optimise manual and inefficient processes from hire to retire through employee self-service, digitisation and AI. Automate essential processes and run them from the ServiceNow AI Platform, a single system of action for the enterprise. Now Assist for HRSD    Accelerate productivity with generative AI experiences on the ServiceNow AI Platform. Universal Request    Provide a unified employee service experience and improve agent collaboration. Put AI to work for people    Empower your people with business-ready AI from ServiceNow. From the front office to the back office and all in between, our intelligent platform harnesses AI to put smart answers at your employees' fingertips, letting you translate that intelligence into action. Built on the ServiceNow AI Platform    HRSD includes AI-powered platform capabilities so you can optimise processes and deliver great experiences. One platform to make work flow    ServiceNow AI Platform is the single cloud application platform all ServiceNow products run on. The ServiceNow AI Platform arms you with the tools and processes you need to improve your security posture. ServiceNow AI Platform    Digitise your entire business, deliver next-generation experiences and accelerate innovation at scale. Look who's winning with ServiceNow. Key features for HRSD        Now Assist for HRSD    Accelerate productivity with generative AI experiences on the ServiceNow AI Platform. Key features for HRSD        Now Assist for HRSD    Accelerate productivity with generative AI experiences on the ServiceNow AI Platform. Data Sheets    HR Service Delivery        Ebooks    Software as Savings     The 3 Imperatives Driving Employee Experience in 2024 and Beyond        Analyst Reports    The Total Economic Impact of ServiceNow HR Service Delivery.",
      "score": 0.8783249,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/community/hrsd/ct-p/human-resources-service-delivery",
      "title": "HRSD - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "Title: HRSD - ServiceNow Community\n## HR Service Delivery (HRSD). * Now Assist for HR: Quick Start Guide. ##### Now Assist for HR: Quick Start Guide. Welcome to the Now Assist for HR Quick Start Guide! If you are looking to learn more about what's new in ServiceNow's HR Service Delivery Zurich release, this session is for you! Agentic AI for Hiring Experiences: Overview   This article presents an overview of agentic AI workflow capabilities available within Hiring Experiences applications in ServiceNow HR Service Delivery (HRSD). Agentic AI for Growth Experiences: Overview   This article presents a comprehensive overview of agentic AI workflow capabilities offered within Growth Experiences applications in ServiceNow HR Service Delivery (HRSD). This blog aims to be an in-depth look at the features coming in the HR Service Delivery space as of the March 2026 store release. ### Featured Resolve HR cases via Agentic AI: Resource Guide. This article has a list of assets that will be useful to get started and implement the Resolve HR case flow via Agentic Workflow(s) available for out of the Box for HR services with fulfillment type - “Manual”. Zurich Release Deep Dive – HRSD     This blog aims to be a deep dive blog on the features coming in HR Service Delivery space as of the July 2025 Store/Zurich Family Release. ### Is it possible to create a different HR case from a single form? Hello experts,Is it possible to create a single form that you can use to apply a different HR case? HR Criteria configured on HR Services only validate against the Subject Person and cannot directly restrict Requestors from searching for ineligible HR Services in the backend (Agent Workspace / Case Creation Page). There is a bulk loader tool in the platform for creating multiple HR Cases in a single step. ### HR Agent Workspace – Change Comments (TinyMCE) background color (production-safe approach) background color (production-safe approach)\"). Hi everyone, I have a requirement in HR Agent Workspace (Next Experience / Polaris UI – Zurich) to change the background color of the Comments  field to red. Context Workspace: HR Agent Workspace UI Framework: Now Experience Comments field is render...",
      "score": 0.8351749,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/community/now-assist-articles/getting-now-assist-for-hr-service-delivery-hrsd-right/ta-p/3299243",
      "title": "Getting Now Assist for HR Service Delivery (HRSD) ... - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "Train Agents and Keep Them in the Loop \n\n  \n\nProblem: Without proper onboarding, agents either don’t trust the AI or trust it too much. \n\n  \n\nWhat to Do: \n\n Use real examples in training to show how AI supports decision-making but doesn’t replace human judgment.\n\n Emphasize the “human-in-the-loop” approach. AI offers help, but the agent still owns the outcome.\n\n Set realistic expectations about the AI’s limitations. It’s only as good as the data it’s working with.\n\n Make it easy for agents to flag issues or give feedback inside the workspace.\n\n Schedule regular check-ins to gather input, answer questions, and make improvements based on what is working. [...] Maintain consistency and quality via governance.\n\n Tag articles with clear metadata like topic and audience, so they’re easier for the AI to find and rank.\n\n Add summaries and section headings to improve readability for both agents and AI models.\n\n Assign owners to each knowledge article so there’s accountability for keeping content up to date.\n\n Create a regular process to review and improve knowledge content based on what agents need.\n\nExample: There are implementations that deliver weak search results and irrelevant agent recommendations due to inconsistent tagging. A targeted cleanup and retagging project led to a measurable improvement in response to relevance. \n\n  \n\nRoll Out Features Gradually and Plan for Growth [...] Continue to gather feedback and monitor usage analytics regularly to determine how users interact with the implemented AI features. Identify what’s working, what’s underused, and where users are struggling.\n\n Outdated or irrelevant articles reduce AI effectiveness. Set a recurring schedule to review and refresh knowledge base content. Encourage teams to contribute new articles, update existing ones, and share insights. A rich, well-maintained knowledge base is the foundation of effective AI.\n\n Continue to train agents and embed an ongoing OCM plan. Make sure agents understand how to use AI tools effectively and that adoption is supported through regular communication and reinforcement.\n\n### Final Thoughts",
      "score": 0.8296166,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/community/hrsd-forum/hr-service-delivery-hrsd-a-smarter-way-to-support-employees/m-p/3461036",
      "title": "HR Service Delivery (HRSD): A Smarter Way to Suppo... - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "Title: HR Service Delivery (HRSD): A Smarter Way to Suppo... - ServiceNow Community\n* HR Service Delivery (HRSD): A Smarter Way to Suppo... # HR Service Delivery (HRSD): A Smarter Way to Support Employees. In today’s digital workplace, employees expect HR services to be **fast, transparent, and easy to access**. This is where **Human Resource Service Delivery (HRSD)** plays a crucial role. HRSD helps organizations modernize HR operations by delivering employee services through a **single, unified platform**. ## What is Human Resource Service Delivery (HRSD)? **Human Resource Service Delivery (HRSD)** is a structured approach to delivering HR services efficiently using automation, workflows, and self-service capabilities. With **ServiceNow HRSD**, employees can:. Instead of employees searching for the right HR contact, HRSD provides **one central channel** for all HR-related needs. HRSD directly impacts both **employee experience** and **business productivity**. * Reduced manual effort for HR teams. * Consistent and compliant HR service delivery. When HR services are simple and reliable, employees spend less time following up and more time focusing on their core responsibilities. * The employee submits a payroll request through the HR portal. ### HR Case & Knowledge Management. HR teams can create, manage, and resolve HR cases while maintaining a centralized knowledge base. HRSD automates employee lifecycle processes that span multiple departments. ### HR Integrations. ### HR Performance Analytics. These insights help HR leaders make **data-driven decisions**. * Upload and access HR documents. * Track HR requests on mobile. ## How HRSD Creates Business Value. By centralizing HR services, HRSD:. * Allows HR teams to focus on strategic initiatives. The left one looks quite similar to the images from here: HR lifecycle events 101—What are they and how do you configure them? ‎01-06-2026 01:10 AM  - edited  ‎01-06-2026 01:14 AM. @UmesH77 the blog is from 2018 and it's in the screenshot you shared:.       "title": "Virtual Agent for HR Service Delivery - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "## What is the ServiceNow Virtual Agent?\n\nServiceNow Virtual Agent is a chatbot solution native to the Now Platform which was recently launched during our London release. With Virtual Agent you can build conversational workflows for employees and customers to resolve common HR requests and inquiries via the chat interface of your choice.\n\nEnhance the Employee Experience using ServiceNow Virtual Agent\n\nWith Virtual Agent for HR Service Delivery, you can boost productivity across the entire HR department by:\n\n1. Improving service, offering employees 24/7 automated support for common HR issues\n2. Reducing agent workload, empowering your HR staff to focus on more meaningful work\n3.       "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/community/hrsd-articles/hr-service-delivery-welcome-guide/ta-p/2310525",
      "title": "HR Service Delivery Welcome Guide - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "HR Services, HR Agent Workspace, HR Security, Dashboards and Reporting, and others then you create the foundation upon which you can build an ever improving, robust HR professional experiences that enables engaging and productive employee experiences.  Your HRSD service delivery solution should be designed to provide services to employees in the most effective manner possible.    Learning Resources on HR Service Delivery  The one place to go and read about HRSD applications in detail is the Product Documentation. Following two courses will help you understand some of the key HRSD applications and how they work.    Human Resources (HR) Fundamentals – Instructor Led  HRSD Fundamentals – On Demand   If you are new to ServiceNow, we also recommend that you take this instructor-led course of [...] |  |  |  |\n --- \n| Welcome to ServiceNow®  HR Service Delivery  Are you ready to start your HR Service Delivery (HRSD) implementation journey? With ServiceNow HRSD, you’ll boost employee productivity and engagement, provide efficient, personalized HR services, and help your employees stay connected with HR anytime and anywhere. This guide gives you the information you need to get going fast, including proven guidance and links to valuable resources—all designed to set you on the path to success. While this guide is primarily designed for ServiceNow platform owners, it also contains useful information if you have a different role in your organization.  Let’s get started! |  |  | [...] | Start by familiarizing yourself with HRSD  and Employee Experience  HRSD is a scoped application. There are two types of available application scopes: private application scope (commonly referred to as scoped apps) and global scope. Each ServiceNow application has an application scope that determines which of its resources are available to other parts of the system. Application scoping ensures that one application does not impact another application. You can specify what parts of the application other applications can access by setting application access settings. More details of a scoped application can be found here within the ‘Understanding Application Scope on the Now Platform’ community article.  HR Service Delivery is a suite of applications that drive employee productivity from",

      "title": "HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Professional Plus Suite - ServiceNow University",
      "content": "Skip to page content)Skip to chat). February Monthly Speed Networking Event - A ServiceNow University Community Event. Join us on February 3rd for a virtual networking event where you will have the opportunity to meet other learners from different industries and career paths, build lasting connections globally, and learn more about opportunities in the ServiceNow ecosystem! HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Professional Plus Suite. 2 Days 19 Hours 25 Minutes. 2 Days 19 Hours 25 Minutes. # HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Professional Plus Suite. This Learning Path provides all required training and exams to become ServiceNow Certified HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Pro Plus Suite. ## Achievements. By completing this path you will achieve the following:. HR Service Delivery Professional Plus Suite Path. Points help advance your overall rank. ## About this Path. This Learning Path provides all the required training and exams to become a ServiceNow Certified Now Assist HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Pro Plus Suite. Engage in several types of training options to learn and demonstrate your advanced skills on how to incorporate Performance Analytics, Predictive Intelligence, and Virtual Agent, and Now Assist in your next HR Service Delivery implementation. Throughout this learning path, attendees will learn to:. Review the ServiceNow HRSD solution and its capabilities. Configure HR criteria, User criteria, document templates, and HR Profiles. Define and manage HR Services, Categories, HR Service Catalogs, Catalog Items, and Lifecycle Events. Configure HR Case and Task Templates. Create HR Knowledge articles and learn about the Knowledge Management lifecycle. Explore Skills and auto-assign HR Cases based on skills. Learn about the security constraints that determine access to HR data. Gain practical knowledge of HR Case Management and HR surveys. Drill into results via reporting and dashboards. Configure HR Virtual Agent conversations. Configuring and utilizing the capabilities of Now Assist. # Content. Human Resources Service Delivery (HRSD) Professional Suite. Now Assist for HR Service Delivery (HRSD) Implementer. To opt-out of us sharing information relating to cookies and similar technologies with third parties for advertising purposes, select \"Opt-Out\". To exercise other rights you may have related to cookies, select \"More Info\" or submit a request through the \"Your Privacy Rights\" page.",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://newsroom.workday.com/2025-09-16-Workday-and-Microsoft-to-Deliver-Unified-AI-Agent-Experience-for-the-Enterprise",
      "title": "Workday and Microsoft to Deliver Unified AI Agent Experience for the Enterprise - Sep 16, 2025",
      "content": "Enhance Human-Agent Collaboration: The new integration between Workday and Microsoft's agent systems will allow seamless interoperability between different AI agents. For example, an employee could ask a Microsoft Employee Self-Service Agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot to update career goals or submit a peer review. The request would be handed off to the Workday agent inline, allowing the employee to complete the workflow in Workday agent without leaving Microsoft Employee Self-Service Agent. This will help deliver a unified, secure, and context-preserving experience in the flow of work. [...] \"Our partnership with Workday is a breakthrough in delivering the trust organizations need to adopt AI,\" said Charles Lamanna, president, business and industry Copilot, Microsoft. \"By seamlessly registering agents built with Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry into Workday's Agent System of Record, we're giving customers full visibility into their AI ecosystem. This integration equips leaders with actionable analytics to measure ROI, enforce governance, and ensure their AI agents are secure and delivering real value.\"\n\nEmpowering the Intelligent Enterprise\n\nThis latest innovation between Workday and Microsoft will create a seamless and secure experience for building and managing AI agents across the enterprise, allowing customers to: [...] SAN FRANCISCO, Sept. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Workday Rising 2025 -- Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced a new collaboration with Microsoft to help organizations securely manage their people and AI agents across their platforms. Customers that build AI agents using Microsoft Azure AI Foundry and Copilot Studio will be able to easily register and manage them within the Workday Agent System of Record (ASOR). This integration between Workday ASOR and Microsoft Entra Agent ID will help ensure every agent built on Microsoft agent building platforms has a verified identity and the right business context to operate safely and effectively across the business.\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")",
      "score": 0.99995697,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

This integration equips leaders with actionable analytics to measure ROI, enforce governance, and ensure their AI agents are secure and delivering real value.\"\n\nEmpowering the Intelligent Enterprise\n\nThis latest innovation between Workday and Microsoft will create a seamless and secure experience for building and managing AI agents across the enterprise, allowing customers to:\n\nFor More Information [...] ###### Press Release\n\n# Workday and Microsoft to Deliver Unified AI Agent Experience for the Enterprise\n\nNew Integration Between Workday Agent System of Record and Microsoft Entra Agent ID to Elevate Human-Agent Collaboration\n\nPartnership Provides Companies with the Business Context to Effectively Manage Their AI Agents and Unlock True AI Value",
