      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://newsroom.workday.com/2025-09-16-Workday-and-Microsoft-to-Deliver-Unified-AI-Agent-Experience-for-the-Enterprise?refCamp=7014X000002s3hcQAA",
      "title": "Workday and Microsoft to Deliver Unified AI Agent Experience for the Enterprise - Sep 16, 2025",
      "content": "SAN FRANCISCO, Sept. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Workday Rising 2025 -- Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced a new collaboration with Microsoft to help organizations securely manage their people and AI agents across their platforms. Customers that build AI agents using Microsoft Azure AI Foundry and Copilot Studio will be able to easily register and manage them within the Workday Agent System of Record (ASOR). This integration between Workday ASOR and Microsoft Entra Agent ID will help ensure every agent built on Microsoft agent building platforms has a verified identity and the right business context to operate safely and effectively across the business.\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\") [...] \"Our partnership with Workday is a breakthrough in delivering the trust organizations need to adopt AI,\" said Charles Lamanna, president, business and industry Copilot, Microsoft. \"By seamlessly registering agents built with Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry into Workday's Agent System of Record, we're giving customers full visibility into their AI ecosystem. This integration equips leaders with actionable analytics to measure ROI, enforce governance, and ensure their AI agents are secure and delivering real value.\"\n\nEmpowering the Intelligent Enterprise\n\nThis latest innovation between Workday and Microsoft will create a seamless and secure experience for building and managing AI agents across the enterprise, allowing customers to:\n\nFor More Information\n\nAbout Workday [...] This press release contains forward-looking statements including, among other things, statements regarding Workday's plans, beliefs, and expectations. These forward-looking statements are based only on currently available information and our current beliefs, expectations, and assumptions. Because forward-looking statements relate to the future, they are subject to inherent risks, uncertainties, assumptions, and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict and many of which are outside of our control. If the risks materialize, assumptions prove incorrect, or we experience unexpected changes in circumstances, actual results could differ materially from the results implied by these forward-looking statements, and therefore you should not rely on any forward-looking statements.",
      "score": 0.9998148,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/discussions/microsoft365copilot/copilot-integration-with-salesforce-or-workday-using-copilot-studio/4073815",
      "title": "Copilot Integration with Salesforce or Workday using Copilot Studio | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "0.91, 0.51, [...] _typename\":\"MainSideQuiltSection\",\"columnMap\":{\"main\":[{\"id\":\"messages [...] \",\"title\":\"Microsoft 365 Copilot\",\"shortTitle\":\"Microsoft 365",
      "score": 0.9997086,

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft365copilotblog/microsoft-365-copilot-app-learning-series-real%E2%80%91world-scenarios-for-your-workday/4502132",
      "title": "Microsoft 365 Copilot app learning series: Real‑world scenarios for your workday | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "## What this series covers\n\nAcross four short, focused videos, we walk through how Microsoft 365 Copilot supports everyday work—from starting your day, to running better meetings, to catching up when you miss one.\n\nEach session is practical and scenario‑based, showing how Copilot works across the Microsoft 365 Copilot app, Microsoft Teams, and Outlook.\n\nHere’s what you can expect:\n\n### Getting started with the Microsoft 365 Copilot app: A guided tour\n\nStart with the basics. This introductory session walks through the features in the Microsoft 365 Copilot app that don't require a subscription. It shows how the Microsoft 365 Copilot app serves as a central hub for your workday.\n\nYou’ll learn how to: [...] Brand Logo\n\n## Blog Post\n\n# Microsoft 365 Copilot app learning series: Real‑world scenarios for your workday\n\nPatrick_Gan's avatar\nIcon for Microsoft rank\n\nIf you’ve heard more about Microsoft 365 Copilot and wondering where to begin, you’re not alone. With AI becoming part of everyday work, many people are looking for guidance that’s practical, approachable, and grounded in real workflows—not just feature lists.\n\nThat’s why we’re excited to introduce a new training video series designed to help you get started with the Microsoft 365 Copilot app, one step at a time. This series is built for people who want to understand how Copilot fits into a real workday—whether you’re brand new or just starting to explore what’s possible.\n\n## What this series covers [...] ### Share this page\n\nShare to LinkedIn\nShare to Facebook\nShare to X\nShare to Reddit\nShare to Blue Sky\nSubscribe to RSS\nShare to Email",
      "score": 0.9996923,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://marketplace.workday.com/en-US/apps/547968/benefits-copilot/overview",
      "title": "Benefits Copilot | Workday Marketplace",
      "content": "A faster, easier way to connect benefits vendors and manage enrollment on Workday—no costly IT work or firms needed. A faster, easier way to connect benefits vendors and manage enrollment on Workday—no costly IT work or firms needed. Noyo Benefits Copilot is a software platform that augments the Workday Benefits Module, making it easy to connect vendors and automate enrollment. HR teams can activate vendors on-demand—in weeks, not months—using Noyo’s broad network of pre-built integrations. Many vendors offer real-time connectivity through Noyo. HR teams use the Benefits Copilot app to track every enrollment change, confirm success, and automatically detect errors—with deep insights for faster resolution. Powered by advanced API and AI technology, Benefits Copilot is Workday-certified and used by Workday’s own HR team. * **Pre-built vendor integrations:** Choose from a broad network of ready-to-use integrations, Noyo handles the rest. Go live with multiple vendors in weeks without the costs of complex IT work and consultants. * **No ongoing maintenance:** Noyo manages all vendor connection maintenance and upgrades, eliminating $100K–$500K in annual IT costs at scale. * **Powered by leading API + AI:** Noyo’s real-time API integration with Workday ensures your data stays in sync. * **Add more vendors faster at a lower cost:** Connect multiple vendors in weeks. * **Significant cost savings at scale:** Noyo handles all setup and maintenance, saving employers hundreds of thousands in ongoing IT and vendor management costs. * **A better employee experience:** Quickly and confidently answer employee questions about enrollment statuses without chasing down data or waiting on vendors and firms. The Workday Design Approved badge is awarded to Silver level partners based on the review of an approved use case and proposed reference architecture for their integration. By Beamery, Inc. Beamery’s Talent Operating System allows enterprises to attract, engage and retain top talent and manage the talent journey, delivering an improved experience at scale, and accelerating time-to-hire.The Beamery-Workday Certified CRM integration is the only certified CRM integration with Workday. With the Beamery-Workday Certified CRM integration, new candidates and candidate updates in Beamery can be pushed directly into Workday, and Applicant and Candidate data that resides in Workday is pulled in the opposite direction to Beamery, keeping both systems fully in sync.",
      "score": 0.9978677,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next/welcome-to-google-cloud-next26",
      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "Canvas gives our customers an interactive editor directly within Gemini Enterprise. It allows teams to easily create and edit Docs and Slides, and even export to Microsoft 365 files, within the same experience.\n Agent Gallery provides access to third-party agents from partners like Adobe, Atlassian, Lovable, and ServiceNow, and is adding more third-party connectors for Asana, Mailchimp, Workday, and more. These integrations enable your agents to retrieve data and execute tasks with your systems-of-record. [...] Gemini momentum with ISVs: Leading companies like Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds more are deeply integrating Gemini into their products, bringing more AI-powered features and agents to their users.\n More enterprise-grade agents from partners: Already, thousands of agents built by our partners are available in Google Cloud Marketplace. Now, you can access select agents directly through Gemini Enterprise, making it easier for you to find the right agent for your team’s needs from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow and Workday. [...] Ecosystem Integrations via Agent Marketplace help instantly deploy specialized agents from partners like Atlassian, Box, Lovable, Oracle, ServiceNow, Workday, and many more—directly from Gemini Enterprise. Also, you can leverage new integrations that enable agents from partners that use a unified Agent Registry with reusable skills and managed environments to treat all protocols as first-class citizens.\n Model Context Protocol allowsyou to connect our Agent Platform to any MCP Server. We are also exposing Google Cloud Services as MCP to allow you to interact seamlessly with Google Cloud from any agent.",
      "score": 0.51952136,

    {
      "url": "https://www.zdnet.com/article/google-chrome-enterprise-update-auto-browse-skills-gemini-summary/",
      "title": "Google brings Auto Browse and Skills to Chrome Enterprise - and a new 'Gemini Summary' | ZDNET",
      "content": "1. Auto browse\n\n## 2. Skills\n\nGoogle is also bringing \"Skills\" to Chrome Enterprise, letting you save and reuse AI workflows. This one-click feature launched recently for consumers in Chrome, but is now coming to Workspace. The idea is, if you find yourself asking Gemini the same thing throughout the workday, you can save that prompt as a Skill and run it instantly on any page in your browser.\n\nAlso: I tested ChatGPT Plus vs. Gemini Pro to see which is better\n\nJust type / in the Gemini side panel, select your Skill, and it runs immediately on the page you're viewing. Over time, you can build a library of repeatable tasks customized to how you or your team works. As a safeguard, most Skills require confirmation before running. [...] 3. Gemini Summary\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\nHow to get started with Goose, a free open-source alternative to Claude Code\nHow to remove Copilot AI from Windows 11 today\nComputer trash symbol on dynamic digital background. Glowing digital data delete icon abstract 3d illustration. Bright recycling sign.\nIs that an AI image?\nHow to get started with Goose, a free open-source alternative to Claude Code\nHow to remove Copilot AI from Windows 11 today\nComputer trash symbol on dynamic digital background. Glowing digital data delete icon abstract 3d illustration. Bright recycling sign.\nIs that an AI image?\n\n### \n\n### Related\n\nasus-proart-pa27-forward\n\n### Motorola Moto G (2026) review: Why I'd pick this $200 phone over competing models\n\nevergreen-deals-imagery [...] Google said Workspace prompts are not used to train its AI models.\n\n2. Skills\n\n## 3. Gemini Summary\n\nSince employees may already be using AI tools on their own, Google is flagging a \"Shadow AI\" risk where sensitive data could be shared with unmonitored tools. To address this, Chrome Enterprise now gives IT teams visibility into AI usage across the workplace, including which tools employees access, unusual behavior from extensions or agents, and unapproved tools.",
      "score": 0.069541745,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "url": "https://community.ibm.com/community/user/blogs/reza-beykzadeh/2025/06/19/automating-aws-infrastructure-creation-bedrock",
      "title": "Automating AWS Infrastructure Creation with Amazon Bedrock Agent",
      "content": "By integrating AWS Bedrock, Lambda, and Amazon OpenSearch Serverless, this solution leverages the full power of automation, eliminating manual steps while ensuring that cloud environments are provisioned securely and efficiently. The Bedrock Agentic interface takes user input and translates it into real-time infrastructure actions, making cloud management much more accessible and responsive. [...] With the deployment steps and testing guidance outlined, you are now equipped to test and deploy this fully automated system. The Bedrock Agentic feature not only simplifies cloud resource provisioning but also reduces human error, ensuring a smooth and reliable cloud infrastructure setup. By automating the management of your AWS resources, you can scale your cloud environment quickly, securely, and with minimal effort. [...] 5. Test Additional Commands\n\nTo further test the solution, try adding more complex requests. For example:\n\nThe agent should handle these requests by invoking the appropriate Lambda functions defined in the CloudFormation template.\n\nExample test command to create a subnet:\n\nplease create a public and a private subnet with equal cidr in us gov west 1a\n\n6. Troubleshooting\n\nConclusion\n\nThe AWS Bedrock Agentic feature allows for dynamic, serverless infrastructure management by automating the process of provisioning resources based on user inputs. With Bedrock's ability to process natural language requests, you can easily deploy, manage, and modify AWS resources such as VPCs, EC2 instances, and security groups — all triggered by simple commands.",

      "title": "Build AWS Architect Agent with Bedrock - AWS",
      "content": "搜索\n\n### Amazon Web Services\n\nIn this video, AWS experts demonstrate how to build an AWS Solutions Architect Agent using Amazon Bedrock. The agent leverages the AWS Well-Architected Framework and can deploy Lambda functions. The presenters walk through creating tools for citing the framework and deploying code, then show how to connect these tools to an AI agent in Amazon Bedrock. They explain concepts like Chain of Thought and demonstrate querying the finished agent. This practical guide showcases the power of combining large language models with custom tools to create AI assistants tailored for AWS architecture tasks.     {
      "url": "https://venturebeat.com/ai/aws-brings-multi-agent-orchestration-to-bedrock",
      "title": "AWS brings multi-agent orchestration to Bedrock | VentureBeat",
      "content": "“Using multi-agent collaboration in Amazon Bedrock, customers can get more accurate results by creating and assigning specialized agents for specific steps of a project and accelerate tasks by orchestrating multiple agents working in parallel,” AWS said.\n\nCustomers build their specialized agents on Bedrock, and then make a supervisor or orchestrator agent to help manage the other agents. AWS said the supervisor agent “handles the coordination, like breaking up and routing tasks to the right agents, giving specific agents access to the information they need to complete their work and determining what actions can be processed in parallel.” [...] AWS first introduced agentic capabilities in 2023 with the release of Agents on Bedrock, which offered enterprises a way to start building agents. During its re:Invent event this week, AWS customers like PagerDuty and GitLab announced new agents that let users develop their applications on their platforms and use AWS agents to enhance their workflows.\n\n## Multiple agents becoming the norm [...] # AWS brings multi-agent orchestration to Bedrock\n\nCredit: VentureBeat generated with MidJourney\n\nAWS is doubling down on AI agents with the announcement of multiagent capabilities on its Amazon Bedrock platform.\n\nDuring his keynote at the AWS re:Invent conference, AWS CEO Matt Garman said customers building agents on Bedrock wanted a means to make agents that work together.",
      "score": 0.3553503,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agent-os-aws-bedrock.html",
      "title": "Agent OS expands to AWS with Bedrock integration: PwC",
      "content": "Shared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\n# PwC expands agent OS to AWS, bringing Bedrock and AWS-native tools to enterprise AI workflows\n\nPaige Hayes\n\nGlobal AWS Alliance Leader, PwC US\n\nTodd Supplee\n\nPartner, US AWS Practice and Alliance Leader, PwC US\n\nAdam Hood\n\nPrincipal, AWS Data & AI Leader, PwC US\n\nPwC has announced a major expansion of its enterprise AI orchestration platform, agent OS, with support for Amazon Web Services (AWS) environments and native integration of Amazon Bedrock. This enhancement enables organizations to run agent OS within AWS and orchestrate AI workflows using Bedrock-hosted models alongside AWS-native tools —without the need to refactor for another cloud provider.\n\n## What’s new [...] ## What’s new\n\nThe AWS-compatible release introduces a set of integrated tools and execution support designed to enhance enterprise AI workflows in native AWS environments:\n\n## Why it matters to AWS customers\n\nScaling AI agents starts here — with faster setup, native integration and no need to re-architect.\n\nThis expansion supports PwC’s commitment to helping businesses scale secure, responsible AI within their existing infrastructure. With support for AWS, agent OS now enables hybrid cloud strategies by bringing together AWS-native tooling and agent OS’s enterprise orchestration capabilities in a single solution. [...] PwC will continue to evolve agent OS with support for other leading platforms and ecosystem tools — helping enterprise customers operationalize AI at scale while maintaining control, transparency and trust.\n\n## Explore our new AI Agent solutions on AWS Marketplace\n\n## Explore PwC’s agent OS — the AI agent platform for enterprises\n\n## {{filterContent.facetedTitle}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC. All rights reserved. PwC refers to the PwC network and/or one or more of its member firms, each of which is a separate legal entity. Please see www.pwc.com/structure for further details.",
      "score": 0.3180195,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "http://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agent-os-aws-bedrock.html",
      "title": "Agent OS expands to AWS with Bedrock integration: PwC",
      "content": "Shared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\n# PwC expands agent OS to AWS, bringing Bedrock and AWS-native tools to enterprise AI workflows\n\nPaige Hayes\n\nGlobal AWS Alliance Leader, PwC US\n\nTodd Supplee\n\nPartner, US AWS Practice and Alliance Leader, PwC US\n\nAdam Hood\n\nPrincipal, AWS Data & AI Leader, PwC US\n\nPwC has announced a major expansion of its enterprise AI orchestration platform, agent OS, with support for Amazon Web Services (AWS) environments and native integration of Amazon Bedrock. This enhancement enables organizations to run agent OS within AWS and orchestrate AI workflows using Bedrock-hosted models alongside AWS-native tools —without the need to refactor for another cloud provider.\n\n## What’s new [...] ## What’s new\n\nThe AWS-compatible release introduces a set of integrated tools and execution support designed to enhance enterprise AI workflows in native AWS environments:\n\n## Why it matters to AWS customers\n\nScaling AI agents starts here — with faster setup, native integration and no need to re-architect.\n\nThis expansion supports PwC’s commitment to helping businesses scale secure, responsible AI within their existing infrastructure. With support for AWS, agent OS now enables hybrid cloud strategies by bringing together AWS-native tooling and agent OS’s enterprise orchestration capabilities in a single solution. [...] PwC will continue to evolve agent OS with support for other leading platforms and ecosystem tools — helping enterprise customers operationalize AI at scale while maintaining control, transparency and trust.\n\n## Explore our new AI Agent solutions on AWS Marketplace\n\n## Explore PwC’s agent OS — the AI agent platform for enterprises\n\n## {{filterContent.facetedTitle}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC. All rights reserved. PwC refers to the PwC network and/or one or more of its member firms, each of which is a separate legal entity. Please see www.pwc.com/structure for further details.",

      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/semantic-kernel/tag/aws-bedrock",
      "title": "AWS Bedrock - Tag | Microsoft Agent Framework",
      "content": "# Showing tag results for AWS Bedrock\n\n### Integration of AWS Bedrock Agents in Semantic Kernel\n\nOverview of AWS Bedrock Agents\nAWS Bedrock Agents provide a managed service that facilitates the experimentation and rapid deployment of AI agents. Users can leverage proprietary AWS models as well as a diverse selection of models from various providers available on AWS Bedrock.\nSemantic Kernel's Integration with AWS Bedrock\nSemantic Kernel now ...\n\n## Popular topics\n\n## Links\n\n## Design\n\n## Archive\n\n## Follow this blog\n\nlinkedin\n\n## Stay informed\n\n##### Code Block",

    {
      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/integration-of-aws-bedrock-agents-in-semantic-kernel",
      "title": "Integration of AWS Bedrock Agents in Semantic Kernel | Microsoft Agent Framework",
      "content": "heart\n\n# Integration of AWS Bedrock Agents in Semantic Kernel\n\n## \n\n## \n\n### Overview of AWS Bedrock Agents\n\nAWS Bedrock Agents provide a managed service that facilitates the experimentation and rapid deployment of AI agents. Users can leverage proprietary AWS models as well as a diverse selection of models from various providers available on AWS Bedrock.\n\n### Semantic Kernel’s Integration with AWS Bedrock\n\nSemantic Kernel now integrates with AWS Bedrock Agents, enabling users to leverage kernel functions alongside features such as code interpretation and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) powered by the AWS knowledge base. If you manage resources on AWS and are exploring a multi-cloud AI solution, this integration provides a valuable opportunity.\n\nExperimental [...] In .Net, do the following\n\n`using Amazon.BedrockAgent;\nusing Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Bedrock;\nusing Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Bedrock.Extensions;\n...\nvar client = new AmazonBedrockAgentClient();\nvar agentModel = await client.CreateAndPrepareAgentAsync(new()\n{\nAgentName = \"[YOUR AGENT NAME]\",\nDescription = \"[YOUR AGENT DESCRIPTION]\",\nInstruction = \"[YOUR AGENT INSTRUCTION]\",\nAgentResourceRoleArn = \"[YOUR AGENT RESOURCE ROLE ARN]\",\nFoundationModel = \"[YOUR FOUNDATIONLA MODEL]\",\n});\nvar bedrockAgent = new BedrockAgent(agentModel, client);`\n\n##### Use an existing agent\n\nYou can also retrieve agents that you created previously and use it in Semantic Kernel.\n\nIn Python, do the following [...] In Python, do the following\n\n`import boto3\nfrom semantic_kernel.agents.bedrock.bedrock_agent import BedrockAgent\nAGENT_ID = \"[YOUR AGENT ID]\"\nclient = boto3.client(\"bedrock-agent\")\nagent_model = client.get_agent(agentId=AGENT_ID)[\"agent\"]\nbedrock_agent = BedrockAgent(agent_model)`\n\nIn .Net, do the following\n\n`using Amazon.BedrockAgent;\nusing Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Bedrock;\n...\nvar client = new AmazonBedrockAgentClient();\nvar getAgentResponse = await this.Client.GetAgentAsync(new() { AgentId = \"[YOUR AGENT ID]\" });\nvar bedrockAgent = new BedrockAgent(getAgentResponse.Agent, client);`\n\n##### Interacting with the agent\n\nInteracting with the agent is simple and intuitive.\n\nIn Python, simply do the following",
      "score": 0.30551133,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 3.08,
  "request_id": "fe0b7e3a-8649-4a78-ac3d-1443a59e3ac1"
}

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/partners/how-google-cloud-partner-ecosystem-is-building-the-agentic-enterprise",
      "title": "How Google Cloud partner ecosystem is building the agentic enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "KPMG is deploying Gemini Enterprise with a life sciences company and launching a new Financial Close Companion agent built with Workday and Google Cloud.\n\nKyndryl is deepening its Google Cloud partnership with expanded Google Distributed Cloud services for sovereign, AI-ready applications.\n\nMcKinsey is launching the McKinsey Google Transformation Group to accelerate enterprise AI outcomes with Gemini Enterprise, combining its strategic expertise with Google's AI stack to help organizations scale agentic transformation\n\nPwC is launching a dedicated Google Cloud AI Center of Excellence to help organizations scale AI adoption, pairing industry expertise with Gemini Enterprise to deploy AI agents that reason, act, and automate processes at scale. [...] Today, Agent Gallery provides access to a wide range of third-party agents. These agents have been built on top of our secure, enterprise-grade infrastructure, meaning customers can deploy them within their businesses with the highest levels of governance and confidence. Today, this includes agents built by Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and more.\n\n### Empowering global consulting partners to drive AI transformations\n\nToday, Google Cloud’s global consulting and systems integrator partners offer customers more than 330,000 experts trained on implementing Google AI. At Next, we are expanding our partnerships with every major systems integrator, including: [...] Additionally, we will begin to embed teams of Google Cloud engineers with a subset of global partners, including Accenture, Capgemini, Cognizant, Deloitte, HCLTech, PwC, and TCS in order to help their customers more rapidly prototype and deploy AI agents within their businesses. AI-native services partners, including Altimetrik, Artefact, Covasant, Deepsense, Distyl.ai, Northslope, Quantium, Tribe.ai, and Tryolabs will launch Gemini Enterprise practices, receiving credits for sandbox development, technical upskilling, and referral opportunities.",
      "score": 0.78749067,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next/welcome-to-google-cloud-next26",
      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "Gemini momentum with ISVs: Leading companies like Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds more are deeply integrating Gemini into their products, bringing more AI-powered features and agents to their users.\n More enterprise-grade agents from partners: Already, thousands of agents built by our partners are available in Google Cloud Marketplace. Now, you can access select agents directly through Gemini Enterprise, making it easier for you to find the right agent for your team’s needs from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow and Workday. [...] Manufacturing: Bosch, EcoVadis, GE Appliances, Tata Steel\n Governments:, German Armed Forces, Maryland, NATO, UK Department for Transport, U.S. Department of Energy, U.S. Department of Transportation, U.S. Food and Drug Administration, SENAI Brazil\n ISVs: Lovable, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP,  ServiceNow, UKG, Workday\n Systems Integrators: Accenture, BCG, Capgemini, Cognizant, Deloitte, HCLTech, IBM, Infosys, Kyndryl, KPMG, McKinsey, PwC, TCS\n Startups: Emergent AI, Jusbrasil, Notion, Parallel Web Systems, Reve, Safe Superintelligence, Thinking Machines Lab, Unico\n SMBs: Apex Leaders, B3Networks, Constructora Las Galias, OneUnited Bank, Papel Semente, Premji Invest, Thrive Restaurant Group\n And many, many more showcased on our website. [...] At Next, we’re highlighting new ways that our partners can help you access Google Cloud AI across more of your applications and interfaces, including:",
      "score": 0.6862234,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/partner-built-agents-available-in-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Partner-built agents available in Gemini Enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "What's new: Today, we’re bringing partner-built agents from our Agent Marketplace directly into the Agent Gallery inside the Gemini Enterprise app. This creates a powerful, centrally governed hub for discovering and managing specialized AI from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and more.\n\nWhy this matters for your business: With millions of paid seats across thousands of companies, the Gemini Enterprise app allows organizations to automate complex, end-to-end workflows in a single, unified environment. Five key benefits include: [...] Sana from Workday: Sana Self-Service Agent instantly finds and summarizes information from Workday and other knowledge sources, providing personalized answers and assistance with tasks. With over 300 skills across areas like pay, time, and absence, the agent handles everyday HR and finance tasks globally, reducing support tickets and allowing teams to focus on high-value work.\n\nSaviynt: Identity Security for AI enables continuous discovery and inventory of agents, identifying their functions, access, and risks. It treats agents as first-class identities with defined purposes and accountable owners. This blend of posture visibility and enforced accountability converts opaque automation into governable, enterprise-ready identities. [...] ServiceNow: Now Assist for IT Operations Management operates inside the ServiceNow AI Platform, harnessing the power of GenAI to elevate operational intelligence and automation across IT environments. It introduces a suite of AI-driven capabilities designed to optimize every stage of alert and incident management. \n\nSheetgo: Sheetgo is an AI-ready data execution platform that transforms spreadsheet-driven operations into governed, reliable pipelines for BigQuery and Gemini Enterprise.\n\nSkyflow: The Skyflow Runtime Data Security Agent helps teams securely deploy agents on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. It provides expert guidance and on-demand data protection with fine-grained access controls, enabling enterprises to meet compliance without risking privacy.",
      "score": 0.62852204,
      "raw_content": null
    },

Explore frontline workforce solutions.\n\nLearn More\n\nBetter work days start here.\n\nWorkday Sana is the AI experience platform that lets you harness the knowledge of your entire enterprise.\n\nLearn More\n\nThe all-in-one solution for your midsize business.\n\nDiscover how Workday GO simplifies your operations by bringing HR, finance, and payroll into one integrated platform.\n\nLearn More\n\nPUMA simplified HR operations and enabled smarter decisions with AI-powered tools. Learn how they stay a step ahead with Workday.\n\nLearn More",

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/three-tiers-of-agentic-ai---and-when-to-use-none-of-them/4510377",
      "title": "Three tiers of Agentic AI - and when to use none of them",
      "content": "That gap is not a capability problem. The infrastructure has matured. Tool calling is standard across all major models. Frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Microsoft Agent Framework abstract orchestration logic. Model Context Protocol standardizes how agents access external tools and data sources. Google's Agent-to-Agent protocol now under Linux Foundation governance with over 50 enterprise technology partners including Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, and Workday standardizes how agents coordinate with each other. The protocols are in place. The frameworks are production ready.\n\nThe gap is a selection and governance problem. [...] ### References\n\nOutSystems — State of AI Development Report - \n\nDatabricks — State of AI Agents Report - \n\nGartner — 2025 Microsoft 365 and Copilot Survey -  (Paywalled primary source — publicly reported via techpartner.news: )\n\nAnthropic — Model Context Protocol (MCP) - \n\nGoogle Cloud — Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A) . \n\nNeuBird AI — Production Operations Deployment Announcement NeuBird AI Closes $19.3M Funding Round to Scale Agentic AI Across Enterprise Production Operations\n\nReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models — Yao et al. \n\nEnterprise Integration Patterns — Gregor Hohpe & Bobby Woolf, Addison-Wesley \n\nsgangaramani's avatar\nIcon for Microsoft rank\n\n### Share this page [...] ### Share this page\n\nShare to LinkedIn\nShare to Facebook\nShare to X\nShare to Reddit\nShare to Blue Sky\nSubscribe to RSS\nShare to Email",
      "score": 0.22367407,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/release-notes",
      "title": "Google Cloud release notes",
      "content": "Collect OpenAI Audit logs\n   Collect OpenTelemetry Netflow Receiver logs\n   Collect Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications logs\n   Collect Oracle NetSuite - NetSuite Applications Suite logs\n   Collect Oracle NetSuite logs\n   Collect Vectra Alerts logs\n   Collect Vectra XDR logs\n   Collect Windows Event logs (XML format)\n   Collect WinSCP logs\n   Collect Workday User Activity logs\n   Collect WP Engine logs\n   Collect XAMS by Xiting logs\n   Collect Yubico OTP logs\n   Collect Zero Networks logs\n   Collect Zix Email Encryption logs\n   Collect Zscaler NSS Feeds for Alerts logs\n   Collect ZyXEL ZyWALL logs [...] Announcement\n\nThe following AlloyDB AI functions are available in Preview: [...] You can now use AI function acceleration and the new `AI Function Apply` node to run faster queries with AI functions. This feature optimizes the execution of SQL queries that use the `ai.if` and `ai.rank` functions in PostgreSQL 17. For more information, see Accelerate performance for queries with AI functions.\n   You can now use optimized AI functions to accelerate your AI queries while reducing operational costs. By training a smaller, faster proxy model on a sample of your data, AlloyDB can process most AI queries locally and only fall back to a remote LLM when necessary. For more information, see Accelerate queries using optimized functions.",
      "score": 0.16043726,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/whats-new-in-the-agentic-data-cloud",
      "title": "What's New in the Agentic Data Cloud | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "# What’s New in the Agentic Data Cloud | Google Cloud Blog\nJump to Content\n\nCloud\n\nBlog\n\nContact salesGet started for free\n\nCloud\n\nBlog\n\nSolutions & technology\n\nSecurity\n\nEcosystem\n\nIndustries\n\n   Solutions & technology\n   Ecosystem\n   Developers & Practitioners\n   Transform with Google Cloud\n\n   AI & Machine Learning\n   API Management\n   Application Development\n   Application Modernization\n   Chrome Enterprise\n   Compute\n   Containers & Kubernetes\n   Data Analytics\n   Databases\n   DevOps & SRE\n   Maps & Geospatial\n   Security\n   Infrastructure\n   Infrastructure Modernization\n   Networking\n   Productivity & Collaboration\n   SAP on Google Cloud\n   Storage & Data Transfer\n   Sustainability\n\n   Security & Identity\n   Threat Intelligence [...] Continuous enrichment: The Knowledge Catalog delivers continuous enrichment by analyzing usage logs across your organization and profiling data behind the scenes. It learns how your enterprise actually uses data, not just what it is. This extends to unstructured data. The moment a file lands in Google Cloud Storage, our Smart Storage (Preview) instantly tags and enriches images and, soon, PDF objects. The Knowledge Catalog also identifies useful collections of unstructured data and uses Gemini to automatically generate missing schemas, mapping complex relationships so your AI is no longer flying blind. Register by April 1 and save $200 with Early Bird pricing. Register Now\n\n# The enterprise AI platform for people, money, and agents.",
      "score": 0.580091,

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/ai-isnt-the-problem-your-data-might-be.html",
      "title": "AI isn’t the problem; your data might be.: PwC",
      "content": "Our enterprise data agents weren’t built in a lab and handed off for testing. It was developed alongside industry leading enterprises navigating complex data and AI transformation, shaped from day one by real governance requirements and real business pressures. The solution is designed to integrate across ERP, CRM, data lake, and multi cloud environments, allowing organizations to modernize without disrupting existing investments. That practical foundation is what sets our enterprise data agents apart.\n\n## What does this look like in practice? [...] That's where many AI strategies hit a wall. Not because the models aren't capable, but because the data isn't ready. This is the challenge PwC and Google Cloud are helping address an ecosystem of enterprise data agents, a set of more than 30 agents that organize, govern, and activate enterprise data for AI at scale.\n\n## The data problem could be bigger than you think\n\nPicture this: Your team spends months manually combing through databases, cross-referencing records, and tracing the source of a single transaction—often alongside their day-to-day responsibilities. Eventually, someone declares the data “clean enough.” [...] Companies aren't lacking data. They're lacking trusted data, and the ability to certify it at scale. In fact, PwC’s 2025 Digital Trust Insights survey found that data protection and trust rank as the number one priority for business executives globally. Industry leaders recognize that without trusted data, transformation efforts stall before they scale.\n\n## Enter our enterprise data agents\n\nOur enterprise data agents are a collection of intelligent, AI-enabled agents purpose-built to handle the entire spectrum of enterprise data functions—faster, smarter, and more consistently than manual processes ever could.",
      "score": 0.081996456,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://docs.workato.com/en/mcp/registry/excel-mcp-server.html",
      "title": "MCP servers - {{ $frontmatter.connector_name }} | Workato Docs",
      "content": "| Tool | Description |\n --- |\n| get\\_workbook\\_info | Retrieves worksheet names, named ranges, Excel Table definitions, and session metadata for a workbook. |\n| get\\_workbook\\_content | Retrieves bounded cell data, formulas, and table contents from ranges or worksheets you specify. |\n| append\\_rows | Appends one or more rows of data to an existing worksheet or Excel Table. |\n| update\\_range\\_values | Updates values or formulas in a range or Excel Table you specify. |\n| batch\\_update | Applies multiple workbook updates as a single atomic change. |\n| add\\_worksheet | Adds a new worksheet (tab) to an existing workbook. |\n| rename\\_worksheet | Renames an existing worksheet (tab) in a workbook. |\n| delete\\_worksheet | Deletes an existing worksheet (tab) from a workbook. | [...] ### # append\\_rows tool\n\nThe append\\_rows tool appends one or more rows of data to an existing worksheet or Excel Table. Your LLM uses this tool to add new entries to a tracker or table, log operational records over time, or reserve empty rows before population.\n\nTry asking:\n\n`Add these three deals to my Pipeline tracker.`\n`Append today's sales data to the Revenue sheet.`\n`Log these customer records in the CRM table.`\n`Add a new row to the expense tracker with Date: 1/15/2026, Amount: $250.`\n\n### # update\\_range\\_values tool\n\nThe update\\_range\\_values tool updates values or formulas in a range or Excel Table you specify. Your LLM uses this tool to update specific values, correct or replace values in a range, write or update formulas, or populate previously appended empty rows. [...] | Permission | Description | Relevant action or trigger |\n --- \n| `Files.Read.All` | Allows the app to read the signed-in user's files. | Get cells, Get rows, List tables, List worksheets, and Search workbooks |\n| `Files.ReadWrite.All` | Allows the app to read and write to the signed-in user's files. | All actions in `Files.Read` and Add a table, Add a worksheet, Add rows in batch, Delete row, and Update row. |\n| `Sites.Read.All` | Allows the app to read documents in all SharePoint site collections on behalf of the signed-in user. | Get cells, Get rows, List tables, List worksheets, and Search workbooks |",
      "score": 0.19808003,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day.     {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/legal/responsible-ai-and-bias-mitigation.html",
      "title": "Workday & HiredScore's Focus on Compliance and Bias Mitigation | Workday US",
      "content": "## Why we are sharing these results\n\nWe are sharing this information because transparency is an important part of responsible AI. Customers and other stakeholders should be able to understand not only that Workday has governance processes in place, but also that we subject our tools to independent analysis and share meaningful information about the results.\n\nPublishing this summary is one way we demonstrate our commitment to building and deploying AI tools responsibly and to earning trust through evaluation, documentation, and openness.\n\n## Analytic Results Summary\n\nDate of Testing Completion: March 20, 2026\n\nDistribution Date: September 1, 2025 - February 28, 2026 [...] ## Why we are sharing these results\n\nWe are sharing this information because transparency is an important part of responsible AI. Customers and other stakeholders should be able to understand not only that Workday has governance processes in place, but also that we subject our tools to independent analysis and share meaningful information about the results.\n\nPublishing this summary is one way we demonstrate our commitment to building and deploying AI tools responsibly and to earning trust through evaluation, documentation, and openness.\n\n## Why we are sharing these results [...] Need help signing in?\n\nTo access your Workday account, please reach out to your HR or IT department for a link to your company's unique sign in page.\n\n# Responsible AI and Bias Mitigation for HiredScore Spotlight\n\nAt Workday, we understand that responsible AI requires rigorous evaluation, independent review, and transparency about how our tools perform in practice. As part of that commitment, we asked Secretariat, an independent third party, to conduct an analysis of Workday’s deployment of HiredScore Spotlight using Workday’s own applicant flow data.\n\nWe are publishing a summary of that analysis to provide greater transparency into Workday’s responsible AI practices and to support customer trust in Workday as a responsible developer and deployer of AI tools.",
      "score": 0.75268626,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/topics/ai/ai-in-banking.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in Banking | Workday",
      "content": "Strengthening trust and compliance: AI aids in risk mitigation and governance, helping banks strengthen compliance by reducing the risk of bias in hiring, improving audit readiness, and enhancing policy adherence.\n\n## The evolution of AI in banking.\n\nFrom the rigid if-then logic of 1980s expert systems to detect simple fraud patterns, banking technology has evolved to the adaptive machine learning (ML) algorithms of the 2000s. These systems could continuously learn from transactional data and automatically identify complex patterns without explicit programming. [...] Elevating human potential and productivity: AI helps amplify talent potential and boost productivity by automating repetitive tasks, augmenting human capabilities, and freeing professionals to focus on strategic, higher-value work. This shift is crucial for improving employee experience and increasing efficiency.\n Driving strategic growth: AI allows banks to improve strategic decisions by enhancing forecast accuracy and optimizing resource allocation through deeper, data-driven insights.\n Strengthening trust and compliance: AI aids in risk mitigation and governance, helping banks strengthen compliance by reducing the risk of bias in hiring, improving audit readiness, and enhancing policy adherence.\n\n## What is AI in banking? [...] ## 3. Leverage platform-based AI.\n\nRather than building models from scratch, leading institutions partner with cloud providers to leverage embedded AI. Solutions such as Workday democratize AI benefits, offering pre-built capabilities and AI agents that deliver rapid results without the heavy IT burden of custom model development.\n\n## 4. Regulatory prep.\n\nWith platform-based AI, the focus shifts from training models to establishing governance. You can use your technology partner's responsible AI framework to ensure compliance, allowing your teams to confidently monitor transactions and mitigate risks within a secure environment.\n\n## 4. Regulatory prep.",
      "score": 0.6150191,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://marketplace-qa-testingenv.workday.com/en-US/apps/403403/betterup-integration-with-workday-hcm/overview",
      "title": "BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM | Workdaysandbox",
      "content": "Title: BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM | Workdaysandbox\nBetterUp Integration with Workday HCM | Workdaysandbox. To access your Workday account, please reach out to your HR or IT department for a link to your company’s unique sign in page.More Information  WORKDAY.COMSign In  PRODUCT & COMMUNITY LOGINSWorkday Adaptive PlanningWorkday PeakonWorkday ExtendWorkday Community. BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM. BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM. Seamless data transition from Workday into BetterUp’s People Analytics Dashboard. BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM. Seamless data transition from Workday into BetterUp’s People Analytics Dashboard. Image 4: BetterUp Integration with Workday HCM. The integration delivers deeper workforce insight by connecting employee attribute data (Department, Location, Tenure) to BetterUp’s behavioral data (Productivity, Goal Achievement, Well-being) and gives leaders more insight than ever into how they can best support their workforce with coaching. ### Workday Packaged Solution. The Workday Packaged Solution badge identifies solutions built by Workday to offer accelerated value and address complex challenges. Leveraging all facets of the Workday platform, these solutions complement the product roadmap and benefit customers across various industries and regions. They range from analytic solutions, Extend applications, and integration solutions developed by Workday to strategically connect with third-party systems including vendor-agnostic Integration HeadStart Solutions for deployment acceleration and ISV Co-Innovation Solutions, created in collaboration with strategic ISV partners. ### Built on Workday. The Built on Workday badge is awarded to partner-built apps that have been reviewed and approved by Workday subject matter experts. This evaluation ensures their compliance with Workday standards and best practices, their alignment with our product roadmap to ensure they are deployment-ready, as well as the partners ability to support and maintain the app. Our Responsible AI badge helps to set expectations for and encourage partners to build responsible and future-proof AI solutions that customers can more easily trust and subsequently deploy and adopt. Solutions that go through this badging process have demonstrated they adhere and align to Workday’s Responsible AI governance framework including obligations focused on ethical AI. *   Analytics and Reporting%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Analytics%20and%20Reporting%22)). *   Financial Management%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Financial%20Management%22)). *   Human Capital Management%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Human%20Capital%20Management%22)). *   Payroll%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Payroll%22)). *   Platform and Product Extensions%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Platform%20and%20Product%20Extensions%22)). *   Spend Management%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Spend%20Management%22)). *   Talent Management%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Talent%20Management%22)). *   Workday Adaptive Planning%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Workday%20Adaptive%20Planning%22)). *   Workday Peakon Employee Voice%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Workday%20Peakon%20Employee%20Voice%22)). *   Workforce Management%40appdirect_level2_pc%3D(%22Workforce%20Management%22)).",

    {
      "url": "https://jobsus.deloitte.com/denver-co/workday-hcm-manager/3A6987BF4DBA4799B93F42081C7F2638/job",
      "title": "Workday HCM Manager | Deloitte Jobs",
      "content": "Title: Workday HCM Manager | Deloitte Jobs\n## Workday HCM Manager Denver, CO Posted yesterday. Our Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work. As a Workday HCM Manager on our Human Capital team, you will:. * Lead the Workday Human Capital Management (HCM) functional areas end-to-end, owning solution design, configuration, and successful delivery. * Advise stakeholders on Workday HCM capabilities by demonstrating features, translating needs into functional options, and recommending best-fit approaches. * Elicit and document requirements and maintain clear artifacts (e.g., process designs, configuration workbooks, and design decisions) to support governance and traceability. * Configure Workday HCM in alignment with approved designs, security/data considerations, and client operating requirements. * Deliver knowledge transfer to the client team, including training, documentation, and transition-to-support activities to enable long-term sustainability. * Provide functional leadership for support team members as needed, including task management, quality review, and issue escalation. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise. Our HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. * 7+ years of professional experience delivering Workday HCM solutions. * 3+ years of experience driving implementations & configurations of Workday HCM modules. The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case.",
      "score": 0.48074743,
      "raw_content": null
    },

{
  "query": "Responsible AI HR technology Workday",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147287BR",
      "title": "Principal Consultant-Workday Functional-Denmark - Copenhagen Denmark - Careers | Infosys",
      "content": "Role – Principal consultant  \nTechnology – Workday Functional HCM  \nLocation – Denmark  \nCompensation – Competitive (including bonus)  \n  \nJob Description:  \nWorkday Functional Lead is responsible for owning the functional design, configuration, and successful delivery of Workday solutions aligned to business requirements. This role acts as the primary functional authority for assigned Workday modules, ensuring best‑practice configuration, data integrity, business process alignment, and user adoption.  \nThe Functional Lead partners closely with HR, IT, Program/Project Managers, integration teams, and external system integrators to translate business needs into scalable Workday solutions across implementations, enhancements, and ongoing optimizations.  \n  \n  \nYour role: [...] With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem. [...] Serve as the functional subject-matter expert (SME) for Workday modules – CoreHCM, recruitment, compensation, Benefits, Absence/ Time tracking\n Lead end‑to‑end functional delivery from requirements through post‑go‑live support\n Guide business stakeholders toward standard, scalable Workday solutions\n Ensure configuration aligns with enterprise process, security, and compliance standards\n Mentor functional analysts and ensure delivery quality across workstreams\n\n  \nResponsibilities:",
      "score": 0.99942076,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial Close, Built with Workday and Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise",
      "content": "The Financial Close Companion is the direct result of a powerful ecosystem, combining KPMG's deep industry knowledge in finance and responsible, trusted AI, Workday's leading AI platform for HR and finance, and Google Cloud's cutting-edge AI capabilities. Purpose‑built for finance teams, the Financial Close Companion addresses a persistent industry pain point—the lengthy, time‑consuming manual close—by delivering intelligent automation, structured workflows, and human‑like analysis of financial reports such as trial balances. In many organizations, closing the books involves numerous manual steps, high levels of coordination, and long hours. The Financial Close Companion tackles these challenges by: [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement. [...] \"The future of finance depends on how well you can connect your people and your numbers,” said Kathleen Curry, global vice president, global channels at Workday, “By building directly on Workday’s platform, KPMG has created a tool that brings HR and financial data together to finally solve the headache of the manual month-end close. This partnership isn't just about new technology; it’s about giving finance teams their time back so they can stop chasing data and start advising the business.\"",
      "score": 0.9988121,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/partner-built-agents-available-in-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Partner-built agents available in Gemini Enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Sana from Workday: Sana Self-Service Agent instantly finds and summarizes information from Workday and other knowledge sources, providing personalized answers and assistance with tasks. With over 300 skills across areas like pay, time, and absence, the agent handles everyday HR and finance tasks globally, reducing support tickets and allowing teams to focus on high-value work.\n\nSaviynt: Identity Security for AI enables continuous discovery and inventory of agents, identifying their functions, access, and risks. It treats agents as first-class identities with defined purposes and accountable owners. This blend of posture visibility and enforced accountability converts opaque automation into governable, enterprise-ready identities. [...] What's new: Today, we’re bringing partner-built agents from our Agent Marketplace directly into the Agent Gallery inside the Gemini Enterprise app. This creates a powerful, centrally governed hub for discovering and managing specialized AI from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and more.\n\nWhy this matters for your business: With millions of paid seats across thousands of companies, the Gemini Enterprise app allows organizations to automate complex, end-to-end workflows in a single, unified environment. Five key benefits include: [...] Ambiguous AI: Ambiguous Recruiting Coworker manages candidate communication, multi-round scheduling, and pipeline tracking from application to offer. It works natively across Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Sheets, Google Drive, and more, while looping you in for key decisions.\n\nAmdocs: The Telco Customer Experience Agent autonomously resolves complex customer service issues across digital and contact center channels. Combining Amdocs Cognitive Core with Gemini Enterprise for CX, it reasons, orchestrates, and executes end‑to‑end telco processes, improving resolution speed, cost efficiency, and customer satisfaction.",
      "score": 0.99805814,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147288BR",
      "title": "Program Manager-Workday HCM-Denmark - Copenhagen Denmark - Careers | Infosys",
      "content": "Own the lifecycle of Workday HCM programs from initiation through stabilization\n Define program scope, roadmap, success metrics, and benefits realization\n Establish governance models, steering committees, and escalation paths\n Ensure alignment with HR strategy, enterprise architecture, security, and data standards\n\n Oversee design, configuration, testing, data migration, cutover, and deployment\n Manage dependencies across integrations, reporting, security, and HR operations\n Ensure adherence to Workday best practices while balancing business needs\n\n Serve as the primary interface between HR leadership, IT, Workday, and system integrators\n Manage implementation partners, AMS providers, and vendor performance\n Communicate status, risks, and outcomes to executives and HR leadership forums [...] Requisition ID\n\n147288BR\n\nRole – Program Manager  \nTechnology – Workday HCM  \nLocation – Denmark  \nCompensation – Competitive (including bonus)  \n  \nJob Description:  \n  \nThe Workday HCM Program Manager leads enterprise-wide Workday Human Capital Management programs, owning strategy, execution, governance, and value realization across HR technology initiatives. This role is accountable for delivering scalable, complaint, and user-centric Workday HCM solutions that support the full employee lifecycle.  \nThe Program Manager serves as the single point of accountability for multi-workstream HCM programs, coordinating HR, IT, Payroll, Security, Legal, and external Workday partners to ensure successful delivery and adoption.  \n  \nYour role:  \nAs a Workday HCM Program Manager, you will: [...] With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/news/press-releases/2026/04/22/salesforce-google-cloud-launch-new-integrations-deep-context/",
      "title": "Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations",
      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day. A security escalation surfaces in Slack, context lives in a Google Doc, approvals sit in Salesforce, and stakeholder alignment happens over email. Instead of patching together point solutions, companies can now build an interconnected system of engagement that enables teams to move faster across the business and turn ideas into agentic action, all from the same conversation.\n\nKey Integration Features: [...] “Businesses are ready to go all in on agentic AI, and that requires infrastructure and models that can operate across the entire enterprise. Our deepened partnership with Google Cloud gives joint customers exactly that, so they can deploy Agentforce across every part of their business and accelerate their Agentic Enterprise transformation,” said Srini Tallapragada, President and Chief Engineering Officer at Salesforce.\n\n## Working Where Teams Already Are: Slack, Google Workspace, and Gemini Enterprise [...] Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations\n\nLAS VEGAS, Cloud Next ’26 — April 22, 2026 — Google Cloud and Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) today announced an expanded partnership that will enable AI agents to execute end-to-end workflows across both platforms by solving the long-standing challenge of fragmented data and disconnected systems. With new integrations across Salesforce and Google Cloud, customers can deploy AI agents in tools like Slack and Google Workspace, while Agentforce and Gemini Enterprise provide the intelligence and context behind the scenes. This allows agents to work across systems, reduces risky data movement, and eliminates time lost to context switching.",
      "score": 0.3504214,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/ai-isnt-the-problem-your-data-might-be.html",
      "title": "AI isn't the problem; your data might be. - PwC",
      "content": "Think of it less like a traditional data platform and more like a highly capable team of AI agents working around the clock. We provide agents dedicated to data quality and data lineage—so you always know where your data came from and how it reached you. Other agents handle governance, certification, and ontology, while tracking semantic or schema changes and their impact on code. There are even agents that allow your teams to literally talk to their data in plain language—no SQL required. Governance, policy enforcement, and auditability are embedded directly into execution workflows, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and responsible AI standards without slowing innovation. [...] Our enterprise data agents weren’t built in a lab and handed off for testing. It was developed alongside industry leading enterprises navigating complex data and AI transformation, shaped from day one by real governance requirements and real business pressures. The solution is designed to integrate across ERP, CRM, data lake, and multi cloud environments, allowing organizations to modernize without disrupting existing investments. That practical foundation is what sets our enterprise data agents apart.\n\n## What does this look like in practice? [...] Companies aren't lacking data. They're lacking trusted data, and the ability to certify it at scale. In fact, PwC’s 2025 Digital Trust Insights survey found that data protection and trust rank as the number one priority for business executives globally. Industry leaders recognize that without trusted data, transformation efforts stall before they scale.\n\n## Enter our enterprise data agents\n\nOur enterprise data agents are a collection of intelligent, AI-enabled agents purpose-built to handle the entire spectrum of enterprise data functions—faster, smarter, and more consistently than manual processes ever could.",
      "score": 0.10302443,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agentic-commerce-discoverability.html",
      "title": "Agentic commerce discoverability: What if AI agents can't find you?",
      "content": "{{item.text}}\n\n## Contact us\n\nEric Shea\n\nEric Shea\n\nCommerce Lead, PwC US\n\nLinkedin Follow\nJason Ruge\n\nJason Ruge\n\nPrincipal, US Google Cloud Alliance Leader, PwC US\n\nLinkedin Follow\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC. The next question is practical: If AI agents increasingly influence what customers see, compare, and buy, how should retailers design their commerce systems to increase exposure?\n\nFrom PwC’s perspective, two capabilities now sit at the center of agentic commerce:\n\n## Rewriting discoverability",

      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-managed-security-service-google.html",
      "title": "Managed security service on Google Security Operations: PwC",
      "content": "This launch represents the next step in PwC and Google Cloud’s broader alliance, focused on helping clients build secure, AI-enabled enterprises and respond to evolving cyber threats with confidence.\n\n## About PwC\n\nAt PwC, we help clients build trust and reinvent so they can turn complexity into competitive advantage. We’re a tech-forward, people-empowered network with more than 364,000 people in 137 countries. Across audit and assurance, tax and legal, deals and consulting, we help clients build, accelerate, and sustain momentum. Find out more at www.pwc.com.\n\n© 2026 PwC. All rights reserved.\n\n## Contact us\n\nSydney Perkins\n\nAdvisory – Alliances and Partnerships, Cloud, Engineering, Data and AI (CEDA), PwC US [...] The new managed security service from PwC can deliver AI-enabled threat detection and response with simplified operations and predictable pricing.\n\nPwC today announced the launch of its unified detection and response managed security service, enabled by Google Security Operations, designed to help organizations strengthen cybersecurity, reduce operational complexity, and scale protection in an increasingly dynamic threat landscape. [...] The announcement builds on PwC’s recently announced collaboration agreement with Google Cloud to help organizations transform their security practices with AI-driven capabilities. PwC is also the winner of the 2026 Google Cloud Partner of the Year Award in the Global Security category, underscoring its continued investment in delivering leading cyber outcomes for clients.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/",
      "title": "Workday: The Enterprise AI Platform for Managing HR and Finance",
      "content": "Seamlessly manage all your HR and finance needs on an open, unified platform with AI at the core. It’s a new work day.\n\nExplore Workday AI\n\nBuild an agile frontline workforce.\n\nRedefine the hiring experience and empower both managers and employees. Explore frontline workforce solutions.\n\nLearn More\n\nBetter work days start here.\n\nWorkday Sana is the AI experience platform that lets you harness the knowledge of your entire enterprise.\n\nLearn More\n\nThe all-in-one solution for your midsize business.\n\nDiscover how Workday GO simplifies your operations by bringing HR, finance, and payroll into one integrated platform.\n\nLearn More\n\nPUMA simplified HR operations and enabled smarter decisions with AI-powered tools. Learn how they stay a step ahead with Workday.\n\nLearn More [...] ### Operations\n\nDrive faster, smarter frontline operations and enable a connected, agile workforce with AI agents and mobile tools.\n\nFrontline Operations   Workforce Management   Global Compliance   Mobile\n\nA Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud HCM Suites for 1,000+ Employee Enterprises. Read Report\n\nAI that truly understands work.\n\nWorkday AI is the engine that powers our products and AI agents. Learn how it actively supports your people and enables them to do their best work.\n\nExplore Workday AI\n\n## 11,000+ organizations worldwide trust Workday.\n\nFrom forward-thinking midsize businesses to Fortune 500 leaders.\n\nAll Customer Stories\n\nReady to talk?  \n Get in touch.\n\nContact Sales\n\n##### Please accept cookies to continue.     {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/topics/finance/what-is-financial-consolidation.html",
      "title": "What is financial consolidation? - Workday",
      "content": "AI transforms financial consolidation from a backward-looking process into predictive intelligence. Machine learning algorithms detect anomalies in real time, flagging unusual transactions or data patterns before they are consolidated in reports. Finance teams address issues promptly, rather than discovering errors weeks later during audits.\n\nPredictive analytics enhances business outcomes by forecasting consolidated results before the close is completed. Executives review mid-period performance projections and adjust strategies accordingly. AI models analyze historical consolidation data to predict which entities are likely to miss deadlines or submit questionable figures. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls.",
      "score": 0.8266142,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "Leaders must be able to answer critical questions: which agents are delivering the most value? Where should we invest more? What skills are needed for agents to deliver even more value? When is an agent no longer performing as expected? How do we continuously improve our human-machine teams?\n\nWith the Workday Agent System of Record, organizations can manage AI agents with the same confidence, accountability, and visibility they expect from Workday—while building a smarter, more adaptive organization powered by humans and machines working together.\n\nA remarkable 82% of organizations are already using AI agents. But is your team ready? Read our latest report to learn how businesses are maximizing human potential with AI, featuring insights from nearly 3,000 global leaders. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents.",

      "title": "What is employee experience? Improve your EX strategy. - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting [...] Not only does this shift necessitate user-friendly technology, it also increasingly demands solutions with AI baked in. With workforces becoming increasingly tech savvy, companies have to match the current pace of technological development.\n\n### 4. Enabling skills development and talent performance.\n\nThere’s no glossing over employee needs when it comes to skills development and talent performance. Ensuring your employees are supported in their development is critical to employee retention. [...] In the past, working with clunky digital interfaces was commonplace. That means companies have to employ intuitive user interfaces, strong integrations between applications, and smooth user experiences.\n\nNot only does this shift necessitate user-friendly technology, it also increasingly demands solutions with AI baked in.       "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation\n\n  # While the EU AI Act Stalls, Workday Leads on Trusted, Compliant AI\n\n  Workday champions a strong, workable EU AI Act while already operating ahead of the curve with independently verified governance that defines the standard for trusted AI.\n\n   \n\n  Rich Sauer\n\n  LATEST\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community   Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners [...] Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection",
