      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/prompt-when-do-you-work-best/",
      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog | Microsoft",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times. Maybe your workday feels like a mysterious blend of brilliance and busyness? Now you can use AI to help decode how you’re spending your time.       "url": "https://www.databricks.com/blog/how-conversational-analytics-removes-bi-bottleneck",
      "title": "How conversational analytics removes the BI bottleneck | Databricks Blog",
      "content": "Ari: A lot, to be honest. The fundamental thing they are missing is that Genie is built on their data, with their terminology, and governed by their business rules. A generic chatbot has been trained on the internet — Taylor Swift lyrics, historical trivia, whatever. Genie knows what your company means when it uses the terms customer and it knows your fiscal year. It knows your product catalog. That granular specificity is important. And Genie goes way beyond question and answer. Genie does open-ended research. It makes predictions. It connects to your adjacent systems like Salesforce, Workday, SAP, Slack, so you can ask questions that span your entire business. I have seen executives use it to ask genuinely open questions like, \"What should I be focusing on today?\" And those executives [...] Data+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\nData+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\n# How conversational analytics removes the BI bottleneck\n\nA conversation with Databricks Head of Technical Evangelism Ari Kaplan on conversational analytics, modern databases, and why the gap between leaders and laggards is closing fast\n\nby  Catherine Brown\n\nThere is a question circulating in boardrooms and data leadership meetings right now that goes something like this: \"We already have BI. We already have a database solution. So why do we need something different?\" [...] Here is what has changed. With Lakebase, you can provision a new database instantly — not in days or weeks. And most databases created today are provisioned by AI.  \nThe second thing is cost. Traditionally, if you needed a production environment, a test environment, and a QA environment, you were making three copies of your data. Triple the storage, triple the cost. With Lakebase, you can fork off a hundred environments without making a single copy of the underlying data. I was just in Brazil, and companies were telling me they cut their total cost of ownership by 40% after the move. The highest I have heard is 98%. Not everyone gets there, but the savings are real wherever I go.",
      "score": 0.23485425,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "ServiceNow ITAM Developer - Okta",
      "content": "This is a highly collaborative and integrated role. You will work closely with our SAM Managers to translate complex asset management business processes into technical solutions that identify and capture cost-saving opportunities. You will manage a sophisticated ecosystem that connects financial data from Workday and NetSuite, procurement data from Coupa, and endpoint data from JAMF and Intune. Your expertise will bridge the gap between technical data modeling and strategic business outcomes.\n\nResponsibilities [...] Build and maintain automated workflows for the hardware lifecycle, following Common Service Data Model (CSDM) best practices to keep the Asset and CI tables in sync.\n Implement HAM Pro features like Hardware Model Normalization, Mobile Asset Tracking, and Asset Inventory Audit workflows.\n Manage Cloud Cost Management (CCM) workflows to provide visibility and optimization for AWS and GCP spend.\n Manage Software Spend Detection by integrating financial data from Workday and NetSuite to identify tail spend and shadow IT.\n Design advanced dashboards and Performance Analytics widgets to report on compliance, cloud savings, and hardware refresh cycles for executive leadership. [...] Lead technical delivery for ServiceNow SAM and HAM, focusing on the Content Library, Normalization engines, and Reconciliation processes.\n Partner with business stakeholders to analyze asset management business processes and implement automated workflows that drive license reclamation and cost-avoidance strategies.\n Oversee and troubleshoot complex integrations with core systems, including Coupa, Okta, JAMF, and Intune, ensuring data flow is consistent and accurate.\n Build, configure, and maintain integrations with SaaS connectors (e.g., Salesforce, Workday, Adobe) and develop custom integrations for unique software vendors using IntegrationHub.",

  "query": "Workday AI POV",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/au/en/why-kpmg/alliances/workday/workday-student.html",
      "title": "Transform student management with KPMG and Workday",
      "content": "### AI driven insights and predictive capability\n\nKPMG brings deep student lifecycle knowledge to make Workday Student’s AI and predictive analytics actionable, helping universities anticipate student needs and risks early. This shifts institutions from reactive administration to more proactive interventions, smarter resource planning and stronger outcomes.\n\n### Intelligent automation and streamlined workflows\n\nRoutine administration is automated through Workday Student’s configurable workflows and KPMG’s leading practices and accelerators. The result is improved accuracy, speed and compliance, freeing staff to focus on higher value student support and academic decision-making.\n\n### Strengthened compliance, governance and change readiness [...] Rapid advances in technology are offering universities an ideal opportunity to improve their student experiences and reshape operations. Even as demographics shift, enrolment patterns evolve and legacy systems strain under modern demands, institutions have a powerful chance to reimagine how they support staff and enable students.\n\n## What is Workday Student?\n\nWorkday Student, co created with leading universities, is a modern, mobile first, end to end student administration platform that removes silos by unifying academic, financial, Human Capital Management (HCM) and operational processes into a single cloud native, AI fuelled system.\n\n### KPMG’s trusted approach [...] The digital backpack isn’t just about today; it’s about thriving in the age of disruption. AI-driven insights, predictive analytics, dynamic course management and autonomous workflows will redefine how universities engage students and staff and manage operations. With KPMG and Workday, you can lead this change with confidence.\n\n## Benefits of a digital backpack for universities\n\nWorkday supplies the platform, KPMG makes it work for you.\n\n### A single, connected student operating model",
      "score": 0.59033114,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud. [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement. [...] “As organizations face pressure for faster, more efficient financial reporting, the need for a streamlined and automated financial close is critical. By harnessing the power of Gemini Enterprise and integrating seamlessly with Workday, that the agent directly addresses these challenges and not only enhances efficiency and accuracy but also saves valuable time for accountants,” said Brian Anderson, Workday Platform Leader, KPMG LLP. “This collaboration allows finance teams to close faster with greater confidence while freeing professionals to focus on higher-impact strategic work.”",
      "score": 0.48308808,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Project Manager - Project Delivery Specialist - - 340017",
      "content": "The Team\n\nAI& Engineering leverages cutting-edge engineering capabilities to build, deploy, and operate integrated/verticalized sector solutions in software, data, AI, network, and hybrid cloud infrastructure. These solutions are powered by engineering for business advantage, transforming mission-critical operations. We enable clients to stay ahead with the latest advancements by transforming engineering teams and modernizing technology & data platforms. Our delivery models are tailored to meet each client's unique requirements.\n\nQualifications\n\nRequired [...] Strong understanding of documentation, knowledge management, and operational reporting\n Strong stakeholder management, coordination, and communication skills\n Bachelor's degree, preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer Engineering, or related IT discipline; or equivalent experience\n Limited immigration sponsorship may be available\n Ability to travel 10%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve [...] All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability or protected veteran status, or any other legally protected basis, in accordance with applicable law.\n\nRequisition code: 340017\n\nJob ID   340017\n\n## SCAM ALERT\n\n## Caution against fraudulent job offers!",

      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/southeast-asia/en/about/press-room/deloitte-launches-asia-pacific-health-institute-with-new-report-on-technology-enabled-healthcare-access.html",
      "title": "Deloitte launches Asia Pacific Health Institute with new report on technology-enabled healthcare access | Deloitte Southeast Asia",
      "content": "4. Prepare the workforce and reskill staff:  \nPreparing the healthcare workforce and reskilling staff is crucial to successfully integrate technology into clinical workflows. By selecting tools that reduce administrative burdens, organisations preserve valuable clinician time—enabling more meaningful patient contact and improving overall care quality.\n\n5. Establish governance frameworks for clinical AI:  \nRobust governance and regulation are essential for safe and effective AI implementation, mitigating risks and maintaining public trust. Policymakers can define clear regulatory pathways, clinical validation standards, bias monitoring and audit mechanisms to ensure AI tools used in healthcare are safe, effective, ethical and equitable while maintaining transparency and public trust. {
  "query": "Workday AI",
  "follow_up_questions": null,

  "images": [],
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/au/en/why-kpmg/alliances/workday/workday-student.html",
      "title": "Transform student management with KPMG and Workday",
      "content": "### AI driven insights and predictive capability\n\nKPMG brings deep student lifecycle knowledge to make Workday Student’s AI and predictive analytics actionable, helping universities anticipate student needs and risks early. This shifts institutions from reactive administration to more proactive interventions, smarter resource planning and stronger outcomes.\n\n### Intelligent automation and streamlined workflows\n\nRoutine administration is automated through Workday Student’s configurable workflows and KPMG’s leading practices and accelerators. The result is improved accuracy, speed and compliance, freeing staff to focus on higher value student support and academic decision-making.\n\n### Strengthened compliance, governance and change readiness [...] Rapid advances in technology are offering universities an ideal opportunity to improve their student experiences and reshape operations. Even as demographics shift, enrolment patterns evolve and legacy systems strain under modern demands, institutions have a powerful chance to reimagine how they support staff and enable students.\n\n## What is Workday Student?\n\nWorkday Student, co created with leading universities, is a modern, mobile first, end to end student administration platform that removes silos by unifying academic, financial, Human Capital Management (HCM) and operational processes into a single cloud native, AI fuelled system.\n\n### KPMG’s trusted approach [...] Workday services partner logo services partner\nWorkday services partner logo advisory partner\nWorkday services partner logo AMS partner\nWorkday extend partner logo\n\n## How KPMG + Workday Student can help transform the student experience confidently\n\nKPMG and Workday help universities bring HCM, finance and student operations together into one cloud native, AI ready ecosystem. By combining KPMG’s pre-built models, accelerators and sector experience with Workday Student’s intelligent platform, institutions can transform their student experience at the front and back ends while accessing new efficiencies and reduced risk and costs.",
      "score": 0.7827459,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "“As organizations face pressure for faster, more efficient financial reporting, the need for a streamlined and automated financial close is critical. By harnessing the power of Gemini Enterprise and integrating seamlessly with Workday, that the agent directly addresses these challenges and not only enhances efficiency and accuracy but also saves valuable time for accountants,” said Brian Anderson, Workday Platform Leader, KPMG LLP. “This collaboration allows finance teams to close faster with greater confidence while freeing professionals to focus on higher-impact strategic work.” [...] The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud. [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement.",
      "score": 0.7180613,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "http://mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/superagency-in-the-workplace-empowering-people-to-unlock-ais-full-potential-at-work",
      "title": "AI in the workplace: A report for 2025 - McKinsey",
      "content": "Missing: Workday | Show results with:Workday",

      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000122345597/staff-ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment/",
      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment, New York | ServiceNow Careers",
      "content": "Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations [...] ### Additional Information\n\nWork Personas\n\nWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third-party service.\n\nEqual Opportunity Employer [...] ### Job Description\n\nAbout the Role\n\nYou'll own the end-to-end technical relationship with enterprise customers, translating complex business challenges into high-impact AI solutions built on the Moveworks Platform. This is a customer-facing, field-based role — you'll be the primary technical point of contact throughout the full implementation lifecycle, from solution design through launch. You are equally comfortable whiteboarding with a customer's IT team in the morning and collaborating with internal engineering and product teams in the afternoon.\n\nThis is not a back-office engineering role — you will be in front of customers regularly and are expected to travel up to 25% of the time.\n\nWhat You'll Own\n\nAbout You",
      "score": 0.04539074,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/our-insights/transformation/winning-combination.html",
      "title": "Winning Combination",
      "content": "## Going beyond technology\n\nThat’s why leading solutions for managed detection and response (MDR) are powered by AI and automations, leveraging 24/7 human expertise.\n\nIn this subscription-based model for continuous monitoring, leading managed services providers combine AI, machine learning, and data analytics with security analysts, proactive threat hunters, engineers and incident responders. Working together, they quickly identify, contain and respond to cyber threats.\n\nThese professionals bring critical discernment to matters such as: [...] #### \n\n###### \n\nKPMG Logo\n\n#### \n\n###### \n\n#### \n\n###### \n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n# Winning combination\n\nCyber managed services must blend AI with human expertise\n\nShare\n\nAI can process vast quantities of data with speed and precision, alerting you to a cybersecurity threat in seconds.\n\nIt can also detect unusual patterns that might otherwise slip through the cracks, generate insights on evolving threats like deep fakes, and expedite containment. It can even move from reactive to predictive, identifying and neutralizing threats before they materialize.\n\nBut that’s not enough. While AI excels at repetitive, data-intensive tasks, human oversight is still needed for the nuances of cybersecurity.\n\n## Going beyond technology [...] Indeed, some security operations centers have unified their many monitoring tools into a single pane of glass for centralized visibility, but this dashboard can still become very noisy, generating a million alerts a day. In managed services, skilled cyber analysts work with sophisticated AI to filter and prioritize threat notifications.\n\nForce multiplier\n\nAI-enabled managed services can greatly extend the capacity of a security operations center. It’s no wonder that cybersecurity is one of the top areas of managed services adoption:\n\nSource: KPMG and HFS Research Managed Services Outlook Survey\n\n## Deep domain knowledge",

{
  "query": "Workday AI",
  "follow_up_questions": null,
  "answer": null,
  "images": [],
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://go.theregister.com/feed/www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_enterprise/",
      "title": "Google says it has all the answers for AI agent sprawl • The Register",
      "content": "It comes as the challenge facing businesses has shifted from building individual AI agents to managing hundreds or thousands of them at once - something Workday and others are trying to tackle too.\n\n\"The early versions of AI models were really focused on answering questions that people had and assisting them with creative tasks. Now we’re seeing as the models evolve people wanting to delegate tasks and sequences of tasks to agents,\" Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told reporters during a press briefing. \"And these agents then being able to turn around and use a computer, use all of GCP and Workspace as a tool.\"\n\nTo meet the moment, Google rolled out infrastructure in the form of its eighth generation of TPU chips and security updates through its purchase of Wiz.\n\nboxing [...] Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is organized around four pillars: build, scale, govern, and optimize. On the build side, Google introduced Agent Studio, a low-code interface for creating agents using natural language, alongside an upgraded Agent Development Kit with a new graph-based framework for orchestrating multiple agents working together, the company said during a media prebriefing.\n\nIt also provides an agent registry that gives organizations a central catalog of each internal agent and tool, the company said. Also inside the new platform is an agent marketplace that offers pre-built agents from partners including Atlassian, Oracle, ServiceNow, and Workday. [...] More like these\n\n×\n\n### More about\n\n Google AI\n Google Cloud Next\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n Google Brain\n\n### Broader topics\n\n AI\n Google Cloud\n Google Cloud Platform\n\n#### More about\n\n3 comment bubble on white COMMENTS\n\n#### More about\n\n Google AI\n Google Cloud Next\n\nMore like these\n\n×\n\n### More about\n\n Google AI\n Google Cloud Next\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n Google Brain\n\n### Broader topics\n\n AI\n Google Cloud\n Google Cloud Platform\n\n#### TIP US OFF\n\nSend us news\n\n### Other stories you might like\n\n#### Musk bets Tesla's AI future on Intel node that isn't finished yet\n\nEV maker leaning on still-in-development 14A process for Terafab, says it needs to build own silicon\n\nSystems23 Apr 2026 |\n\n#### AI bats away ping-pong challenge as rise of the machines continues",
      "score": 0.680136,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147287BR",
      "title": "Principal Consultant-Workday Functional-Denmark - Infosys",
      "content": "With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem. [...] Responsibilities:  \n  \n\n Own functional design for assigned Workday modules\n Translate business requirements into Workday configuration using best practices\n Lead requirement workshops, design sessions, and solution walkthroughs\n Ensure solutions balance business needs with Workday standard functionality\n Review and validate configuration completed by functional analysts or partners\n Ensure configuration is scalable, auditable, and future-ready\n\n   \nRequired [...] Role – Principal consultant  \nTechnology – Workday Functional HCM  \nLocation – Denmark  \nCompensation – Competitive (including bonus)  \n  \nJob Description:  \nWorkday Functional Lead is responsible for owning the functional design, configuration, and successful delivery of Workday solutions aligned to business requirements. This role acts as the primary functional authority for assigned Workday modules, ensuring best‑practice configuration, data integrity, business process alignment, and user adoption.  \nThe Functional Lead partners closely with HR, IT, Program/Project Managers, integration teams, and external system integrators to translate business needs into scalable Workday solutions across implementations, enhancements, and ongoing optimizations.  \n  \n  \nYour role:",

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran",
      "content": "Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n Headless 360: Salesforce's latest pitch to let AI do the dev work\n How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk\n Minnesota State payroll problems grew after Workday launch, auditors say\n\nSpeaking to The Register in January, Fivetran CEO George Fraser said vendor pricing is publicly documented and can sometimes be negotiated down.\n\n\"This is about a lot of different data sources. When data sources make it difficult to get access to your own data, it just slows everything down,\" he said.\n\nWith some vendors, a number of projects never get off the ground because it is so hard to get the data, and the developers simply gave up, he said. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n SalesForce\n Workday [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### SaaS\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n# Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n## Report also slams multiple vendors for poor data integration and egress fees\n\nicon  Lindsay Clark\n\nWed 22 Apr 2026  //  16:40 UTC\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\n\njigsaw_puzzle_648\n\n## As AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\n\nREAD MORE [...] It's an issue organizations should watch closely as they build AI agent systems, which depend on company data to generate meaningful responses. Most major vendors offer AI agents within their own platforms but getting those agents to work across different applications is another matter.\n\nFiveTran's newly launched benchmark indicates a number of companies ranking poorly on its categories of data coverage, performance, and egress charges.\n\nAmong the worst on egress fees is Xero, an accounting SaaS for SMBs. FiveTran claims there is a \"tiered monthly fees plus explicit API egress allotments and overage pricing\" to get to the data.",
      "score": 0.4179133,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://forums.theregister.com/forum/all/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran • The Register Forums",
      "content": "My Account\n\nThe Register Home Page\n\n## Topics\n\n### Security\n\n### Off-Prem\n\n### On-Prem\n\n### Software\n\n### Offbeat\n\n## More from The Register\n\n### Special Features\n\n### Vendor Voice\n\n### Resources\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n# back to article Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\nback to article\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\njigsaw\\_puzzle\\_648\nAs AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\nREAD MORE …\n\n## COMMENTS\n\nunstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit [...] ### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon\n\nanonymous\nThumb Up\nThumb Down\nStop\nGo\nWTF?\nFAIL\nHappy\nMeh\nUnhappy\nAngel\nDevil\nFlame\nMushroom\nMegaphone\nTrollface\nFacepalm\nHolmes\nHeadmaster\nCoat\nJoke\nPint\nCoffee/keyboard\nAlert\nBlack Helicopters\nBoffin\nPirate\nAlien\nTerminator\nBig Brother\nChildcatcher\nLinux\nGimp\nWindows\nIT Angle\n\nBiting the hand that feeds IT\n\n## About Us\n\n## Our Websites\n\n## Your Privacy\n\nCopyright.\nAll rights reserved\n© 1998–2026 [...] unstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit\n\nRaaS lacks pagination, causing large report failures\n\nAs a programmer writing a program which will read a different program's output, I do not want pagination in that other program's output, because it is more junk I have to program around to filter out.\n\nI want an uninterrupted flow of pure data.\n\nClaiming that a lack of pagination causes report failures smells like bullshit.\n\n## POST COMMENT House rules\n\nNot a member of The Register?\nCreate a new account here.\n\nRemember me on this computer?\n \nPost anonymously?\n\n### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon",

    {
      "url": "https://www.databricks.com/blog/how-conversational-analytics-removes-bi-bottleneck",
      "title": "How conversational analytics removes the BI bottleneck - Databricks",
      "content": "Ari: A lot, to be honest. The fundamental thing they are missing is that Genie is built on their data, with their terminology, and governed by their business rules. A generic chatbot has been trained on the internet — Taylor Swift lyrics, historical trivia, whatever. Genie knows what your company means when it uses the terms customer and it knows your fiscal year. It knows your product catalog. That granular specificity is important. And Genie goes way beyond question and answer. Genie does open-ended research. It makes predictions. It connects to your adjacent systems like Salesforce, Workday, SAP, Slack, so you can ask questions that span your entire business. I have seen executives use it to ask genuinely open questions like, \"What should I be focusing on today?\" And those executives [...] Data+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\nData+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\n# How conversational analytics removes the BI bottleneck\n\nA conversation with Databricks Head of Technical Evangelism Ari Kaplan on conversational analytics, modern databases, and why the gap between leaders and laggards is closing fast\n\nby  Catherine Brown\n\nThere is a question circulating in boardrooms and data leadership meetings right now that goes something like this: \"We already have BI. We already have a database solution. So why do we need something different?\" [...] Here is what has changed. With Lakebase, you can provision a new database instantly — not in days or weeks. And most databases created today are provisioned by AI.  \nThe second thing is cost. Traditionally, if you needed a production environment, a test environment, and a QA environment, you were making three copies of your data. Triple the storage, triple the cost. With Lakebase, you can fork off a hundred environments without making a single copy of the underlying data. I was just in Brazil, and companies were telling me they cut their total cost of ownership by 40% after the move. The highest I have heard is 98%. Not everyone gets there, but the savings are real wherever I go.",
      "score": 0.23204939,
      "raw_content": null
    },

Apr 2026 | 1\n\n#### Forget one chip to rule them all: With TPU 8, Google has an AI arms race to win\n\nx86 gets the boot as Google pairs up its TPUs with some Arm-based Axion cores\n\nAI + ML22 Apr 2026 | 1\n\n#### France's 'Secure' ID agency probes breach as crooks claim 19M records\n\nGov admits 'incident' as forum sellers boast of fresh haul covering up to a third of the population\n\nCyber-crime22 Apr 2026 | 20",

    {
      "url": "https://venturebeat.com/data/the-modern-data-stack-was-built-for-humans-asking-questions-google-just-rebuilt-its-for-agents-taking-action",
      "title": "The modern data stack was built for humans asking questions ...",
      "content": "The practical implication for data engineering teams is that the Knowledge Catalog scales to the full data estate, not just the curated subset that a small team of data stewards can maintain by hand. The catalog covers BigQuery, Spanner, AlloyDB and Cloud SQL natively, and federates with third-party catalogs including Collibra, Atlan and Datahub. Zero-copy federation extends semantic context from SaaS applications including SAP, Salesforce Data360, ServiceNow and Workday without requiring data movement.\n\n## Google's lakehouse goes cross cloud\n\nGoogle has had a data lakehouse called BigLake since 2022. Initially it was limited to just Google data, but in recent years has had some limited federation capabilities enabling enterprises to query data found in other locations. [...] Now, with AI agents increasingly expected to take actions directly on behalf of the business, Gutmans argued that data platforms must evolve into systems of action.\n\"We need to make sure that all of enterprise data can be activated with AI, that includes both structured and unstructured data,\" Gutmans said. \"We need to make sure that there's the right level of trust, which also means it's not just about getting access to the data, but really understanding the data.\" [...] Cross-cloud egress costs are a hidden tax on agentic AI. Storage-based federation via open Iceberg standards is emerging as the architectural answer across Google, Databricks and Snowflake. Enterprises locked into proprietary federation approaches should be stress-testing those costs at agent-scale query volumes.\n\nGutmans argues the pipeline-writing era is ending. Data engineers who move toward outcome-based orchestration now will have a significant head start.\n\n##### Subscribe to get latest news!\n\nDeep insights for enterprise AI, data, and security leaders\n\nBy submitting your email, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Notice.\n\n## More\n\n© 2026 VentureBeat. All rights reserved.",
      "score": 0.055152804,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000122345597/staff-ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment/",
      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "### Additional Information\n\nWork Personas\n\nWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third-party service.\n\nEqual Opportunity Employer [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations [...] ### Job Description\n\nAbout the Role\n\nYou'll own the end-to-end technical relationship with enterprise customers, translating complex business challenges into high-impact AI solutions built on the Moveworks Platform. This is a customer-facing, field-based role — you'll be the primary technical point of contact throughout the full implementation lifecycle, from solution design through launch. You are equally comfortable whiteboarding with a customer's IT team in the morning and collaborating with internal engineering and product teams in the afternoon.\n\nThis is not a back-office engineering role — you will be in front of customers regularly and are expected to travel up to 25% of the time.\n\nWhat You'll Own\n\nAbout You",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-managed-security-service-google.html",
      "title": "Managed security service on Google Security Operations: PwC",
      "content": "Unlike traditional managed security models that rely on large, centralized teams and highly customized deployments, our unified detection and response service is built on a standardized, multitenant architecture with client-specific instances and data segregation. This approach combines the consistency and cost-efficiency of a standardized service with a more tailored client experience—supported by smaller, dedicated teams rather than large-scale analyst pools.\n\nThe service can be deployed in as little as 30 days, compared to traditional models that often take months to implement. This model enables organizations to access advanced AI-driven security operations capabilities without the significant upfront investment in technology and specialized talent typically required. [...] PwC’s unified detection and response service brings together threat detection and response, vulnerability management, incident response, and penetration testing into a single, standardized managed service.  Delivered using Google’s Security Operations platform, this service enables continuous monitoring and response while leveraging automation, AI, and agentic-driven workflows to improve speed, consistency, and cost-efficiency. [...] The new managed security service from PwC can deliver AI-enabled threat detection and response with simplified operations and predictable pricing.\n\nPwC today announced the launch of its unified detection and response managed security service, enabled by Google Security Operations, designed to help organizations strengthen cybersecurity, reduce operational complexity, and scale protection in an increasingly dynamic threat landscape.",

      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/whats-new-in-the-agentic-data-cloud",
      "title": "What's New in the Agentic Data Cloud | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Continuous enrichment: The Knowledge Catalog delivers continuous enrichment by analyzing usage logs across your organization and profiling data behind the scenes. It learns how your enterprise actually uses data, not just what it is. This extends to unstructured data. The moment a file lands in Google Cloud Storage, our Smart Storage (Preview) instantly tags and enriches images and, soon, PDF objects. The Knowledge Catalog also identifies useful collections of unstructured data and uses Gemini to automatically generate missing schemas, mapping complex relationships so your AI is no longer flying blind.     {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/topics/finance/what-is-financial-consolidation.html",
      "title": "What is financial consolidation? - Workday",
      "content": "AI transforms financial consolidation from a backward-looking process into predictive intelligence. Machine learning algorithms detect anomalies in real time, flagging unusual transactions or data patterns before they are consolidated in reports. Finance teams address issues promptly, rather than discovering errors weeks later during audits.\n\nPredictive analytics enhances business outcomes by forecasting consolidated results before the close is completed. Executives review mid-period performance projections and adjust strategies accordingly. AI models analyze historical consolidation data to predict which entities are likely to miss deadlines or submit questionable figures. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls.",
      "score": 0.8266142,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "Leaders must be able to answer critical questions: which agents are delivering the most value? Where should we invest more? What skills are needed for agents to deliver even more value? When is an agent no longer performing as expected? How do we continuously improve our human-machine teams?\n\nWith the Workday Agent System of Record, organizations can manage AI agents with the same confidence, accountability, and visibility they expect from Workday—while building a smarter, more adaptive organization powered by humans and machines working together.\n\nA remarkable 82% of organizations are already using AI agents. But is your team ready? Read our latest report to learn how businesses are maximizing human potential with AI, featuring insights from nearly 3,000 global leaders. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents.",

      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation\n\n  # While the EU AI Act Stalls, Workday Leads on Trusted, Compliant AI\n\n  Workday champions a strong, workable EU AI Act while already operating ahead of the curve with independently verified governance that defines the standard for trusted AI.\n\n   \n\n  Rich Sauer\n\n  LATEST\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community   Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners [...] Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "“As organizations face pressure for faster, more efficient financial reporting, the need for a streamlined and automated financial close is critical. By harnessing the power of Gemini Enterprise and integrating seamlessly with Workday, that the agent directly addresses these challenges and not only enhances efficiency and accuracy but also saves valuable time for accountants,” said Brian Anderson, Workday Platform Leader, KPMG LLP. “This collaboration allows finance teams to close faster with greater confidence while freeing professionals to focus on higher-impact strategic work.” [...] The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud. [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement.",
      "score": 0.7180613,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://go.theregister.com/feed/www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_enterprise/",
      "title": "Google says it has all the answers for AI agent sprawl - The Register",
      "content": "It comes as the challenge facing businesses has shifted from building individual AI agents to managing hundreds or thousands of them at once - something Workday and others are trying to tackle too.\n\n\"The early versions of AI models were really focused on answering questions that people had and assisting them with creative tasks. Now we’re seeing as the models evolve people wanting to delegate tasks and sequences of tasks to agents,\" Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told reporters during a press briefing. \"And these agents then being able to turn around and use a computer, use all of GCP and Workspace as a tool.\"\n\nTo meet the moment, Google rolled out infrastructure in the form of its eighth generation of TPU chips and security updates through its purchase of Wiz.\n\nboxing [...] Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is organized around four pillars: build, scale, govern, and optimize. On the build side, Google introduced Agent Studio, a low-code interface for creating agents using natural language, alongside an upgraded Agent Development Kit with a new graph-based framework for orchestrating multiple agents working together, the company said during a media prebriefing.\n\nIt also provides an agent registry that gives organizations a central catalog of each internal agent and tool, the company said. Also inside the new platform is an agent marketplace that offers pre-built agents from partners including Atlassian, Oracle, ServiceNow, and Workday.       "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran",
      "content": "Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n Headless 360: Salesforce's latest pitch to let AI do the dev work\n How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk\n Minnesota State payroll problems grew after Workday launch, auditors say\n\nSpeaking to The Register in January, Fivetran CEO George Fraser said vendor pricing is publicly documented and can sometimes be negotiated down.\n\n\"This is about a lot of different data sources. When data sources make it difficult to get access to your own data, it just slows everything down,\" he said.\n\nWith some vendors, a number of projects never get off the ground because it is so hard to get the data, and the developers simply gave up, he said. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n SalesForce\n Workday [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### SaaS\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n# Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n## Report also slams multiple vendors for poor data integration and egress fees\n\nicon  Lindsay Clark\n\nWed 22 Apr 2026  //  16:40 UTC\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\n\njigsaw_puzzle_648\n\n## As AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\n\nREAD MORE [...] It's an issue organizations should watch closely as they build AI agent systems, which depend on company data to generate meaningful responses. Most major vendors offer AI agents within their own platforms but getting those agents to work across different applications is another matter.\n\nFiveTran's newly launched benchmark indicates a number of companies ranking poorly on its categories of data coverage, performance, and egress charges.\n\nAmong the worst on egress fees is Xero, an accounting SaaS for SMBs. FiveTran claims there is a \"tiered monthly fees plus explicit API egress allotments and overage pricing\" to get to the data.",
      "score": 0.4179133,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://forums.theregister.com/forum/all/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran • The Register Forums",
      "content": "My Account\n\nThe Register Home Page\n\n## Topics\n\n### Security\n\n### Off-Prem\n\n### On-Prem\n\n### Software\n\n### Offbeat\n\n## More from The Register\n\n### Special Features\n\n### Vendor Voice\n\n### Resources\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n# back to article Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\nback to article\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\njigsaw\\_puzzle\\_648\nAs AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\nREAD MORE …\n\n## COMMENTS\n\nunstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit [...] ### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon\n\nanonymous\nThumb Up\nThumb Down\nStop\nGo\nWTF?\nFAIL\nHappy\nMeh\nUnhappy\nAngel\nDevil\nFlame\nMushroom\nMegaphone\nTrollface\nFacepalm\nHolmes\nHeadmaster\nCoat\nJoke\nPint\nCoffee/keyboard\nAlert\nBlack Helicopters\nBoffin\nPirate\nAlien\nTerminator\nBig Brother\nChildcatcher\nLinux\nGimp\nWindows\nIT Angle\n\nBiting the hand that feeds IT\n\n## About Us\n\n## Our Websites\n\n## Your Privacy\n\nCopyright.\nAll rights reserved\n© 1998–2026 [...] unstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit\n\nRaaS lacks pagination, causing large report failures\n\nAs a programmer writing a program which will read a different program's output, I do not want pagination in that other program's output, because it is more junk I have to program around to filter out.\n\nI want an uninterrupted flow of pure data.\n\nClaiming that a lack of pagination causes report failures smells like bullshit.\n\n## POST COMMENT House rules\n\nNot a member of The Register?\nCreate a new account here.\n\nRemember me on this computer?\n \nPost anonymously?\n\n### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon",
      "score": 0.3462102,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.databricks.com/blog/how-conversational-analytics-removes-bi-bottleneck",
      "title": "How conversational analytics removes the BI bottleneck - Databricks",
      "content": "Ari: A lot, to be honest. The fundamental thing they are missing is that Genie is built on their data, with their terminology, and governed by their business rules. A generic chatbot has been trained on the internet — Taylor Swift lyrics, historical trivia, whatever. Genie knows what your company means when it uses the terms customer and it knows your fiscal year. It knows your product catalog. That granular specificity is important. And Genie goes way beyond question and answer. Genie does open-ended research. It makes predictions. It connects to your adjacent systems like Salesforce, Workday, SAP, Slack, so you can ask questions that span your entire business. I have seen executives use it to ask genuinely open questions like, \"What should I be focusing on today?\" And those executives",
      "score": 0.23204939,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/topics/finance/what-is-financial-consolidation.html",
      "title": "What is financial consolidation? - Workday",
      "content": "AI transforms financial consolidation from a backward-looking process into predictive intelligence. Machine learning algorithms detect anomalies in real time, flagging unusual transactions or data patterns before they are consolidated in reports. Finance teams address issues promptly, rather than discovering errors weeks later during audits.\n\nPredictive analytics enhances business outcomes by forecasting consolidated results before the close is completed. Executives review mid-period performance projections and adjust strategies accordingly. AI models analyze historical consolidation data to predict which entities are likely to miss deadlines or submit questionable figures. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls.",

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "Leaders must be able to answer critical questions: which agents are delivering the most value? Where should we invest more? What skills are needed for agents to deliver even more value? When is an agent no longer performing as expected? How do we continuously improve our human-machine teams?\n\nWith the Workday Agent System of Record, organizations can manage AI agents with the same confidence, accountability, and visibility they expect from Workday—while building a smarter, more adaptive organization powered by humans and machines working together.\n\nA remarkable 82% of organizations are already using AI agents. But is your team ready? Read our latest report to learn how businesses are maximizing human potential with AI, featuring insights from nearly 3,000 global leaders. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents.",
      "score": 0.750937,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/products/adaptive-planning/overview.html",
      "title": "Adaptive Planning: Enterprise Planning Software (EPM) | Workday IN",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Vendor Management System\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS [...] Anticipate what’s next.\n\nDrive smarter outcomes with AI-powered intelligence, backed by human-in-the-loop governance and grounded in enterprise-wide data.\n\nAnticipate what’s next.\n\nDrive smarter outcomes with AI-powered intelligence, backed by human-in-the-loop governance and grounded in enterprise-wide data.\n\nScale your impact.\n\nOperationalize any data source with automated connections—no storage limits or performance lag—just AI-powered insights that grow with your business.\n\nScale your impact.\n\nOperationalize any data source with automated connections—no storage limits or performance lag—just AI-powered insights that grow with your business.\n\nNavigate with ease.\n\nGet the shortest path from data to boardroom-ready decisions with a personalized, intuitive user interface. [...] # EPM Software\n\nUnlock limitless decision intelligence.\n\nUnlock limitless decision intelligence.\n\nFuel your next era of growth by keeping your brightest minds focused on strategy instead of manual workarounds—and discover why more cloud customers trust Workday Adaptive Planning than our largest competitors, combined.\n\nFuel your next era of growth by keeping your brightest minds focused on strategy instead of manual workarounds—and discover why more cloud customers trust Workday Adaptive Planning than our largest competitors, combined.\n\n### Make smarter decisions with AI-powered  planning.\n\n### Make smarter decisions with AI-powered  planning.\n\nAnticipate what’s next.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-sg/topics/hr/employee-experience.html",
      "title": "What is employee experience? Improve your EX strategy. - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting [...] Not only does this shift necessitate user-friendly technology, it also increasingly demands solutions with AI baked in. Machine learning is promoting major changes across the workplace, including automating employee experience surveys and delivering hyper-personalised employee journeys. With workforces becoming increasingly tech savvy, companies have to match the current pace of technological development.\n\n### 4. Enabling skills development and talent performance.\n\nThere’s no glossing over employee needs when it comes to skills development and talent performance. Ensuring your employees are supported in their development is critical to employee retention. [...] In the past, working with clunky digital interfaces was commonplace. But today employees expect work applications to be of the same quality as the ones on their smartphones. That means companies have to employ intuitive user interfaces, strong integrations between applications, and smooth user experiences.\n\nNot only does this shift necessitate user-friendly technology, it also increasingly demands solutions with AI baked in. Machine learning is promoting major changes across the workplace, including automating employee experience surveys and delivering hyper-personalised employee journeys. With workforces becoming increasingly tech savvy, companies have to match the current pace of technological development.\n\n### 3. Providing user-friendly technology.",
      "score": 0.7366474,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation\n\n  # While the EU AI Act Stalls, Workday Leads on Trusted, Compliant AI\n\n  Workday champions a strong, workable EU AI Act while already operating ahead of the curve with independently verified governance that defines the standard for trusted AI.\n\n   \n\n  Rich Sauer\n\n  LATEST\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community   Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners [...] Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection",
      "score": 0.7295395,

    },
    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/prompt-when-do-you-work-best/",
      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times. Maybe your workday feels like a mysterious blend of brilliance and busyness? Now you can use AI to help decode how you’re spending your time.       "title": "Home Page - Workday Rising US",
      "content": "```\n \n```\n\n \n\nSign Up\n\n# A new work day is on the horizon.\n\nThe countdown for Workday Rising is on. Sign up for important news and updates so you can be the first to register and take advantage of $400 off ticket pricing.\n\nSign Up\n\n \n\n## SAVE THE DATE\n\n### Be first in line to register.\n\nMark your calendar. Workday Rising is heading to Las Vegas, October 12–15, 2026.\n\n''\n\n# Stay in the loop.\n\nLoan Depot Logo\n\n“There’s a lot of excitement at Workday Rising, and the buzz around AI truly feels like the game changer that will transform the finance profession and revolutionize the way that we work.”\n\n—SVP, Accounting Systems\n\n''\n\n## Workday digital event library\n\n### Relive last year’s greatest moments. Dive into can’t-miss highlights from last year’s event.\n\nWatch On Demand\n\n''\n\n### Rising to higher standards.\n\nWorkday Rising has achieved the Events Industry Council (EIC) Sustainable Events Standards’ Platinum certification for the second year in a row. We’re showing the world what’s possible when sustainability becomes a core pillar of event design.\n\nRead Blog\n\nSign up to stay in the know.\n\nSign Up\n\nCustomer for Call for Participation for Workday Rising 2026\n\nOur Call for Participation for Workday Rising is now open. Tell us how you’re redefining work with Workday for a chance to join us as a speaker or receive a customer award.\n\nLearn More\n\n''",
