    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran",
      "content": "Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n Headless 360: Salesforce's latest pitch to let AI do the dev work\n How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk\n Minnesota State payroll problems grew after Workday launch, auditors say\n\nSpeaking to The Register in January, Fivetran CEO George Fraser said vendor pricing is publicly documented and can sometimes be negotiated down.\n\n\"This is about a lot of different data sources. When data sources make it difficult to get access to your own data, it just slows everything down,\" he said.\n\nWith some vendors, a number of projects never get off the ground because it is so hard to get the data, and the developers simply gave up, he said. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n SalesForce\n Workday [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### SaaS\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n# Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n## Report also slams multiple vendors for poor data integration and egress fees\n\nicon  Lindsay Clark\n\nWed 22 Apr 2026  //  16:40 UTC\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\n\njigsaw_puzzle_648\n\n## As AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\n\nREAD MORE [...] It's an issue organizations should watch closely as they build AI agent systems, which depend on company data to generate meaningful responses. Most major vendors offer AI agents within their own platforms but getting those agents to work across different applications is another matter.\n\nFiveTran's newly launched benchmark indicates a number of companies ranking poorly on its categories of data coverage, performance, and egress charges.\n\nAmong the worst on egress fees is Xero, an accounting SaaS for SMBs. FiveTran claims there is a \"tiered monthly fees plus explicit API egress allotments and overage pricing\" to get to the data.",
      "score": 0.4179133,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://forums.theregister.com/forum/all/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran • The Register Forums",
      "content": "My Account\n\nThe Register Home Page\n\n## Topics\n\n### Security\n\n### Off-Prem\n\n### On-Prem\n\n### Software\n\n### Offbeat\n\n## More from The Register\n\n### Special Features\n\n### Vendor Voice\n\n### Resources\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n#### User topics\n\n#### Article topics\n\n# back to article Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\nback to article\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\njigsaw\\_puzzle\\_648\nAs AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\nREAD MORE …\n\n## COMMENTS\n\nunstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit [...] ### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon\n\nanonymous\nThumb Up\nThumb Down\nStop\nGo\nWTF?\nFAIL\nHappy\nMeh\nUnhappy\nAngel\nDevil\nFlame\nMushroom\nMegaphone\nTrollface\nFacepalm\nHolmes\nHeadmaster\nCoat\nJoke\nPint\nCoffee/keyboard\nAlert\nBlack Helicopters\nBoffin\nPirate\nAlien\nTerminator\nBig Brother\nChildcatcher\nLinux\nGimp\nWindows\nIT Angle\n\nBiting the hand that feeds IT\n\n## About Us\n\n## Our Websites\n\n## Your Privacy\n\nCopyright.\nAll rights reserved\n© 1998–2026 [...] unstar\n\nSilver badge\n\n#### Smells Like Bullshit\n\nRaaS lacks pagination, causing large report failures\n\nAs a programmer writing a program which will read a different program's output, I do not want pagination in that other program's output, because it is more junk I have to program around to filter out.\n\nI want an uninterrupted flow of pure data.\n\nClaiming that a lack of pagination causes report failures smells like bullshit.\n\n## POST COMMENT House rules\n\nNot a member of The Register?\nCreate a new account here.\n\nRemember me on this computer?\n \nPost anonymously?\n\n### Enter your comment\n\n### Add an icon",
      "score": 0.3462102,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/in/news/press-releases/2026/04/22/salesforce-google-cloud-launch-new-integrations-deep-context/",
      "title": "Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations - Salesforce",
      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day. A security escalation surfaces in Slack, context lives in a Google Doc, approvals sit in Salesforce, and stakeholder alignment happens over email. Instead of patching together point solutions, companies can now build an interconnected system of engagement that enables teams to move faster across the business and turn ideas into agentic action, all from the same conversation.\n\nKey Integration Features: [...] The agentic enterprise is officially here. Through this Google Cloud and Salesforce partnership, companies can unify fragmented data, automate complex processes, and enable true human-AI collaboration within one cohesive architecture. Built for companies who want to grow faster and smarter in the next era of AI.\n\n#### Related\n\nSalesforce at the event\n\n### Bengaluru Becomes Second City Globally to Host UpLink’s Radical Urban Challenge\n\nDairyDay logo\n\n### Dairy Day Ice Cream Collaborates with Salesforce to Power Its Next Phase of Growth\n\n#### Availability\n\n#### About Salesforce\n\nSalesforce is the #1 AI CRM, empowering companies to connect with their customers in a whole new way through the power of CRM + Data + AI + Trust. For more information, visit www.salesforce.com. [...] Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations\n\nLAS VEGAS, Cloud Next ’26 — April 22, 2026 — Google Cloud and Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) today announced an expanded partnership that will enable AI agents to execute end-to-end workflows across both platforms by solving the long-standing challenge of fragmented data and disconnected systems. With new integrations across Salesforce and Google Cloud, customers can deploy AI agents in tools like Slack and Google Workspace, while Agentforce and Gemini Enterprise provide the intelligence and context behind the scenes. This allows agents to work across systems, reduces risky data movement, and eliminates time lost to context switching.",
      "score": 0.073277526,
      "raw_content": null
    }

  "response_time": 4.33,

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/authors/dean-arnold.html",
      "title": "Dean Arnold | Workday Aus & NZ",
      "content": "Australia (English)\n\n# Dean Arnold\n\nVice President, AI Platform\n\nDean Arnold serves as vice president of AI Platform at Workday, leading the platform organization that builds essential AI and machine learning capabilities across the unified finance and HR cloud. He defines strategy for agentic human-digital workforce collaboration and scales the Open Agent Ecosystem with partners. This work aligns with Workday's pivot to AI-enabled experiences that differentiate the platform and demonstrate value to customers.\n\nPreviously, he led the Agent System of Record, and guided Workday Extend as VP and chief architect after roles in integration and collaboration architecture. His tenure reflects consistent delivery of scalable systems and ecosystem growth overall. [...] Dean holds an honors bachelor's degree in computer science from the University of Surrey.",
      "score": 0.8437023,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "Leaders must be able to answer critical questions: which agents are delivering the most value? Where should we invest more? What skills are needed for agents to deliver even more value? When is an agent no longer performing as expected? How do we continuously improve our human-machine teams?\n\nWith the Workday Agent System of Record, organizations can manage AI agents with the same confidence, accountability, and visibility they expect from Workday—while building a smarter, more adaptive organization powered by humans and machines working together.\n\nA remarkable 82% of organizations are already using AI agents. But is your team ready? Read our latest report to learn how businesses are maximizing human potential with AI, featuring insights from nearly 3,000 global leaders. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents.",
      "score": 0.750937,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/prompt-when-do-you-work-best/",
      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times.     {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/workday_rippling_slack_flunk_fivetran_data_access_test/",
      "title": "Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran",
      "content": "Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl\n Headless 360: Salesforce's latest pitch to let AI do the dev work\n How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk\n Minnesota State payroll problems grew after Workday launch, auditors say\n\nSpeaking to The Register in January, Fivetran CEO George Fraser said vendor pricing is publicly documented and can sometimes be negotiated down.\n\n\"This is about a lot of different data sources. When data sources make it difficult to get access to your own data, it just slows everything down,\" he said.\n\nWith some vendors, a number of projects never get off the ground because it is so hard to get the data, and the developers simply gave up, he said. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n SalesForce\n Workday [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### SaaS\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n# Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n## Report also slams multiple vendors for poor data integration and egress fees\n\nicon  Lindsay Clark\n\nWed 22 Apr 2026  //  16:40 UTC\n\nWorkday, Rippling, and Salesforce-owned Slack rank among the worst performers for enterprise data movement, according to a new industry benchmark tracking the speeds needed to power analytics, machine learning, and AI agents.\n\njigsaw_puzzle_648\n\n## As AI agents join SaaS, AWS tells users to expect more pricing puzzles\n\nREAD MORE [...] It's an issue organizations should watch closely as they build AI agent systems, which depend on company data to generate meaningful responses. Most major vendors offer AI agents within their own platforms but getting those agents to work across different applications is another matter.\n\nFiveTran's newly launched benchmark indicates a number of companies ranking poorly on its categories of data coverage, performance, and egress charges.\n\nAmong the worst on egress fees is Xero, an accounting SaaS for SMBs. FiveTran claims there is a \"tiered monthly fees plus explicit API egress allotments and overage pricing\" to get to the data.",
      "score": 0.4179133,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next/welcome-to-google-cloud-next26",
      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "Gemini momentum with ISVs: Leading companies like Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds more are deeply integrating Gemini into their products, bringing more AI-powered features and agents to their users.\n More enterprise-grade agents from partners: Already, thousands of agents built by our partners are available in Google Cloud Marketplace. Now, you can access select agents directly through Gemini Enterprise, making it easier for you to find the right agent for your team’s needs from partners like Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow and Workday. [...] Manufacturing: Bosch, EcoVadis, GE Appliances, Tata Steel\n Governments:, German Armed Forces, Maryland, NATO, UK Department for Transport, U.S. Department of Energy, U.S. Department of Transportation, U.S. Food and Drug Administration, SENAI Brazil\n ISVs: Lovable, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP,  ServiceNow, UKG, Workday\n Systems Integrators: Accenture, BCG, Capgemini, Cognizant, Deloitte, HCLTech, IBM, Infosys, Kyndryl, KPMG, McKinsey, PwC, TCS\n Startups: Emergent AI, Jusbrasil, Notion, Parallel Web Systems, Reve, Safe Superintelligence, Thinking Machines Lab, Unico\n SMBs: Apex Leaders, B3Networks, Constructora Las Galias, OneUnited Bank, Papel Semente, Premji Invest, Thrive Restaurant Group\n And many, many more showcased on our website. [...] Cross-Cloud Lakehouse, standardized on Apache Iceberg, is our Lakehouse that enables you to leave your data in AWS or Azure (coming later this year) while querying it instantly—without the friction of vendor lock-in or the cost of data movement. We provide zero-copy access to applications, operating systems, and AI platforms, such as Databricks, Palantir, Salesforce Data360, SAP, ServiceNow, Snowflake, Workday, and more.\n Lightning Engine for Apache Spark delivers a real-time, serverless engine that is up to 4.5x faster than open-source alternatives and offers up to 2x better price-performance over the leading competitor for large datasets.",
      "score": 0.41137156,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/partners/how-google-cloud-partner-ecosystem-is-building-the-agentic-enterprise",
      "title": "How Google Cloud partner ecosystem is building the agentic enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "KPMG is deploying Gemini Enterprise with a life sciences company and launching a new Financial Close Companion agent built with Workday and Google Cloud.\n\nKyndryl is deepening its Google Cloud partnership with expanded Google Distributed Cloud services for sovereign, AI-ready applications.\n\nMcKinsey is launching the McKinsey Google Transformation Group to accelerate enterprise AI outcomes with Gemini Enterprise, combining its strategic expertise with Google's AI stack to help organizations scale agentic transformation\n\nPwC is launching a dedicated Google Cloud AI Center of Excellence to help organizations scale AI adoption, pairing industry expertise with Gemini Enterprise to deploy AI agents that reason, act, and automate processes at scale. [...] Today, Agent Gallery provides access to a wide range of third-party agents. These agents have been built on top of our secure, enterprise-grade infrastructure, meaning customers can deploy them within their businesses with the highest levels of governance and confidence. Today, this includes agents built by Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Lovable, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Replit, S&P Global, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and more.\n\n### Empowering global consulting partners to drive AI transformations\n\nToday, Google Cloud’s global consulting and systems integrator partners offer customers more than 330,000 experts trained on implementing Google AI. At Next, we are expanding our partnerships with every major systems integrator, including: [...] Box is launching new Box Agents powered by Gemini 3 Flash and Gemini Enterprise, helping enterprises transform static files into actionable intelligence by natively integrating AI orchestration into their secure content management workflows.\n\nDocuSign is using Gemini to power new features that summarize complex agreements, identify key clauses, and help users understand the implications of their contracts.\n\nOracle is launching the Oracle AI Database Connector for Gemini Enterprise. This new agent enables end users to ask business questions of their Oracle data in natural language in Gemini Enterprise, without needing to write SQL or understand the underlying data model.",
      "score": 0.3623603,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/three-tiers-of-agentic-ai---and-when-to-use-none-of-them/4510377",
      "title": "Three tiers of Agentic AI - and when to use none of them",
      "content": "That gap is not a capability problem. The infrastructure has matured. Tool calling is standard across all major models. Frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Microsoft Agent Framework abstract orchestration logic. Model Context Protocol standardizes how agents access external tools and data sources. Google's Agent-to-Agent protocol now under Linux Foundation governance with over 50 enterprise technology partners including Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, and Workday standardizes how agents coordinate with each other. The protocols are in place. The frameworks are production ready.\n\nThe gap is a selection and governance problem. [...] What we built: Copilot Studio handled all high-frequency, low-complexity queries shipment tracking, account status, open cases through Power Platform connectors. Zero custom code. That covered roughly 78% of actual interaction volume. Requests requiring multi-source reasoning competitive positioning on a specific lane, churn risk across an account portfolio, contract renewal analysis delegated via authenticated HTTP action to a pro-code multi-agent service on Azure. A retrieval agent pulled deal history and market intelligence through MCP-exposed tools. A synthesis agent composed the recommendation with confidence scoring. Structured JSON back to the low-code layer, rendered as an adaptive card in Teams. [...] ### Share this page\n\nShare to LinkedIn\nShare to Facebook\nShare to X\nShare to Reddit\nShare to Blue Sky\nSubscribe to RSS\nShare to Email",
      "score": 0.32357296,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "AI isn't the problem; your data might be. - PwC",
      "content": "That's where many AI strategies hit a wall. Not because the models aren't capable, but because the data isn't ready. They're lacking trusted data, and the ability to certify it at scale. In fact, PwC’s 2025 Digital Trust Insights survey found that data protection and trust rank as the number one priority for business executives globally. Industry leaders recognize that without trusted data, transformation efforts stall before they scale.\n\n## Enter our enterprise data agents\n\nOur enterprise data agents are a collection of intelligent, AI-enabled agents purpose-built to handle the entire spectrum of enterprise data functions—faster, smarter, and more consistently than manual processes ever could.",

    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/the-new-gemini-enterprise-one-platform-for-agent-development",
      "title": "The new Gemini Enterprise: one platform for agent development | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Activating third-party agents from within the Gemini Enterprise app: We are now bringing the full catalog of partner-built agents from Google Cloud Marketplace directly into the Agent Gallery. You no longer need to leave your workflow to find and deploy powerful third-party agents. The app provides a secure request-and-approval gateway that connects employee needs to IT-validated solutions.\n A growing library of trusted solutions: Agent Gallery features an expansive collection of validated agents from SaaS leaders and innovative startups. For example, you can use specialized agents from partners like Adobe, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and Workday–directly within the Gemini Enterprise app. [...] For knowledge workers: Gemini Enterprise app is the front door for AI for every employee in your organization. Knowledge workers can build their own agents with our no-code Agent Designer or leverage a wide range of partner-built agents from Google Cloud Marketplace. And these agents can be easily shared across the organization with full governance and compliance controls built-in. Inbox in Gemini Enterprise provides a central location to manage and monitor agents, including long-running agents. [...] 1. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform isour new developer platform and evolution of Vertex AI. It brings together our full suite of models, development, and tuning services, with new features for businesses to build, scale, govern, and optimize agents that can work autonomously to execute complex business workflows. Learn more here.\n2. Gemini Enterprise app lets teams discover, create, share, and run AI agents in a single, secure environment. The Gemini Enterprise app is built on the Agent Platform, meaning all governance, security, and identity capabilities come standard, and it integrates with your enterprise data – including data in third-party systems–through connectors.",
      "score": 0.05086082,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times. Maybe your workday feels like a mysterious blend of brilliance and busyness? Now you can use AI to help decode how you’re spending your time.       "title": "Principal Consultant-Workday Functional-Denmark - Infosys",
      "content": "With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey.     {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/the-new-gemini-enterprise-one-platform-for-agent-development",
      "title": "The new Gemini Enterprise: one platform for agent development | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Activating third-party agents from within the Gemini Enterprise app: We are now bringing the full catalog of partner-built agents from Google Cloud Marketplace directly into the Agent Gallery. You no longer need to leave your workflow to find and deploy powerful third-party agents. The app provides a secure request-and-approval gateway that connects employee needs to IT-validated solutions.\n A growing library of trusted solutions: Agent Gallery features an expansive collection of validated agents from SaaS leaders and innovative startups. For example, you can use specialized agents from partners like Adobe, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and Workday–directly within the Gemini Enterprise app. [...] For knowledge workers: Gemini Enterprise app is the front door for AI for every employee in your organization. Knowledge workers can build their own agents with our no-code Agent Designer or leverage a wide range of partner-built agents from Google Cloud Marketplace. And these agents can be easily shared across the organization with full governance and compliance controls built-in. Inbox in Gemini Enterprise provides a central location to manage and monitor agents, including long-running agents. [...] 1. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform isour new developer platform and evolution of Vertex AI. It brings together our full suite of models, development, and tuning services, with new features for businesses to build, scale, govern, and optimize agents that can work autonomously to execute complex business workflows. Learn more here.\n2. Gemini Enterprise app lets teams discover, create, share, and run AI agents in a single, secure environment. The Gemini Enterprise app is built on the Agent Platform, meaning all governance, security, and identity capabilities come standard, and it integrates with your enterprise data – including data in third-party systems–through connectors.",

      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/topics/finance/what-is-financial-consolidation.html",
      "title": "What is financial consolidation? - Workday",
      "content": "AI transforms financial consolidation from a backward-looking process into predictive intelligence. Machine learning algorithms detect anomalies in real time, flagging unusual transactions or data patterns before they are consolidated in reports. Finance teams address issues promptly, rather than discovering errors weeks later during audits.\n\nPredictive analytics enhances business outcomes by forecasting consolidated results before the close is completed. Executives review mid-period performance projections and adjust strategies accordingly. AI models analyze historical consolidation data to predict which entities are likely to miss deadlines or submit questionable figures. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls. [...] Automation accelerates beyond basic data collection. Workday uses AI to suggest elimination entries, recommend currency conversion adjustments, and auto-classify transactions based on historical patterns. The system learns from corrections and improves accuracy over time.\n\nNatural language processing lets users query consolidated data conversationally. Instead of building complex reports, finance teams ask questions and receive instant analysis. For example, a CFO can simply ask, “Show me Q3 profit margins for subsidiaries in Asia compared to last year and explain significant variances,” and receive immediate visual analysis with AI-generated explanations. Conversational querying democratises financial insights across the organisation while maintaining governance controls.",
      "score": 0.8266142,
      "raw_content": null

      "content": "Workday automates routine matching and approvals, while AI will help resolve complex exceptions—empowering finance teams to devote their energy to strategic analysis. The future belongs to finance teams that leverage technology to anticipate rather than react.\n\n## The Workday role in healthcare supply chain success. [...] ## Future outlook: Finance and healthcare supply chains in the cloud era.\n\nAI is transforming supply chain financial oversight from a reactive to a predictive approach. Algorithms detect price anomalies across thousands of SKUs, forecast demand disruptions before they impact patient care, and recommend optimal reorder points that balance carrying costs against stockout risk. [...] Predictive analytics: Machine learning algorithms identify cost anomalies and forecast supply needs before shortages disrupt care delivery\n Audit readiness: Complete transaction trails from requisition through payment, eliminate compliance gaps, and reduce audit preparation time\n Scalability: Cloud infrastructure accommodates mergers, new facilities, and demand fluctuations without expensive hardware investments or system replacements\n Mobile functionality: Real-time approval workflows prevent bottlenecks that force emergency orders at premium prices",

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "Leaders must be able to answer critical questions: which agents are delivering the most value? Where should we invest more? What skills are needed for agents to deliver even more value? When is an agent no longer performing as expected? How do we continuously improve our human-machine teams?\n\nWith the Workday Agent System of Record, organizations can manage AI agents with the same confidence, accountability, and visibility they expect from Workday—while building a smarter, more adaptive organization powered by humans and machines working together.\n\nA remarkable 82% of organizations are already using AI agents. But is your team ready? Read our latest report to learn how businesses are maximizing human potential with AI, featuring insights from nearly 3,000 global leaders. [...] ## From Introduction to Enterprise Scale\n\nOrganizations wouldn’t hire thousands of employees without an HR system to manage them. The same discipline is now required for AI agents. With more than 20 years as a leader in helping customers manage their people, Workday is uniquely positioned to extend that trusted foundation to AI agents.\n\nWith ASOR, AI agents are managed as part of the overall organizational structure, alongside employees. This makes it easier to understand how agents are used, what skills they have, and how they support critical business operations and functions. Equally, ASOR ensures agents are managed securely, governed consistently, and aligned with workforce and financial planning from day one. [...] AI governance goes beyond data access and security. It requires an understanding of regional governance and compliance requirements. As standards emerge, ASOR will allow organizations to understand their compliance and conformance to global, regional, and organizational policies. This level of accountability and governance is not just for Workday-delivered agents, but also those from our partners and third parties. The result is enterprise-wide visibility, governance, and insights into all AI agents.",

      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation\n\n  # While the EU AI Act Stalls, Workday Leads on Trusted, Compliant AI\n\n  Workday champions a strong, workable EU AI Act while already operating ahead of the curve with independently verified governance that defines the standard for trusted AI.\n\n   \n\n  Rich Sauer\n\n  LATEST\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community   Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners [...] Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection",

    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/prompt-when-do-you-work-best/",
      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times. Maybe your workday feels like a mysterious blend of brilliance and busyness? Now you can use AI to help decode how you’re spending your time.       "url": "https://digitalcareers.infosys.com/global-careers/company-job/description/reqid/147287BR",
      "title": "Principal Consultant-Workday Functional-Denmark - Infosys",
      "content": "With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. [...] Responsibilities:  \n  \n\n Own functional design for assigned Workday modules\n Translate business requirements into Workday configuration using best practices\n Lead requirement workshops, design sessions, and solution walkthroughs\n Ensure solutions balance business needs with Workday standard functionality\n Review and validate configuration completed by functional analysts or partners\n Ensure configuration is scalable, auditable, and future-ready\n\n   \nRequired [...] Role – Principal consultant  \nTechnology – Workday Functional HCM  \nLocation – Denmark  \nCompensation – Competitive (including bonus)  \n  \nJob Description:  \nWorkday Functional Lead is responsible for owning the functional design, configuration, and successful delivery of Workday solutions aligned to business requirements.       "url": "https://www.zdnet.com/article/google-chrome-enterprise-update-auto-browse-skills-gemini-summary/",
      "title": "Google brings Auto Browse and Skills to Chrome Enterprise - ZDNET",
      "content": "1. Auto browse\n\n## 2. Skills\n\nGoogle is also bringing \"Skills\" to Chrome Enterprise, letting you save and reuse AI workflows. This one-click feature launched recently for consumers in Chrome, but is now coming to Workspace. The idea is, if you find yourself asking Gemini the same thing throughout the workday, you can save that prompt as a Skill and run it instantly on any page in your browser.\n\nAlso: I tested ChatGPT Plus vs. Gemini Pro to see which is better\n\nJust type / in the Gemini side panel, select your Skill, and it runs immediately on the page you're viewing. Over time, you can build a library of repeatable tasks customized to how you or your team works. As a safeguard, most Skills require confirmation before running. [...] Google said Workspace prompts are not used to train its AI models.\n\n2. Skills\n\n## 3. Gemini Summary\n\nSince employees may already be using AI tools on their own, Google is flagging a \"Shadow AI\" risk where sensitive data could be shared with unmonitored tools. To address this, Chrome Enterprise now gives IT teams visibility into AI usage across the workplace, including which tools employees access, unusual behavior from extensions or agents, and unapproved tools. [...] # Google brings Auto Browse and Skills to Chrome Enterprise - and a new 'Gemini Summary'\n\nelyse-betters-picaro\nGoogle brings Auto browse and Skills to Chrome Enterprise - and a new 'Gemini Summary'\n\nFollow ZDNET: Add us as a preferred source on Google.\n\n### ZDNET's key takeaways\n\nGoogle has announced new Chrome features specifically for companies. The update brings AI capabilities to workers while giving IT teams greater control over which AI tools are used across the workplace. Chrome Enterprise is now a place where work gets done, AI operates inline, and administrators can enforce security, all within the browser.\n\nAlso:Google bets $32B on AI agent cyber force as security arms race escalates\n\n## 1. Auto Browse",

You are equally comfortable whiteboarding with a customer's IT team in the morning and collaborating with internal engineering and product teams in the afternoon.\n\nThis is not a back-office engineering role — you will be in front of customers regularly and are expected to travel up to 25% of the time.\n\nWhat You'll Own\n\nAbout You",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/au/en/why-kpmg/alliances/workday/workday-student.html",
      "title": "Transform student management with KPMG and Workday",
      "content": "### AI driven insights and predictive capability\n\nKPMG brings deep student lifecycle knowledge to make Workday Student’s AI and predictive analytics actionable, helping universities anticipate student needs and risks early. This shifts institutions from reactive administration to more proactive interventions, smarter resource planning and stronger outcomes.\n\n### Intelligent automation and streamlined workflows\n\nRoutine administration is automated through Workday Student’s configurable workflows and KPMG’s leading practices and accelerators. The result is improved accuracy, speed and compliance, freeing staff to focus on higher value student support and academic decision-making.\n\n### Strengthened compliance, governance and change readiness [...] Rapid advances in technology are offering universities an ideal opportunity to improve their student experiences and reshape operations. Even as demographics shift, enrolment patterns evolve and legacy systems strain under modern demands, institutions have a powerful chance to reimagine how they support staff and enable students.\n\n## What is Workday Student?\n\nWorkday Student, co created with leading universities, is a modern, mobile first, end to end student administration platform that removes silos by unifying academic, financial, Human Capital Management (HCM) and operational processes into a single cloud native, AI fuelled system.\n\n### KPMG’s trusted approach [...] Workday services partner logo services partner\nWorkday services partner logo advisory partner\nWorkday services partner logo AMS partner\nWorkday extend partner logo\n\n## How KPMG + Workday Student can help transform the student experience confidently\n\nKPMG and Workday help universities bring HCM, finance and student operations together into one cloud native, AI ready ecosystem. By combining KPMG’s pre-built models, accelerators and sector experience with Workday Student’s intelligent platform, institutions can transform their student experience at the front and back ends while accessing new efficiencies and reduced risk and costs.",
      "score": 0.7827459,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "“As organizations face pressure for faster, more efficient financial reporting, the need for a streamlined and automated financial close is critical. By harnessing the power of Gemini Enterprise and integrating seamlessly with Workday, that the agent directly addresses these challenges and not only enhances efficiency and accuracy but also saves valuable time for accountants,” said Brian Anderson, Workday Platform Leader, KPMG LLP. “This collaboration allows finance teams to close faster with greater confidence while freeing professionals to focus on higher-impact strategic work.” [...] The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud. [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement.",
      "score": 0.7180613,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/prompt-when-do-you-work-best/",
      "title": "An AI prompt to make your workday work better | Microsoft Signal Blog",
      "content": "A single prompt can reveal when you’re most focused, most creative and most reactive, no spreadsheets required. Give this one a try and get your schedule to work with you, not against you.\n\nJust go to Microsoft 365 Copilot and type in:\n\n> Look across recent documents, emails, chat activity and file edits from the past two months to identify times of day when I do my most focused work, creative work and reactive work. Then create a profile of my natural work rhythm and give suggestions for structuring my days that support energy, boundaries and recovery.\n\nThe Prompt is a regular Signal feature to give you specific, super-quick ways you can use AI to solve problems, boost your creativity and get more done at work.\n\nLearn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot. [...] Microsoft   Signal    Official Microsoft Blog Microsoft Source  Asia Canada Europe, Middle East and Africa Latin America   Top News on LinkedIn The Monthly Tech-In For Press         \n\nSkip to content\n\nBack to posts\n\n–  The estimated reading time is 1 min.\n\n# When do you work best?\n\nshapes with a torn-paper label reading ‘The prompt.’\n\n### Author\n\nSamantha KubotaMicrosoft writer\n\nWe’ve all looked at the clock and wondered “how is it already 4 p.m.?!” at least a few times. Maybe your workday feels like a mysterious blend of brilliance and busyness? Now you can use AI to help decode how you’re spending your time. [...] Learn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot.\n\n \n\nSurface Pro Surface Laptop Surface Laptop Studio 2 Copilot for organizations Copilot for personal use AI in Windows Explore Microsoft products Windows 11 apps   Account profile Download Center Microsoft Store support Returns Order tracking Certified Refurbished Microsoft Store Promise Flexible Payments   Microsoft in education Devices for education Microsoft Teams for Education Microsoft 365 Education How to buy for your school Educator training and development Deals for students and parents AI for education",

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/three-tiers-of-agentic-ai---and-when-to-use-none-of-them/4510377",
      "title": "Three tiers of Agentic AI - and when to use none of them",
      "content": "That gap is not a capability problem. The infrastructure has matured. Tool calling is standard across all major models. Frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Microsoft Agent Framework abstract orchestration logic. Model Context Protocol standardizes how agents access external tools and data sources. Google's Agent-to-Agent protocol now under Linux Foundation governance with over 50 enterprise technology partners including Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, and Workday standardizes how agents coordinate with each other. The protocols are in place. The frameworks are production ready.\n\nThe gap is a selection and governance problem. [...] What we built: Copilot Studio handled all high-frequency, low-complexity queries shipment tracking, account status, open cases through Power Platform connectors. Zero custom code. That covered roughly 78% of actual interaction volume. Requests requiring multi-source reasoning competitive positioning on a specific lane, churn risk across an account portfolio, contract renewal analysis delegated via authenticated HTTP action to a pro-code multi-agent service on Azure. A retrieval agent pulled deal history and market intelligence through MCP-exposed tools. A synthesis agent composed the recommendation with confidence scoring. Structured JSON back to the low-code layer, rendered as an adaptive card in Teams. [...] ### Share this page\n\nShare to LinkedIn\nShare to Facebook\nShare to X\nShare to Reddit\nShare to Blue Sky\nSubscribe to RSS\nShare to Email",
      "score": 0.32357296,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "Portugal - Português\n\n  + Portugal - Portuguese\n\n  Saudi Arabia - English\n\n  + Saudi Arabia - English\n\n  Singapore - English\n\n  + Singapore - English\n\n  South Africa - English\n\n  + South Africa - English\n\n  대한민국 - 한국어\n\n  + South Korea - Korean\n\n  España - Español\n\n  + Spain - Spanish\n\n  Sverige - Svenska\n\n  + Sweden - Swedish\n\n  Switzerland - English\n\n  + Switzerland - English\n\n  台湾 - 中文\n\n  + Taiwan - Chinese\n\n  ไทย - ไทย\n\n  + Thailand - Thai\n\n  Türkiye - Türkçe\n\n  + Turkiye - Turkish\n\n  United Arab Emirates - English\n\n  + United Arab Emirates - English\n\n  United Kingdom - English\n\n  + United Kingdom - English\n\n  United States - English\n\n  + United States - English\n\n# Workday implementation [...] With Mercer and Workday Financial Management, harness the power of cloud-based enterprise management to take advantage of all of our Workday capabilities.\n\n  Workday financial management\n ### Workday Services\n\n  Our expertise and HR knowledge improves the return you get from Workday.\n\n  Workday Services\n ### Workday automated testing\n\n  Like all ERP/HRIS applications, Workday requires ongoing care and maintenance to ensure smooth operation. We understand the challenges of maintaining operational efficiency, data integrity, and managing change.\n\n  Workday automated testing\n ### Mercer Accelerate™\n\n  Mercer Accelerate™ is a fast, efficient, cost-centric solution for organizations to get the most out of their Workday deployment.\n\n  Mercer Accelerate\n\nRelated products for purchase [...] Contact us\n\n## How we help with Workday implementation\n\nOur consultants take the time to understand your organization, your financial and HR strategy, and the business case for selecting Workday.     {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/news/press-releases/2026/04/22/salesforce-google-cloud-launch-new-integrations-deep-context/",
      "title": "Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations",
      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day. A security escalation surfaces in Slack, context lives in a Google Doc, approvals sit in Salesforce, and stakeholder alignment happens over email. Instead of patching together point solutions, companies can now build an interconnected system of engagement that enables teams to move faster across the business and turn ideas into agentic action, all from the same conversation.\n\nKey Integration Features: [...] Chiesi Group selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences.\n\n## Chiesi Group Selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences to Transform Customer Engagement\n\nSalesforce Announces Salesforce Headless 360\n\n## Introducing Salesforce Headless 360. No Browser Required.\n\n### Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.\n\n## Share article\n\n## Just For You\n\nChiesi Group selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences.\n\n## Chiesi Group Selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences to Transform Customer Engagement\n\nSalesforce Announces Salesforce Headless 360\n\n## Introducing Salesforce Headless 360. No Browser Required.\n\n### Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.\n\n## Explore related content by topic\n\n## Related Articles [...] Under the expanded partnership, the two companies will also develop new features that will make it simpler for customers to transition from manual oversight to autonomous operations – letting employees collaborate where the work is already happening and using their tech stack to execute complex workflows. These integrations bring Google Cloud and Salesforce together across every layer of the Agentic Enterprise — the systems of context, work, agency, and engagement that turn raw intelligence into enterprise work.",

    {
      "url": "https://venturebeat.com/data/the-modern-data-stack-was-built-for-humans-asking-questions-google-just-rebuilt-its-for-agents-taking-action",
      "title": "The modern data stack was built for humans asking questions ...",
      "content": "The practical implication for data engineering teams is that the Knowledge Catalog scales to the full data estate, not just the curated subset that a small team of data stewards can maintain by hand. The catalog covers BigQuery, Spanner, AlloyDB and Cloud SQL natively, and federates with third-party catalogs including Collibra, Atlan and Datahub. Zero-copy federation extends semantic context from SaaS applications including SAP, Salesforce Data360, ServiceNow and Workday without requiring data movement.\n\n## Google's lakehouse goes cross cloud\n\nGoogle has had a data lakehouse called BigLake since 2022. Initially it was limited to just Google data, but in recent years has had some limited federation capabilities enabling enterprises to query data found in other locations. [...] Now, with AI agents increasingly expected to take actions directly on behalf of the business, Gutmans argued that data platforms must evolve into systems of action.\n\"We need to make sure that all of enterprise data can be activated with AI, that includes both structured and unstructured data,\" Gutmans said. \"We need to make sure that there's the right level of trust, which also means it's not just about getting access to the data, but really understanding the data.\" [...] Cross-cloud egress costs are a hidden tax on agentic AI. Storage-based federation via open Iceberg standards is emerging as the architectural answer across Google, Databricks and Snowflake. Enterprises locked into proprietary federation approaches should be stress-testing those costs at agent-scale query volumes.\n\nGutmans argues the pipeline-writing era is ending. Data engineers who move toward outcome-based orchestration now will have a significant head start.\n\n##### Subscribe to get latest news!\n\nDeep insights for enterprise AI, data, and security leaders\n\nBy submitting your email, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Notice.\n\n## More\n\n© 2026 VentureBeat. All rights reserved.",
      "score": 0.055152804,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/about/press-room/deloitte-launches-google-cloud-agentic-transformation-practice.html",
      "title": "Deloitte Launches Google Cloud Agentic Transformation Practice",
      "content": "Deloitte’s “2026 State of AI in the Enterprise” report shows AI tools are now available to the workforce of about 60% of surveyed organizations — industries including consumer; energy, resources and industrials; financial services; life sciences and health care; technology, media and telecommunications; and government and public services — signaling a shift from pilots to true enterprise scale.\n\n\"Organizations are moving beyond AI pilots and into production, and how they make that shift will define their competitive edge,” said Jason Girzadas, CEO, Deloitte US. “With Google AI and Gemini Enterprise, Deloitte helps clients deploy secure, scalable AI that drives productivity, streamlines operations and sharpens decision-making.\" [...] Deloitte provides industry-leading audit, consulting, tax and advisory services to many of the world’s most admired brands, including nearly 90% of the Fortune 500® and more than 9,000 U.S.-based private companies. At Deloitte, we strive to live our purpose of making an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. We bring together distinct talents, technologies, disciplines, and an ecosystem of alliances to help tackle today’s most complex business challenges and drive long-term progress. Deloitte is proud to be part of the largest global professional services network serving our clients in the markets that are most important to them. Bringing more than 180 years of service, our network of member firms spans more than 150 countries and territories. Learn how Deloitte’s [...] Building on Deloitte’s AI experience across Fortune 500 clients and its own business, this expanded collaboration is designed to deliver transformation services for organizations looking to deploy agentic AI securely and at scale. The new practice will deploy Deloitte’s rapidly expanding suite of Google AI and agentic assets and use Deloitte Ascend™, its AI-infused services and delivery platform. Services span strategy and process redesign through implementation, governance and adoption, with an industry-focus on retail, health care, financial services and government and public services.",
      "score": 0.045645304,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

      "url": "https://www.cio.com/microsoft/",
      "title": "Microsoft – CIO",
      "content": "By acquiring the Swedish artificial intelligence company and integrating its technology into its existing people management software, Workday aims to \"create the work experience of the future,\" a scenario of human employees empowered as never before by AI agents.\n\n### Microsoft and Workday collaborate to manage agentic AI workers\n\nWith an ID from Microsoft and a records in Workday, AI agents will be able to securely interoperate with each other and with their human coworkers.\n\n### World’s largest pulp manufacturer streamlined SAP S/4HANA integration for better outcomes\n\n### Welcome to the new world of risk: Microsoft cuts off services to energy company without notice [...] ### Microsoft lets shopping bots loose in a sandbox\n\n## Articles\n\n### Navigating Microsoft Project Online retirement: Risks, costs and strategic opportunities\n\nProject Online’s going away, but smart leaders see more than a deadline — they see a chance to future-proof how their org plans and executes.\n\n### Microsoft ends volume pricing, potentially costing companies millions\n\nThe new cost model takes effect Nov. 1, but experts say Microsoft partners should still have some pricing flexibility as IT leaders look into rightsizing, striking new deals, or alternate solutions.\n\n### Workday buys Sana to address the ‘era of superintelligence for work’ [...] ## Topics\n\n# Microsoft\n\nMicrosoft | News, how-tos, features, reviews, and videos\n\n## Latest from today\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft facing CMA probe of its business software portfolio\n\nThe regulator will commence its investigation in May, examining concerns emerging from its cloud market probe.\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft shuffles more of its senior AI leadership\n\nImage\n\n### FTC digs deeper into Microsoft’s bundling and licensing practices\n\n### What hyperscalers’ hyper-spending on data centers tells CIOs\n\n### Microsoft’s bet on AI causes jitters in the market and the enterprise\n\n### AI market correction: CIOs step away from the hype and vendors adjust\n\n### Microsoft has yet to ignite enthusiasm for agentic AI\n\n### SAP touts Microsoft disaster recovery plan for Europe; Analysts doubt it will work",

      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "“As organizations face pressure for faster, more efficient financial reporting, the need for a streamlined and automated financial close is critical. By harnessing the power of Gemini Enterprise and integrating seamlessly with Workday, that the agent directly addresses these challenges and not only enhances efficiency and accuracy but also saves valuable time for accountants,” said Brian Anderson, Workday Platform Leader, KPMG LLP. “This collaboration allows finance teams to close faster with greater confidence while freeing professionals to focus on higher-impact strategic work.” [...] The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud. [...] This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement.",

      "title": "ServiceNow ITAM Developer - Okta",
      "content": "This is a highly collaborative and integrated role. You will work closely with our SAM Managers to translate complex asset management business processes into technical solutions that identify and capture cost-saving opportunities. You will manage a sophisticated ecosystem that connects financial data from Workday and NetSuite, procurement data from Coupa, and endpoint data from JAMF and Intune. Your expertise will bridge the gap between technical data modeling and strategic business outcomes.\n\nResponsibilities [...] Build and maintain automated workflows for the hardware lifecycle, following Common Service Data Model (CSDM) best practices to keep the Asset and CI tables in sync.\n Implement HAM Pro features like Hardware Model Normalization, Mobile Asset Tracking, and Asset Inventory Audit workflows.\n Manage Cloud Cost Management (CCM) workflows to provide visibility and optimization for AWS and GCP spend.\n Manage Software Spend Detection by integrating financial data from Workday and NetSuite to identify tail spend and shadow IT.\n Design advanced dashboards and Performance Analytics widgets to report on compliance, cloud savings, and hardware refresh cycles for executive leadership. [...] Lead technical delivery for ServiceNow SAM and HAM, focusing on the Content Library, Normalization engines, and Reconciliation processes.\n Partner with business stakeholders to analyze asset management business processes and implement automated workflows that drive license reclamation and cost-avoidance strategies.\n Oversee and troubleshoot complex integrations with core systems, including Coupa, Okta, JAMF, and Intune, ensuring data flow is consistent and accurate.\n Build, configure, and maintain integrations with SaaS connectors (e.g., Salesforce, Workday, Adobe) and develop custom integrations for unique software vendors using IntegrationHub.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/news/press-releases/2026/04/22/salesforce-google-cloud-launch-new-integrations-deep-context/",
      "title": "Salesforce and Google Cloud Launch New Integrations",
      "content": "The modern workday is governed by a hidden toggling tax that costs the average employee two hours of productivity every single day. A security escalation surfaces in Slack, context lives in a Google Doc, approvals sit in Salesforce, and stakeholder alignment happens over email. Instead of patching together point solutions, companies can now build an interconnected system of engagement that enables teams to move faster across the business and turn ideas into agentic action, all from the same conversation.\n\nKey Integration Features: [...] ## Expanding Agentic Context: Optimized Security,Deep Intelligence & Zero Copy Data\n\nThe backbone of this partnership lies in the system of context – the underlying architecture that connects the world’s most advanced AI models with enterprise-scale data. Salesforce and Google Cloud are making it easier for AI to use all your data right where it lives, so you don’t have to worry about security or costly data migration. From CRM records and customer signals to Google Workspace activity and BigQuery warehouses, these data connections allow agents to deliver results at scale that were previously out of reach.\n\nKey Advancements: [...] Chiesi Group selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences.\n\n## Chiesi Group Selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences to Transform Customer Engagement\n\nSalesforce Announces Salesforce Headless 360\n\n## Introducing Salesforce Headless 360. No Browser Required.\n\n### Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.\n\n## Share article\n\n## Just For You\n\nChiesi Group selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences.\n\n## Chiesi Group Selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences to Transform Customer Engagement\n\nSalesforce Announces Salesforce Headless 360\n\n## Introducing Salesforce Headless 360. No Browser Required.\n\n### Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.\n\n## Explore related content by topic\n\n## Related Articles",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agentic-commerce-discoverability.html",
      "title": "Agentic commerce discoverability: What if AI agents can't find you?",
      "content": "More\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nAmerica in motion\n\nExecutive leadership hub - What’s important to the C-suite?\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nTech Effect\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nShared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found [...] ## Rewriting discoverability\n\nIn traditional ecommerce, brands competed for search engine ranking and optimized the visual appeal of their shelf space. Customers manually browsed links, scanned pages, and narrowed options.\n\nBut AI-mediated experiences compress the process of discovery.\n\nAI systems synthesize answers. They reduce a wide range of possible products to a handful of recommended options. Brands that aren’t surfaced in the synthesized response are likely to be entirely excluded from consideration.\n\nThis shift introduces an urgent need for retailers to optimize for generative engines.\n\nGEO (generative engine optimization) isn’t simply SEO under a new name. It requires: [...] Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience (GECX) is designed to bring conversational buying directly to brand websites. GECX uses AI to guide customers through product discovery, answer questions in real time, and move them from intent to purchase. And it all happens seamlessly within the brand’s own digital environment. GECX:\n\nThis does not require re-platforming. It sits on top of a retailer’s existing commerce backbone while preserving merchant-of-record control.\n\nAgentic commerce, therefore, spans two environments simultaneously:\n\nBoth are critical. Designing for one without the other limits scalability.\n\n## The strategic imperative",

      "url": "https://docs.workato.com/en/mcp/registry/excel-mcp-server.html",
      "title": "MCP servers - {{ $frontmatter.connector_name }} | Workato Docs",
      "content": "## # Install the Excel MCP server\n\nComplete the following steps to install a prebuilt MCP server to your project:\n\n1\n\nSign in to your Workato account.\n\n2\n\nGo to AI Hub > MCP servers.\n\n3\n\nClick + Create MCP server.\n\n4\n\nGo to the Start with pre-built MCP Servers using your connected apps section and select the prebuilt MCP server you plan to use.\n\n5\n\nClick Use this server.\n\n6\n\nProvide a name for your MCP server in the Server name field.\n\n7\n\nUse the Location drop-down menu to select the project for the MCP server.\n\n8\n\nGo to the Connections section and connect to your app account.\n\n9\n\nSelect the connection type you plan to use for the MCP server template. [...] User's connection: MCP server tools perform actions based on the identity and permissions of the user who connects to the application. Users authenticate with their own credentials to execute the skill.\n Your connection: This option uses the connection established by the recipe builder and follows the same principles as normal app connections.\n\nSelect your connection typeSelect your connection type\n\nVERIFIED USER ACCESS AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENTS\n\nOnly app connections that use OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant are available for user's connection. Refer to Verified user access for more information.\n\n10\n\nComplete the app-specific connection setup steps in the following section.\n\n## # Excel connection setup\n\nView Excel connection setup steps [...] Add permissionsAdd permissions\n\n3\n\nAdd the required permissions as outlined in the Permissions required to connect section. Depending on your connection type, you must assign Application or Delegated permissions.\n\nAdd permissionsAdd permissions\n\n4\n\nClick Add permissions.\n\nAdmin consent is required for specific permissions. Refer to the Granting admin consent section to learn more.\n\n3\n\nGenerate a client secret\n\n1\n\nGo to Manage > Certificates & Secrets > Client secrets.\n\n2\n\nClick + New client secret.\n\n3\n\nProvide a Description for the client secret and specify an expiry date.\n\n4\n\nClick Add.\n\n5\n\nCopy and save the client secret Value—not the Secret ID—for use in Workato.\n\nCopy and save the client secret valueCopy and save the client secret value\n\n4",
      "score": 0.06309461,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://venturebeat.com/data/the-modern-data-stack-was-built-for-humans-asking-questions-google-just-rebuilt-its-for-agents-taking-action",
      "title": "The modern data stack was built for humans asking questions ...",
      "content": "The practical implication for data engineering teams is that the Knowledge Catalog scales to the full data estate, not just the curated subset that a small team of data stewards can maintain by hand. The catalog covers BigQuery, Spanner, AlloyDB and Cloud SQL natively, and federates with third-party catalogs including Collibra, Atlan and Datahub. Zero-copy federation extends semantic context from SaaS applications including SAP, Salesforce Data360, ServiceNow and Workday without requiring data movement.\n\n## Google's lakehouse goes cross cloud\n\nGoogle has had a data lakehouse called BigLake since 2022. Initially it was limited to just Google data, but in recent years has had some limited federation capabilities enabling enterprises to query data found in other locations. [...] Now, with AI agents increasingly expected to take actions directly on behalf of the business, Gutmans argued that data platforms must evolve into systems of action.\n\"We need to make sure that all of enterprise data can be activated with AI, that includes both structured and unstructured data,\" Gutmans said. \"We need to make sure that there's the right level of trust, which also means it's not just about getting access to the data, but really understanding the data.\" [...] The practical result is that BigQuery can query Iceberg tables sitting on Amazon S3 via Google's Cross-Cloud Interconnect, a dedicated private networking layer, with no egress fees and price-performance Google says is comparable to native AWS warehouses. All BigQuery AI functions run against that cross-cloud data without modification. Bidirectional federation in preview extends to Databricks Unity Catalog on S3, Snowflake Polaris and the AWS Glue Data Catalog using the open Iceberg REST Catalog standard.\n\n## From writing pipelines to describing outcomes\n\nThe Knowledge Catalog and cross-cloud lakehouse solve the data access and context problems. The third pillar addresses what happens when a data engineer actually sits down to build something with all of it.",
      "score": 0.060180597,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/ai-isnt-the-problem-your-data-might-be.html",
      "title": "AI isn't the problem; your data might be. - PwC",
      "content": "More\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nAmerica in motion\n\nExecutive leadership hub - What’s important to the C-suite?\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nTech Effect\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nShared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\n# AI isn’t the problem; your data might be\n\nBrad Foster [...] Companies aren't lacking data. They're lacking trusted data, and the ability to certify it at scale. In fact, PwC’s 2025 Digital Trust Insights survey found that data protection and trust rank as the number one priority for business executives globally. Industry leaders recognize that without trusted data, transformation efforts stall before they scale.\n\n## Enter our enterprise data agents\n\nOur enterprise data agents are a collection of intelligent, AI-enabled agents purpose-built to handle the entire spectrum of enterprise data functions—faster, smarter, and more consistently than manual processes ever could. [...] That's where many AI strategies hit a wall. Not because the models aren't capable, but because the data isn't ready. This is the challenge PwC and Google Cloud are helping address an ecosystem of enterprise data agents, a set of more than 30 agents that organize, govern, and activate enterprise data for AI at scale.\n\n## The data problem could be bigger than you think\n\nPicture this: Your team spends months manually combing through databases, cross-referencing records, and tracing the source of a single transaction—often alongside their day-to-day responsibilities. Eventually, someone declares the data “clean enough.”",
      "score": 0.058286972,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000122345339/ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment/",
      "title": "AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "### Qualifications\n\nRequired:\n\nPreferred:\n\nFD21\n\nFor positions in this location, we offer a base pay of $83,300 - $129,100, plus equity (when applicable), variable/incentive compensation and benefits. Sales positions generally offer a competitive On Target Earnings (OTE) incentive compensation structure. Please note that the base pay shown is a guideline, and individual total compensation will vary based on factors such as qualifications, skill level, competencies, and work location. We also offer health plans, including flexible spending accounts, a 401(k) Plan with company match, ESPP, matching donations, a flexible time away plan and family leave programs. Compensation is based on the geographic location in which the role is located and is subject to change based on work location. [...] ### Job Description\n\nAbout the Role\n\nYou'll own the end-to-end technical relationship with enterprise customers, translating complex business challenges into high-impact AI solutions built on the Moveworks Platform. This is a customer-facing, field-based role — you'll be the primary technical point of contact throughout the full implementation lifecycle, from solution design through launch. You are equally comfortable whiteboarding with a customer's IT team in the morning and collaborating with internal engineering and product teams in the afternoon.\n\nThis is not a back-office engineering role — you will be in front of customers regularly and are expected to travel up to 25% of the time.\n\nWhat You'll Own\n\nAbout You [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations",
