    {
      "url": "https://www.reuters.com/business/google-puts-ai-agents-heart-its-enterprise-money-making-push-2026-04-22/",
      "title": "Google puts AI agents at heart of its enterprise money-making push",
      "content": "Google Cloud, once seen as a laggard to rivals such as Amazon (AMZN.O), opens new tab and Microsoft (MSFT.O), opens new tab, has gained tractionwith enterprise customers, powered by massive ​bets on AI and years of heavy investment in data centers, custom chips, and networking gear.\n\nAt GE Appliances, that shift is already tangible. Marcia Brey, a senior executive and ‌Google ⁠customer, told Reuters that Google's suite of tools and the enterprise data already stored in Google Cloud allowed her logistics and distribution team to deploy AI faster compared to other products the company had tested.\n\n## AGENTS OVER CODING\n\nIn addition to traditional enterprise providers and other hyperscalers, a new class of competitors are quickly emerging in enterprise AI: model providers. [...] Google also announced a set of new governance and security features for AI agents. Agents are powerful digital assistants ​that can plan, decide, and act autonomously, a fast-growing field that has sparked worries over safety, reliability and oversight.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue\n\n“There’s definitely a strategic shift as the models ​become much more sophisticated,” Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told Reuters in an interview. The primary use case of Vertex AI recently shifted from “old-style machine learning” to a sudden explosion in users building their own custom AI agents, Kurian said.\n\nGoogle is seeking to outflank both its traditional cloud rivals and AI upstarts as pressure mounts to prove returns ​on massive generative AI spending. [...] # Google puts AI agents at heart of its enterprise money-making push | Reuters\n\nSkip to main content\n\nReport AdImage 1\n\nExclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv\n\n - Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai is deepening a push into enterprise ​software, signaling to investors at Google's annual cloud conference that AI agents — human-like digital assistants — are a lynchpin of its strategy ‌to monetize artificial intelligence.\n\nAt the three-day conference in Las Vegas that starts Wednesday, Pichai and key Google executives will seek to position the company's AI tools as production-ready infrastructure for enterprise customers who are emerging as the industry's most reliable revenue stream.",
      "score": 0.99253935,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/media/news/kpmg-debutes-ai-digital-assistant-with-workday-googlecloud.html",
      "title": "KPMG Debuts AI Digital Assistant to Enhance Month-End Financial ...",
      "content": "This effort between KPMG, Workday, and Google Cloud brings together deep finance and accounting expertise with leading HR and AI technologies to deliver a trusted, human-centered approach to AI in finance. Through this digital teammate model, KPMG and its alliance partners are enabling a more connected, intelligent financial reporting system that balances automation with accountability and professional judgement. [...] \"The future of finance depends on how well you can connect your people and your numbers,” said Kathleen Curry, global vice president, global channels at Workday, “By building directly on Workday’s platform, KPMG has created a tool that brings HR and financial data together to finally solve the headache of the manual month-end close. This partnership isn't just about new technology; it’s about giving finance teams their time back so they can stop chasing data and start advising the business.\" [...] The launch of the Financial Close Companion marks a significant milestone in KPMG's growing collaboration with Workday and Google Cloud, reflecting a shared vision for how AI agents can support the modern workforce. Through training and redesigned ways of working, finance professionals are beginning to work alongside intelligent agents—integrating AI into the financial reporting process while maintaining human judgement, accountability, and oversight. This approach drives innovation and solves critical business challenges, ultimately enhancing the value delivered to clients.\n\nHear more from KPMG at Google Next in Las Vegas, April 22 – 24. Learn more about KPMG and Workday and KPMG and Google Cloud.",
      "score": 0.9802808,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/ai-isnt-the-problem-your-data-might-be.html",
      "title": "AI isn't the problem; your data might be. - PwC",
      "content": "Companies aren't lacking data. They're lacking trusted data, and the ability to certify it at scale. In fact, PwC’s 2025 Digital Trust Insights survey found that data protection and trust rank as the number one priority for business executives globally. Industry leaders recognize that without trusted data, transformation efforts stall before they scale.\n\n## Enter our enterprise data agents\n\nOur enterprise data agents are a collection of intelligent, AI-enabled agents purpose-built to handle the entire spectrum of enterprise data functions—faster, smarter, and more consistently than manual processes ever could. [...] That's where many AI strategies hit a wall. Not because the models aren't capable, but because the data isn't ready. This is the challenge PwC and Google Cloud are helping address an ecosystem of enterprise data agents, a set of more than 30 agents that organize, govern, and activate enterprise data for AI at scale.\n\n## The data problem could be bigger than you think\n\nPicture this: Your team spends months manually combing through databases, cross-referencing records, and tracing the source of a single transaction—often alongside their day-to-day responsibilities. Eventually, someone declares the data “clean enough.” [...] Think of it less like a traditional data platform and more like a highly capable team of AI agents working around the clock. We provide agents dedicated to data quality and data lineage—so you always know where your data came from and how it reached you. Other agents handle governance, certification, and ontology, while tracking semantic or schema changes and their impact on code. There are even agents that allow your teams to literally talk to their data in plain language—no SQL required. Governance, policy enforcement, and auditability are embedded directly into execution workflows, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and responsible AI standards without slowing innovation.",
      "score": 0.9666631,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agentic-commerce-discoverability.html",
      "title": "Agentic commerce discoverability: What if AI agents can't find you?",
      "content": "Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience (GECX) is designed to bring conversational buying directly to brand websites. GECX uses AI to guide customers through product discovery, answer questions in real time, and move them from intent to purchase. And it all happens seamlessly within the brand’s own digital environment. GECX:\n\nThis does not require re-platforming. It sits on top of a retailer’s existing commerce backbone while preserving merchant-of-record control.\n\nAgentic commerce, therefore, spans two environments simultaneously:\n\nBoth are critical. Designing for one without the other limits scalability.\n\n## The strategic imperative [...] UCP is an open-source standard that creates a common language for commerce between AI agents and businesses. It standardizes product discovery, cart actions, identity linking, checkout, and order execution while preserving merchant-of-record ownership.\n\nThink of UCP as a layer of the transaction, allowing brands to:\n\nInstead of building one-off integrations for each surface, enterprises integrate once and connect across agents.\n\n## Conversational commerce on owned properties\n\nTransaction rails alone are not sufficient. The interaction layer should also evolve. [...] ## The strategic imperative\n\nIn an agent-driven world, being invisible to AI increasingly means being invisible to customers. Fragmented commerce stacks are a competitive liability. And interoperability is a growth enabler.\n\nThe organizations that move early can shape how agents interpret, recommend, and transact across their categories. Those that wait may find that demand is routed elsewhere.\n\nLearn more about how PwC and Google Cloud can help your organization make that early move to shape your agents for success.\n\n## Want to learn more? Join PwC at Google Cloud Next\n\n### April 22-24, 2026 | Las Vegas\n\n## {{filterContent.facetedTitle}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n## Contact us\n\nEric Shea",
      "score": 0.94815457,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/press/22april2026-bcg-google-cloud-partnership-gemini-enterprise-transformation",
      "title": "BCG and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to ...",
      "content": "Customer Service: Modernizing customer operations and engagement leveraging agentic AI, enabling multi-agent orchestration across service, autonomous billing support, AI-powered coaching, intelligent scheduling, retention workflows, and hyperpersonalization.\n Data: Establishing the data foundations required for AI transformation by enabling secure data access, clear data definitions, governance, performance monitoring, and real-time data orchestration across the enterprise. [...] Skip to Main\n\nLog error View Profile  Edit Profile Manage Subscriptions My Saved Content   Logout)\n\nSome Info\n\n# BCG and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to Accelerate Gemini Enterprise Transformation for Global Organizations\n\nPress Release  April 22, 2026\n\nCombining BCG’s Transformation Expertise with Google Cloud’s AI to Drive Enterprise-Wide Agent Adoption\n\nLAS VEGAS, Cloud Next '26—Boston Consulting Group (BCG) and Google Cloud today announced a pivotal expansion of their global strategic partnership, with a focus on moving enterprises past the AI “pilot phase” toward delivering agentic adoption at scale with measurable bottom-line impact. [...] As part of this collaboration, Google forward deployed engineers (FDEs) will partner with BCG on the most challenging customer use cases to prototype and deliver scaled, industry-specific AI solutions and agents that will transform workflows across customer engagements and entire value chains. Google DeepMind—a world-leading AI research lab—will also provide early access to its frontier models, including the Gemini family. BCG’s feedback will help further refine these models to ensure they’re equipped to deliver benefits to customers.\n\nBCG will continue to expand its technical and delivery capabilities around Google Cloud platforms and technologies to bring clients the latest innovations.",
      "score": 0.94699025,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/issues/technology/ai-performance.html",
      "title": "Decoding ROI from AI | PwC",
      "content": "Leaders who are seeing positive financial results from AI are much more likely to use it across their value chain and in more sophisticated ways. These outliers are about twice as likely as their peers to have scaled or embedded AI across major business functions, including strategy, marketing, supply chain, and support services like finance, IT, and HR.\n\nWhen companies put AI in more places and push it deeper into day-to-day execution, they see better results. The organisations that go after the hard problems, they put the more sophisticated deployments in place. Those organisations are seeing outsized return, both top line and profitability. [...] Our research tells us that leading companies are almost two times as likely to be operating at much higher sophistication levels with AI. The companies that are winning are not thinking about AI as a chatbot or going after robotic process automation. They’re fundamentally rewiring their processes, using data differently, and unleashing the power of autonomous agents to change the way they do business every day.\n\nWhat that means is they’re building in guard rails. They’re looking at how to use this throughout every aspect of their organisation. [...] Watch Pete Brown, PwC’s Global Workforce Leader, explain how AI can help unite human potential with tech power.\n\n### Peter Brown\n\nHeadshot of Peter Brown\n\nPlayback of this video is not currently available\n\nTo get the most out of AI, the workforce is absolutely essential. Leaders need to make sure that their people have the skills and the confidence to use AI in the right way and that those skills keep evolving as the technology evolves.",
      "score": 0.8031738,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/as-foundational-ai-improves-providers-can-thrive",
      "title": "As Foundational AI Improves, Providers Can Thrive | BCG",
      "content": "AI-Focused Actions for Professional Services Firms [...] Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives that question the status quo and spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting, technology and design, and corporate and digital ventures. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, fueled by the goal of helping our clients thrive and enabling them to make the world a better place.\n\n© Boston Consulting Group 2026. All rights reserved. [...] AI makers are improving their generative AI (GenAI) tools rapidly. Leveraging significant advances in large language models (LLMs) as well as users’ growing familiarity with such tools, these AI companies are tailoring their general-purpose technology to meet the needs of professional services firms.\n\nA year ago, BCG’s survey of GenAI tool usage in professional services organizations found that tools designed specifically for knowledge workers produced much higher-quality output than general AI tools. This year’s survey, however, turns the table—with general-purpose AI tools producing output on par with specialized tools on many dimensions. (See “About the Survey.”)\n\nAbout the Survey",
      "score": 0.41300356,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/ca/en/services/consulting/perspectives/redefining-operational-excellence.html",
      "title": "Redefining operational excellence in the age of intelligence - Deloitte",
      "content": "4. Build a technology-enabled workforce  \nAs experienced workers retire and new talent’s expectations shift, strengthening role-based capabilities is critical. Digitizing repetitive and administrative tasks helps free teams to focus on higher-value work, while capturing knowledge in structured formats allows AI tools to extend that expertise across the business. Over time, this will make mobile, connected work the standard. It’s a mindset shift for teams to make better decisions and improve continuously. [...] ##### 2. A changing operational workforce\n\nCanada’s ER&I industry is navigating a changing workforce; experienced employees are retiring, while new talent expects flexibility, mobility, and modern tools. Digital capabilities such as mobile workflows, integrated data access, remote expertise, and AI-assisted insights are becoming central to enabling a more informed workforce.\n\n##### 3. Proven technologies in high-impact areas [...] At the same time, the workforce is evolving. Experienced employees are retiring, and newer generations expect modern tools, remote enablement, and less reliance on repetitive manual work. Addressing talent gaps is becoming less about expanding teams and more about enabling people with better data, systems, and technology.  \n  \nOperational excellence has never been a destination; it's a discipline of continuous improvement. The bar keeps moving because competitors keep improving, and technology, data, and AI are accelerating at that pace. Now, the question is no longer whether these tools fit into operational excellence. It's whether leaders are using them intentionally to drive ongoing gains and enable teams to operate smarter and move faster.",
      "score": 0.18242551,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/au/en/why-kpmg/alliances/service-now.html",
      "title": "KPMG and ServiceNow",
      "content": "###### Turbocharge IT\n\nUnify data and workflows on a single system to enhance service and operational management, break down silos and improving collaboration.\n\n###### Unlock people power\n\nOptimise employee experience and enhance service delivery outcomes by incorporating industry insights with emerging technologies, such as generative AI.\n\n###### Integrate risk, inspire confidence\n\nAlign risk strategies and strengthen defences with an integrated risk management suite to proactively identify, assess, monitor and report on risk and compliance exposure.\n\n###### Redefine the customer experience\n\nFoster loyalty and empower teams to focus on high-value interactions by streamlining processes and automating service operations, generating enduring value in every customer engagement.\n\n## Awards [...] KPMG logo\nKPMG logo\n\nMore about Tech, data and AI\n\nMore about retail industry\n\nFinancial Reporting Centre\n\nIndustry & Sector perspectives\n\nWhy KPMG?\n\nAbout us\n\nGraduate & Student Careers\n\nFind your fit\n\nSubmit RFP\n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\nAlliance\n\n# KPMG and ServiceNow\n\n## More productive, more efficient\n\nKPMG and ServiceNow assist organisations in modernising processes across front, middle and back offices. Together, we utilise AI and low code to streamline manual tasks through automated, intuitive systems, aiming to enhance productivity and efficiency throughout your enterprise.\n\n## Make work, work\n\nConsolidate, automate and modernise your service-management processes with KPMG and ServiceNow.\n\n###### Turbocharge IT [...] Olivia Willee\n\n## Stephen Oates\n\nAlliance Director, ServiceNow at KPMG Australia, Stephen has more than 25 years of experience working in professional services organisations. He is responsible for partnering, solutioning, sales and marketing in the technology sector. With a balance of business and technical acumen, complemented by expertise in creating and implementing growth strategies, Stephen focuses on leveraging emerging technologies and digital strategies to assist organisations address challenges and seize new opportunities for growth.\n\nStephen Oates\n\n## Related insights\n\n### Something went wrong\n\n###### Oops!! Something went wrong, please try again\n\n## Frequently asked questions\n\n### What solutions does KPMG offer through its ServiceNow alliance?",

    {
      "url": "https://www.ey.com/en_ch/insights/workforce/how-global-mobility-can-build-a-strategic-ai-foundation",
      "title": "How global mobility can build a strategic AI foundation | EY - Switzerland",
      "content": "In brief\n\nFrom every angle, generative AI (GenAI) and agentic tools are disrupting how organizations consider operations, strategy and investment. Despite a rapid increase in AI adoption levels, most organizations are still seeking a “killer app” for functions that have high strategic importance like mobility, global payroll and human resources (HR) more generally. Many organizations are stuck experimenting with isolated tools, waiting for near-term benefits, while also failing to lay the foundations for long-term impact. Other organizations are filling their operations with semi-autonomous AI agents, only to find that these agents simply turn broken processes into ones that fail faster. [...] How EY can help\n\nEY Mobility Pathway: global workforce management tool\n\nEY Mobility Pathway is a scalable, integrated solution that delivers the mobile talent lifecycle on a single, end-to-end platform. Learn how.\n\nBecause mobility sits at the center of tax, immigration, payroll and employee experience, even small agentic tools can deliver outsized returns. They help teams interpret the signals already hidden in their own processes, clarifying where people struggle, where operations stall and where expectations fall short. The heavy data lift and analysis can be accelerated with AI agents to help teams move faster in their interpretations. [...] Data activation: The user uploads mobility survey data, allowing the agent to apply its instructions directly.\n\nTypical outputs: The agent returns a brief, board‑level view organized into what’s working, what can be improved and what’s not working. Illustrative strengths may include smoother onboarding experiences or effective local HR responsiveness; improvement areas may surface around cross‑regional communication or family support; and challenge areas may highlight pain points such as housing or inconsistent post‑relocation follow‑up.",
      "score": 0.5230555,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.salesforce.com/en/jobs/jr339538/solution-engineer-ai-data-integration-us-federal/",
      "title": "Solution Engineer - AI & Data Integration - US Federal",
      "content": "For roles in San Francisco and Los Angeles: Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring, Salesforce will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.\n\n## Unleash Your Potential\n\nWhen you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. [...] A Unified Mission: Work in a specialized team that owns the entire data-to-AI lifecycle.\n Outcome-First Leadership: Opportunities to lead high-impact discovery sessions that start with agency mission outcomes (e.g. citizen services, public safety, legacy modernization) rather than product features.\n Strategic Growth: Support from leaders committed to your mastery of the “Data 360 + Agentic AI” vision, including integration, analytics, unified data management, and intelligent agentic capabilities.\n Innovation at Scale: Work for the #1 AI CRM company, building the trusted data foundations and agentic architectures required for the next generation of autonomous AI agents that accelerate defense outcomes.\n\nQualifications you’ll need to be successful: [...] Agentic AI Experience: Demonstrated experience (2+ years is highly valued) with agentic AI solutions, including prior hands-on work designing or deploying intelligent agents, LLM integration strategies, orchestration frameworks, prompt engineering, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns.\n Strategic Communication: Excellent ability to reframe technical challenges into mission and business value for C-level government executives and technical stakeholders alike.\n AI Readiness: Deep understanding of “Trusted Context Foundations” for AI, including data grounding, metadata intelligence, and how unified data enables reliable agentic workflows.",
      "score": 0.4221955,

    {
      "url": "https://careers.salesforce.com/en/jobs/jr331252/forward-deployed-engineer-multiple-levels-based-in-south-korea/",
      "title": "Forward Deployed Engineer  (Multiple Levels) <<based in South Korea>>, Korea, Republic of - Seoul | Salesforce Careers",
      "content": "## Unleash Your Potential\n\nWhen you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.\n\n### At Salesforce, we strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates.\n\nIf you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodation Request Form. [...] Engineer Bespoke Agentic AI Solutions: Work directly with customers to design and build custom intelligent agents using the Agentforce platform, ensuring they precisely meet unique business requirements and integrate seamlessly with existing customer ecosystems.\n\nData Configuration & Integration : Own the entire data lifecycle, from strategic design of data models to hands-on building of efficient processing pipelines and establishing seamless integration strategies. You'll ensure data is always ready, optimized, and secure for our advanced AI applications, often navigating complex enterprise data environments. [...] ### Your Impact\n\nDrive Tangible Outcomes through Hands-On Implementation: Be responsible for the end-to-end technical delivery of complex AI solutions, personally writing critical code, configuring systems, and troubleshooting issues, while also guiding and reviewing the work of junior team members to ensure successful deployment and measurable business impact.\n\nBuild Transformative AI Solutions: Take primary ownership of designing, developing, and personally implementing high-quality, scalable production systems. A significant part of this will involve architecting and deploying complex AI and Large Language Model (LLM) based intelligent agents and automated workflows, all built on top of the Salesforce platform.",
      "score": 0.3753177,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ey.com/en_nl/insights/ai/solving-the-workforce-challenge-in-the-age-of-agentic-ai",
      "title": "Solving the workforce challenge in the age of agentic AI | EY - Netherlands",
      "content": "Additionally, innovative tools, such as digital twins, augmented and virtual reality, and connected worker applications, can then facilitate the transfer of tacit knowledge. They can act as the technology process backbone that will guide work execution, regardless of whether the work is being done by agents or people. These technologies enable real-time sharing of expertise and create immersive learning experiences that help bridge the knowledge gap.\n\nAgentic AI adds a disruptive element to workforce training in that this tacit knowledge can form the basis of automated processes, not only reducing reliance on individual expertise but also upending the norms of how work is completed. And the implications may cut against the grain of what you’d expect from your workforce. [...] Today, an operator on a production line would look at a dashboard to decide about rates of throughput, keeping variables such as speed and quality control in mind. Yet if AI agents are autonomously assessing the variables through decision intelligence, the skill set for an operator changes, and a 12-step process today eventually becomes four steps in the future. That decision intelligence is often based on tacit knowledge, which manufacturers struggle to access, standardize and operationalize — but in the past 12 months, the recent AI explosion has brought many promising new solutions to the market that manufacturers should explore.\n\n#### What is the difference between explicit and tacit knowledge? [...] Yet the managers in the middle, who comfortably fulfill a niche in today’s ways of working, often feel trepidation — because they, too, need to reskill, and most companies’ workforce modernization platforms aren’t addressing the leadership level. For instance, a big leadership characteristic is motivating people to do their best. What does it mean when rallying the troops to win the day includes using AI agendas that will be fulfilling many tasks on the critical path? Are these managers equipped to interpret data to validate agent decision-making, or is it in an enterprise resource planning (ERP) system they can’t access or don’t know how to use? How do they orchestrate a team of people plus agents?",
      "score": 0.3706587,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "## Unleash Your Potential\n\nWhen you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.\n\n### At Salesforce, we strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates.\n\nIf you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodation Request Form. [...] This is a high impact role at the intersection of technology, policy, and community outcomes. You will partner with government organisations to modernise service delivery, improve citizen experiences, and drive large scale digital transformation through the next generation of Salesforce technology.\n\nAs a trusted advisor, you will engage senior stakeholders across departments, agencies, and statutory bodies, helping them navigate complex challenges and unlock the value of data, AI, automation, and workflow at scale.\n\n### Your Impact\n\nYou will own and drive strategic customer engagements across the full sales lifecycle, from shaping opportunity through to deal closure and long term account growth. [...] Build and maintain senior relationships across business and technology leaders, acting as a trusted advisor to influence decision making\n\nNavigate government procurement processes, compliance requirements, and funding models with confidence\n\nCollaborate with Solution Engineers, Industry Advisors, Customer Success, and partners to co create scalable, outcome focused solutions\n\nDevelop a deep understanding of sector trends, including the role of AI, data, and automation in public service delivery\n\nRepresent Salesforce across industry forums, roundtables, and executive engagements to strengthen our presence in the public sector ecosystem\n\nContribute to account planning and long term growth strategies across key government portfolios\n\n### About You",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/about/press-room/deloitte-launches-google-cloud-agentic-transformation-practice.html",
      "title": "Deloitte Launches Google Cloud Agentic Transformation Practice",
      "content": "Deloitte has been at the forefront of AI innovation for over a decade, expanding its offerings through a Generative AI practice. With its Deloitte Ascend™ delivery platform, Deloitte technologists, developers and engineers build and deliver new AI solutions, agents and tools for its clients and people. With deep industry knowledge and specialized experience across domains, Deloitte integrates AI following its Trustworthy AI™ framework to manage industry and sector-specific risks. Additionally, Deloitte enhances AI fluency by investing in global learning and training through the Deloitte AI Academy™ .\n\nContact\n\nAlex Frole  \nPublic Relations  \nDeloitte Services LP  \n+1 445 230 9206  \nafrole@deloitte.com\n\nAbout Deloitte [...] Building on Deloitte’s AI experience across Fortune 500 clients and its own business, this expanded collaboration is designed to deliver transformation services for organizations looking to deploy agentic AI securely and at scale. The new practice will deploy Deloitte’s rapidly expanding suite of Google AI and agentic assets and use Deloitte Ascend™, its AI-infused services and delivery platform. Services span strategy and process redesign through implementation, governance and adoption, with an industry-focus on retail, health care, financial services and government and public services. [...] Deloitte continues to bring Gemini Enterprise into organizations and has developed a growing library of over 1000 pre-built, industry-specific AI agents intended to accelerate AI adoption across heart-of-the-business workflows. To extend these agents across providers, Deloitte is also connecting with third-party platforms through Google’s Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, enabling agentic capabilities across high-value, end-to-end workflows.",
      "score": 0.34767053,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.ey.com/en_gr/insights/banking-capital-markets/five-hallmarks-of-effective-ai-strategies-in-banking",
      "title": "Five hallmarks of effective AI strategies in banking | EY - Greece",
      "content": "EY and other research offer reasons for optimism. The EY US Agentic AI Workforce Survey (via EY.com US) found that 84% of desk-based employees are enthusiastic about working with AI agents. However, 56% worry about job security, presenting a paradox that banking leaders will have to address.\n\n#### 5. Futuristic and designed for the long term [...] However, excessive centralization may inhibit the development of innovative solutions and discourage input from front-line staff. AI strategies should clarify where local experimentation is appropriate and outline the non-negotiable policies (e.g., data privacy and transparency in model design) that are best administered centrally. Enterprise-level AI agent libraries (e.g., reusable assets for fraud detection, forecasting and other tasks) can streamline the development of tailored AI applications, with consistent logic across use cases avoiding the sprawl of decentralized pilots. AI agents can pull from integrated finance, tax and risk data and automate regulatory filings, while ensuring data lineage and auditability. They can also automate and streamline stress testing, liquidity risk [...] A governance watchtower approach — including automated controls testing, human oversight, standards for explainability and continuous model validation — has emerged as a leading practice. AI agents can pull from integrated finance, tax and risk data and automate regulatory filings, while supporting data lineage and auditability. “A strong AI strategy means clear ownership of data security, vendor management and governance, so AI is built and run the same way across the bank,” said Gupta. “Standardization is the way to scale responsibly.”",
      "score": 0.25546014,
      "raw_content": null

      "content": "###### Turbocharge IT\n\nUnify data and workflows on a single system to enhance service and operational management, break down silos and improving collaboration.\n\n###### Unlock people power\n\nOptimise employee experience and enhance service delivery outcomes by incorporating industry insights with emerging technologies, such as generative AI.\n\n###### Integrate risk, inspire confidence\n\nAlign risk strategies and strengthen defences with an integrated risk management suite to proactively identify, assess, monitor and report on risk and compliance exposure.\n\n###### Redefine the customer experience\n\nFoster loyalty and empower teams to focus on high-value interactions by streamlining processes and automating service operations, generating enduring value in every customer engagement.\n\n## Awards [...] ### Technology solutions\n\nFrom IT service management to cyber security operations, strategic portfolio management and IT asset management, we help to transform business functions and drive operational efficiency.\n\n### Employee solutions\n\nKPMG collaborates with ServiceNow to deliver services that enhance the employee experience,elevate efficiency and service delivery, connect teams and streamline workflows. Together, we utilise AI and low code to streamline manual tasks through automated, intuitive systems, aiming to enhance productivity and efficiency throughout your enterprise.\n\n## Make work, work\n\nConsolidate, automate and modernise your service-management processes with KPMG and ServiceNow.\n\n###### Turbocharge IT",

      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft365copilotblog/microsoft-365-copilot-readiness-and-resiliency-with-sharepoint-and-m365-backupar/4513048",
      "title": "Microsoft 365 Copilot readiness and resiliency with SharePoint and ...",
      "content": "Restricted Content Discovery (RCD) policy is very powerful control to prevent content from being discovered in Microsoft 365 Copilot as well as in Declarative agents. Site owners/admins can now independently manage RCD (Restricted Content Discovery) policy on a site, by providing appropriate justification on why the policy is being applied or updated.\n\n### 2. SharePoint Admin Agent\n\nIntroducing the SharePoint Admin Agent, Microsoft’s first-party AI assistant for managing your rapidly growing digital estate across Microsoft 365.   \n  \nAs the content backbone of Microsoft 365 – powering Teams, OneDrive, Loop, Copilot, and more - SharePoint sits at the center of content governance, spanning permissions, lifecycle, resilience, and relevance across users, apps, and now AI agents. [...] ### 3. Agent governance in SharePoint, Teams, and OneDrive\n\nMicrosoft Agent 365 is a unified control-plane for agents that enables you to observe, govern and secure agents across your organization – including agents built with Microsoft AI platform and from other partners. Read more.\n\nSpecifically, Agent Registry and Agent Map capabilities provides you a breadth view of agents in your organization. To complement that breadth view, we are introducing depth view of agents in the lens of Microsoft 365 resources, specifically SharePoint sites, Teams, and OneDrives. Thrilled to introduce Agent Access Insights and Heatmap.\n\n#### Agent Access Insights for SharePoint and OneDrive – Generally Available [...] ## Latest Enhancements in Microsoft 365 Content Governance\n\nAs AI becomes embedded in everyday work, governance shifts from being a back‑office function to a strategic enabler. Organizations that succeed with Copilot and agents aren’t just moving fast (70% of early Copilot users said they were more productive) – they are prepared, resilient, and deliberate in balancing AI-driven innovation with security and relevance. AI-first modern governance delivers real value across three dimensions: building readiness before AI is turned on, ensuring resilience when the unexpected happens, and driving relevance so people and AI can focus on what matters. Today, we are excited to announce many content governance innovations that moves organizations forward on their frontier transformation.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/design-your-company-for-ai-not-ai-for-your-company",
      "title": "Design Your Company for AI, Not AI for Your Company | BCG",
      "content": "Redefining the Operating Model and Organizational Structure. The organization redesigned its entire operating model. Work is now organized around small, cross-functional teams that integrate human talent, AI agents, data, and technology to deliver a specific outcome. These teams own employee and client processes end to end, balancing automation, human expertise, and AI-driven insight. Within this model, AI agents execute core tasks across HR and operations, including onboarding, policy support, travel, and learning. Employees are being reskilled to take on new roles—such as AI product owners, model governance managers, and talent data strategists—to ensure that AI capabilities are not layered on, but structurally embedded in the organization. [...] specialized agents, all connected through shared data and governance. Digital employee twins enable more precise talent matching, targeted reskilling, and workforce planning. [...] Learn More\n\nAI Agents\n\nArticle\n\nJanuary 20, 2026\n\nScaling AI Requires New Processes, Not Just New Tools\n\nCompanies deploying AI agents at scale need to redesign their end-to-end processes if they want to achieve the promised step changes in productivity and value creation.\n\nLearn More\n\nArtificial Intelligence\n\nArticle\n\nFebruary 4, 2026\n\nAI Transformation Is a Workforce Transformation\n\nCompanies that are realizing the most value from AI also have the most ambitious upskilling programs—and put the resources in place to support them.\n\nLearn More\n\nABOUT BOSTON CONSULTING GROUP",
      "score": 0.5709301,

    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/transforming-into-an-ai-first-payments-company",
      "title": "Transforming Into An AI-First Payments Company | BCG",
      "content": "Fund and govern for success. Back each bet with real capital, top talent, and end-to-end ownership of data, models, and operations. Stand up an empowered project management office to drive cadence, adoption, and accountability. Link AI investment explicitly to enterprise value, such as earnings or TSR, so that everyone in the organization understands what the spend is for and what it is producing.\n\nTransform culture and the operating model. Redesign the organization so that AI is embedded in core decisionmaking, not bolted on. Payments stacks need to expose secure, governable interfaces so agents can discover, negotiate, and execute transactions. Hardwire KPIs to outcomes. Change how work gets done. [...] The opportunity is enormous—and it is available right now. The leaders who move decisively to build AI-native products, accelerate their development, and reshape their organizations will define the next era of payments.\n\nDownload the slide deck\n\n## Related Content\n\nFinancial Institutions\n\nArticle\n\nMay 21, 2025\n\nFor Banks, the AI Reckoning Is Here\n\nBanks must move beyond pilots to redefine strategy, technology, and governance—or risk losing control of the financial landscape to faster movers.\n\nLearn More\n\nNovember 5, 2025\n\nFrom Branches to Bots: Will AI Agents Transform Retail Banking?\n\nDespite subdued progress, the use of AI, especially agents, will pick up—because banks’ needs and AI’s benefits are too compelling to ignore.\n\nLearn More\n\nThe AI-First Life Insurance Company | Hero [...] Fundamentally overhauling the operating model. The shift is from a model in which people drive core processes with AI as a supplement to one where AI agents drive core processes with human oversight. Dispute resolution, fraud orchestration, routing, pricing—these are increasingly run by agents in real time, with humans setting guardrails and handling exceptions. The financial impact speaks for itself. We see first movers unlocking 20% to 30% operating profit expansion through revenue growth, risk reduction, and cost takeout. Over time, competitive dynamics will compress some of that advantage as AI-first approaches become a baseline requirement, but early movers can expect to hold a durable 10% to15% edge.\n\nRead More\n\nExecutive Perspectives Ambient Hero",
      "score": 0.1409621,

      "title": "BCG and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to ...",
      "content": "Customer Service: Modernizing customer operations and engagement leveraging agentic AI, enabling multi-agent orchestration across service, autonomous billing support, AI-powered coaching, intelligent scheduling, retention workflows, and hyperpersonalization.\n Data: Establishing the data foundations required for AI transformation by enabling secure data access, clear data definitions, governance, performance monitoring, and real-time data orchestration across the enterprise. [...] As part of this collaboration, Google forward deployed engineers (FDEs) will partner with BCG on the most challenging customer use cases to prototype and deliver scaled, industry-specific AI solutions and agents that will transform workflows across customer engagements and entire value chains. Google DeepMind—a world-leading AI research lab—will also provide early access to its frontier models, including the Gemini family. BCG’s feedback will help further refine these models to ensure they’re equipped to deliver benefits to customers.\n\nBCG will continue to expand its technical and delivery capabilities around Google Cloud platforms and technologies to bring clients the latest innovations. [...] Skip to Main\n\nLog error View Profile  Edit Profile Manage Subscriptions My Saved Content   Logout)\n\nSome Info\n\n# BCG and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to Accelerate Gemini Enterprise Transformation for Global Organizations\n\nPress Release  April 22, 2026\n\nCombining BCG’s Transformation Expertise with Google Cloud’s AI to Drive Enterprise-Wide Agent Adoption\n\nLAS VEGAS, Cloud Next '26—Boston Consulting Group (BCG) and Google Cloud today announced a pivotal expansion of their global strategic partnership, with a focus on moving enterprises past the AI “pilot phase” toward delivering agentic adoption at scale with measurable bottom-line impact.",

      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/press/23april2026-bcg-revenue-22nd-consecutive-year-growth",
      "title": "BCG Reports $14.4 Billion in Revenue, Marking 22nd Consecutive ...",
      "content": "“Our people are at the center of our performance,” Schweizer said. “Their judgment, empathy, and ability to combine applied AI with strategic clarity are what enable us to deliver lasting impact for clients.”\n\nServing Clients in an Applied AI World\n\nAs AI reshapes industries, BCG is embedding AI directly into how it designs and delivers client solutions. Over the past year, the firm has reimagined its offerings by integrating AI into core consulting workflows—embedding proprietary knowledge, data, and proven delivery approaches into reusable, human-led agentic processes that accelerate impact. [...] Building Up BCG’s Team to Deliver Transformational Results\n\nBCG continued to expand its global talent base in 2025, growing its workforce to 33,500 employees. The firm added AI engineers, data scientists, IT architects, and deep-industry specialists, while continuing to develop its consulting teams to lead complex end-to-end transformations.\n\nAI upskilling efforts have further accelerated across the firm, equipping teams to combine human judgment with AI-powered tools. BCG employees now use AI tools daily, with nearly 4,000 BCG employees actively developing and scaling AI workflows through advanced coding and automation. LinkedIn’s AI Talent Maturity Index places BCG’s workforce as leaders in the industry. [...] “Our growth reflects the ambition of our clients and the dedication of our teams,” said Christoph Schweizer, BCG’s CEO. “We partner with leaders at defining moments to navigate uncertainty, embrace AI, and turn strategy into sustained advantage. As AI reshapes business, it is also reshaping how we operate as a firm. AI is now woven into every offering, client relationship, and daily case team experience to drive transformation and multiply impact for our clients.”\n\nBuilding Up BCG’s Team to Deliver Transformational Results",

      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/as-foundational-ai-improves-providers-can-thrive",
      "title": "As Foundational AI Improves, Providers Can Thrive | BCG",
      "content": "To make the most of GenAI tools, professional services firms should:  \n  \nTreat data quality as foundational.  \nThis includes building robust pipelines for any first-party data that will be used in agentic processes, while addressing regulatory and privacy concerns. For third-party data, firms should be deliberate about which data sources power each task or workflow, rather than falling back on default options.  \n  \nDo more than simply provide access to AI tools. [...] AI-Focused Actions for Professional Services Firms [...] AI features in specialized tools improve satisfaction and productivity.  \nAcross professional services, employees who leverage the AI features in specialized tools have much higher customer advocacy scores than those who do not. (See Exhibit 2.) This finding suggests that the AI features integrated into information services offerings improve satisfaction and allow users to get more out of the products.Providers’ continued investment in specialized AI products and features will be critical for user activation and retention—and for long-term relevance.\n\nUsers accessing AI features from information services providers have higher customer advocacy scores\n\nWeekly Insights Subscription\n\n### Stay ahead with BCG insights on technology, media, and telecommunications",

      "content": "As a senior member of the team, you will own end-to-end delivery of complex implementations, influence architectural decisions, mentor engineers, and act as a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders.       "title": "How global mobility can build a strategic AI foundation | EY",
      "content": "In brief\n\nFrom every angle, generative AI (GenAI) and agentic tools are disrupting how organizations consider operations, strategy and investment. Despite a rapid increase in AI adoption levels, most organizations are still seeking a “killer app” for functions that have high strategic importance like mobility, global payroll and human resources (HR) more generally. Many organizations are stuck experimenting with isolated tools, waiting for near-term benefits, while also failing to lay the foundations for long-term impact. Other organizations are filling their operations with semi-autonomous AI agents, only to find that these agents simply turn broken processes into ones that fail faster. [...] How EY can help\n\nPeople Advisory Services Tax\n\nDiscover EY’s integrated workforce mobility and payroll management solutions. Expert global mobility services, tax compliance, and social security coordination.\n\n#### Personalized experiences in the short term\n\nOne of the more straightforward and high-visibility uses of agents in the near-term is for personalization of common tasks. EY data show AI use today is still concentrated in certain key areas, including customer or user experience, with 31% using AI to access customer support, and in personal applications like content translation (29%). [...] How EY can help\n\nEY Mobility Pathway: global workforce management tool\n\nEY Mobility Pathway is a scalable, integrated solution that delivers the mobile talent lifecycle on a single, end-to-end platform. Learn how.\n\nBecause mobility sits at the center of tax, immigration, payroll and employee experience, even small agentic tools can deliver outsized returns. They help teams interpret the signals already hidden in their own processes, clarifying where people struggle, where operations stall and where expectations fall short.       "url": "https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/bg-p/technology-blog-sap",
      "title": "Technology Blog Posts by SAP | SAP Community",
      "content": "GitHub Copilot や Cursor に代表されるAIコーディングアシスタントが普及する中、タスクを自律的に計画・実行できるエージェント型AIツールへと進化が進んでいます。",
      "score": 0.18821983,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/as-foundational-ai-improves-providers-can-thrive",
      "title": "As Foundational AI Improves, Providers Can Thrive | BCG",
      "content": "## Adoption and Perceived Value of AI Are Increasing\n\nThe survey also revealed some important findings about how usage of general-purpose and specialized AI tools has evolved over the last year.\n\nAdoption rates.   \nDeployment of general-purpose tools in the professional workplace—such as OpenAI’s ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Claude by Anthropic, and Google’s portfolio of AI solutions—is clearly growing. While ChatGPT remains the most widely used and liked, people are now branching out and leveraging multiple tools. In 2025, professionals regularly used an average of three different tools, versus just over two in 2024. (See Exhibit 3.) [...] AI features in specialized tools improve satisfaction and productivity.  \nAcross professional services, employees who leverage the AI features in specialized tools have much higher customer advocacy scores than those who do not. (See Exhibit 2.) This finding suggests that the AI features integrated into information services offerings improve satisfaction and allow users to get more out of the products.Providers’ continued investment in specialized AI products and features will be critical for user activation and retention—and for long-term relevance.\n\nUsers accessing AI features from information services providers have higher customer advocacy scores\n\nWeekly Insights Subscription\n\n### Stay ahead with BCG insights on technology, media, and telecommunications [...] Do more than simply provide access to AI tools.  \nRealizing firmwide benefits from AI requires consistency across tools, processes, and workflows for all users. Most companies have a small number of power users who tap into AI for more than 20% of their tasks (14% of respondents in our survey) that have made strides in automating their individual workflows. Most workers use AI less systematically, and a substantial group may resist using AI at all.  \n  \nPursue productivity gains now even as tools continue to evolve.",
      "score": 0.16758648,
      "raw_content": null
    },

{
  "query": "skills graph agentic AI HR",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/partner-built-agents-available-in-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Partner-built agents available in Gemini Enterprise | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "Sana from Workday: Sana Self-Service Agent instantly finds and summarizes information from Workday and other knowledge sources, providing personalized answers and assistance with tasks. With over 300 skills across areas like pay, time, and absence, the agent handles everyday HR and finance tasks globally, reducing support tickets and allowing teams to focus on high-value work.\n\nSaviynt: Identity Security for AI enables continuous discovery and inventory of agents, identifying their functions, access, and risks. It treats agents as first-class identities with defined purposes and accountable owners. This blend of posture visibility and enforced accountability converts opaque automation into governable, enterprise-ready identities. [...] Typeface: Typeface Agent acts as a seamless bridge to creative campaigns. It enables instant access to Typeface teams, brand assets, and campaigns via natural language from Gemini Enterprise. Users can trigger high-quality, brand-aligned content creation workflows, streamlining the journey from ideation to production.\n\nUKG: Agentic People Assist — HR Service Delivery elevates the agentic capabilities of the UKG Workforce Operating Platform by empowering employees and managers with knowledge and connected agents to turn intent into action. It orchestrates workflows across time, pay, and HR to deliver real-time, personalized, and measurable outcomes. [...] Ascendo: Ascendo AI Agent helps critical infrastructure teams automate workflows, improve diagnostics, and reduce field escalations. By ingesting logs, telemetry, and service manuals, it recommends optimal actions to support faster, accurate decision-making in complex service environments.\n\nAtlassian: Rovo is an AI teammate seamlessly integrated across Jira, Confluence, and your tool stack. Built on Atlassian’s Teamwork Graph, it surfaces knowledge, automates tasks, and takes intelligent actions to help teams pull context, move faster and stay aligned.",
      "score": 0.38685668,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://cloud.google.com/transform/shift-system-of-action-architecting-the-agentic-data-cloud-AI",
      "title": "Architecting the Agentic Data Cloud | Google Cloud Blog",
      "content": "“To deliver AI that actually works across HR, payroll, and workforce operations, you need a consistent, real-time data layer. With the power of Google’s Agentic Data Cloud, People Fabric is the backbone of UKG’s Workforce Operating Platform — turning fragmented systems into a single source of truth that powers intelligent, agent-driven experiences.”  \nRadhi Chagarlamudi, Group Vice President, Product Engineering, UKG \n\n### The future is active\n\nWhen you build on Google Cloud, you benefit from the same technology that runs Google. [...] Active, multimodel engines: We reengineered the core engines themselves to be the core of the operation. By bringing graph, vector, search, and multimodal data processing natively into the database and powering them with integrated reasoning engines, we’ve created a platform that doesn't just store data, it perceives and reasons. With real-time AI functions, our engines process data as it arrives and trigger autonomous actions the moment your business changes.\n\n### The Agentic Data Cloud in production\n\nThe shift to this new architecture is already underway. It is clear our own customers are not just storing data, but activating it with AI at massive scale. [...] “To secure the AI era, we needed a foundation that could think as fast as the threats evolve. Google's Agentic Data Cloud acts as the core reasoning engine for our security products. Leveraging Spanner’s unified graph and non-graph capabilities, we can now provide our customers with a seamless, highly scalable identity posture that enables AI agents to perceive and act on security gaps in real time.”  \nSreejith Rajkumar, Director of Engineering, Palo Alto Networks",
      "score": 0.3370513,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

      "title": "The New Look of Success: Navigating the 2026 Experience Updates",
      "content": "Comment\n\nCopy Link Bluesky Linkedin\n\nRelated Content\n\n SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026: The Skills-Based Organisation Finally Grows Up in Human Capital Management Blog Posts by Members\n First Half 2026 Release: Agentic AI in SAP SuccessFactors Learning in Human Capital Management Blog Posts by SAP\n SuccessFactors Release 1H 2026, Important notes on EC, Platform, Time Management, my view in Human Capital Management Blog Posts by Members\n From Tracking to Acting: Strategic Insights from the SAP SuccessFactors Learning 1H 2026 Release in Human Capital Management Blog Posts by Members\n\nTop liked authors\n\n| User | Count |\n --- |\n|  | 46 |\n|  | 43 |\n|  | 19 |\n|  | 17 |\n|  | 14 |\n|  | 13 |\n|  | 11 |\n|  | 9 |\n|  | 7 |\n|  | 5 |\n\nView all [...] While change can feel like a lot of moving parts, these updates are a large step towards a modern, intuitive HR ecosystem.     {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/de/events/knowledge/happening.html",
      "title": "What's Happening at Knowledge - Knowledge 2026 - ServiceNow",
      "content": "Vorgestellte Demos\n\n Bessere Experiences bieten\n\n  ### Bessere Experiences bieten\n\n  Erfahren Sie, wie Sie mit generativer KI Kunden und Mitarbeitern Selfservice-Optionen für Anfragen bereitstellen können. (3:17)\n Probleme schneller lösen\n\n  ### Probleme schneller lösen\n\n  Erfahren Sie, wie Ihr Unternehmen manuelle Arbeit reduzieren und Service Desk-Mitarbeiter dabei unterstützen kann, Fälle schneller zu lösen. (4:08)\n Workflows erstellen und automatisieren\n\n  ### Workflows erstellen und automatisieren\n\n  Erfahren Sie, wie Sie die Workflow-Erstellung und die Entwicklung von anwenderdefiniertem Code vereinfachen können. (4:30)\n\nLösungen\n\nIT\n\nProfitieren Sie von vorausschauender Bedarfsermittlung mit effizienter IT und digitalem Betrieb.\n\nLösung erkunden\n\nZugehörige Produkte [...] ### Wir stellen vor: Autonomous Workforce\n\nAutonomous Workforce kann mehr als nur isolierte Aufgaben erledigen. Diesen KI-Experten werden Rollen zugewiesen, komplett mit Geschäftskontext und Berechtigungen, sodass sie komplexe Workflows von A bis Z übernehmen können.\n\nMehr erfahren\n\nEine Plattform, die für alles bereit ist\n\nKI, Daten und Workflows arbeiten auf einer Plattform zusammen. Nur mit ServiceNow funktioniert das in großem Umfang so einfach.\n\nAI Platform erkunden\n\n ### KI\n\n  Nutzen Sie KI in Ihrem gesamten Unternehmen mit unserer zentralen, intelligenten Plattform.\n ### Daten\n\n  Unterstützen Sie all Ihre Workflows sowie KI und Analytics mit Echtzeitzugriff auf Daten aus jeder Quelle.\n ### Workflows [...] Optimieren Sie die Nutzung und Kosten von IT-Assets über den gesamten Lebenszyklus hinweg.\n ### Governance, Risk und Compliance\n\n  Schaffen Sie einen integrierten Ansatz, der die organisationale Resilienz erhöht und Risiken mindert.\n ### Security Operations\n\n  Schützen Sie sich vor Sicherheitsbedrohungen und Angriffen.\n ### Außendienst-Management\n\n  Senken Sie die Außendienstkosten durch gesteigerte Effizienz.\n ### HR-Servicebereitstellung\n\n  Bieten Sie Mitarbeitern sofortige Antworten, Anleitungen und eine schnelle Problemlösung.\n ### EmployeeWorks\n\n  Die Zeiten sind vorbei, in denen Sie den Aufgaben hinterherjagen müssen, die andere KI-Tools hätten erledigen sollen.\n\n### Wir stellen vor: Autonomous Workforce",

      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/global/en/about/people/profiles.margustafson%2Bdb15de12.html",
      "title": "Mark Gustafson | US Human Capital and AI Adoption Leader",
      "content": "Fields marked with an asterisk (\\) are required.\n\nMark Gustafson is a leader in Deloitte Consulting's Human Capital practice, dedicated to navigating the complexities of organization change. With over 20 years of consulting experience, Mark has led multiple large organization and workforce transformations across various industries, functions and geographies, taking a human-centered approach to deliver high-impact solutions and improve business performance.     {
      "url": "https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/model-monitor-scheduling.html",
      "title": "Schedule monitoring jobs - Amazon SageMaker AI",
      "content": "`from sagemaker.model_monitor import (\nCronExpressionGenerator,\nBatchTransformInput,\nMonitoringDatasetFormat,\n)\nfrom time import gmtime, strftime\nmon_schedule_name = 'my-model-monitor-schedule-' + strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d-%H-%M-%S\", gmtime())\nmy_default_monitor.create_monitoring_schedule(\nmonitor_schedule_name=mon_schedule_name,\nbatch_transform_input=BatchTransformInput(\ndestination=\"opt/ml/processing/input\",\ndata_captured_destination_s3_uri=s3_capture_upload_path,\ndataset_format=MonitoringDatasetFormat.csv(header=False),\n),\npost_analytics_processor_script=s3_code_postprocessor_uri,\noutput_s3_uri=s3_report_path,\nstatistics=my_default_monitor.baseline_statistics(),\nconstraints=my_default_monitor.suggested_constraints(),\nschedule_cron_expression=CronExpressionGenerator.hourly(), [...] `from sagemaker.model_monitor import CronExpressionGenerator\nfrom time import gmtime, strftime\nmon_schedule_name = 'my-model-monitor-schedule-' + strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d-%H-%M-%S\", gmtime())\nmy_default_monitor.create_monitoring_schedule(\nmonitor_schedule_name=mon_schedule_name,\nendpoint_input=EndpointInput(\nendpoint_name=endpoint_name,\ndestination=\"/opt/ml/processing/input/endpoint\"\n),\npost_analytics_processor_script=s3_code_postprocessor_uri,\noutput_s3_uri=s3_report_path,\nstatistics=my_default_monitor.baseline_statistics(),\nconstraints=my_default_monitor.suggested_constraints(),\nschedule_cron_expression=CronExpressionGenerator.hourly(),\nenable_cloudwatch_metrics=True,\n)`\n\n###### Example schedule for one-time analysis [...] In these arguments, the `schedule_cron_expression` parameter schedules the\nanalysis to run once, immediately, with the value\n`CronExpressionGenerator.now()`. For any schedule with this setting, the\n`data_analysis_start_time` and `data_analysis_end_time`\nparameters are required. These parameters set the start time and end time of an analysis\nwindow. Define these times as offsets that are relative to the current time, and use ISO\n8601 duration format. In this example, the times `-PT1H` and\n`-PT0H` define a window between one hour in the past and the current\ntime. With this schedule, the analysis evaluates only the data that was collected during\nthe specified window.",
      "score": 0.04996327,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/agentic-commerce-discoverability.html",
      "title": "Agentic commerce discoverability: What if AI agents can't find you?",
      "content": "## Rewriting discoverability\n\nIn traditional ecommerce, brands competed for search engine ranking and optimized the visual appeal of their shelf space. Customers manually browsed links, scanned pages, and narrowed options.\n\nBut AI-mediated experiences compress the process of discovery.\n\nAI systems synthesize answers. They reduce a wide range of possible products to a handful of recommended options. Brands that aren’t surfaced in the synthesized response are likely to be entirely excluded from consideration.\n\nThis shift introduces an urgent need for retailers to optimize for generative engines.\n\nGEO (generative engine optimization) isn’t simply SEO under a new name. It requires: [...] AI models are increasingly capable of reasoning, comparison, and workflow orchestration, but they rely on structured inputs and predictable interfaces. As such, brands optimized only for human browsing risk becoming invisible in AI-generated summaries.\n\nDiscoverability now depends on being included, not just ranking first. Commerce should become agent-addressable.       "url": "https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/async-inference.html",
      "title": "Asynchronous inference - Amazon SageMaker AI",
      "content": "Thanks for letting us know we're doing a good job!\n\nIf you've got a moment, please tell us what we did right so we can do more of it.\n\nThanks for letting us know this page needs work. We're sorry we let you down.\n\nIf you've got a moment, please tell us how we can make the documentation better. [...] `AsyncInferenceConfig`\n\n## How Do I Get Started?\n\nIf you are a first-time user of Amazon SageMaker Asynchronous Inference, we recommend that you do the\nfollowing:\n\nRead Asynchronous endpoint operations for\ninformation on how to create, invoke, update, and delete an asynchronous\nendpoint.\n\nExplore the Asynchronous Inference example notebook in the aws/amazon-sagemaker-examples GitHub repository.\n\nNote that if your endpoint uses any of the features listed in this Exclusions page, you cannot use\nAsynchronous Inference.\n\nWarning Javascript is disabled or is unavailable in your browser.\n\nWarning\n\nTo use the Amazon Web Services Documentation, Javascript must be enabled. Please refer to your browser's Help pages for instructions.\n\nThanks for letting us know we're doing a good job! [...] ## How It Works\n\nCreating an asynchronous inference endpoint is similar to creating real-time inference\nendpoints. You can use your existing SageMaker AI models and only need to specify the\n`AsyncInferenceConfig` object while creating your endpoint configuration\nwith the `EndpointConfig` field in the `CreateEndpointConfig` API.\nThe following diagram shows the architecture and workflow of Asynchronous Inference.\n\n`AsyncInferenceConfig`\n`EndpointConfig`\n`CreateEndpointConfig`\nArchitecture diagram of Asynchronous Inference showing how a user invokes an endpoint.",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/standard/resource-center/data-sheet/ds-training-bundles.html",
      "title": "Prepare for the Work of Tomorrow Today",
      "content": "Unify data and analytics on the ServiceNow AI Platform for ultra-fast workflow performance at scale.\n ### Infrastructure\n\n  Deploy, automate, and scale with trusted and flexible cloud-based infrastructure options built to make work flow.\n ### AI Agents\n\n  AI Agents Take action with autonomous AI agents that work for you.\n ### ServiceNow AI Control Tower\n\n  Connect strategy, governance, management, and performance for all your AI across the enterprise.\n ### Security\n\n  Protect sensitive data and increase security, privacy, and compliance across the enterprise.\n ### App Engine\n\n  Build apps that automate manual work and modernize legacy processes.\n ### ServiceNow Store\n\n  Do more with the ServiceNow AI Platform. Find hundreds of certified, ready to use applications.\n ### Responsible AI [...] Make AI and skills-driven talent planning and development decisions.\n ### Legal Service Delivery\n\n  Modernize operations to make faster decisions and grow productivity.\n ### Workplace Service Delivery\n\n  Automate desk bookings, manage facilities, and optimize operations.\n ### Accounts Payable Operations\n\n  Automate invoice ingestion and pay suppliers accurately.\n ### Sourcing and Procurement Operations\n\n  Deploy easy-to-follow procurement processes.\n ### Supplier Lifecycle Operations\n\n  Streamline supplier onboarding, collaboration, and performance management.\n ### EmployeeWorks\n\n  Stop chasing what other AI tools should have finished.\n\nApp Development\n\nBuild custom apps fast while enhancing developer and business expert productivity.\n\nExplore Solution\n\nRelated Products [...] Gain advanced threat hunting, modeling, and analysis on the ServiceNow AI Platform.\n ### Integrated Risk Management\n\n  Make risk-informed decisions while improving efficiency and resilience.\n ### Third-party Risk Management\n\n  Reduce third-party risk while improving resilience and compliance.\n ### Security Posture Control\n\n  Manage the security of enterprise assets on premises and in the cloud.\n ### Privacy Management\n\n  Manage data privacy in real time as part of a holistic enterprise risk program.\n\nEmployee Experience\n\nAutomate the busywork, so that you can unleash your talent on the big work.\n\nExplore Solution\n\nRelated Products\n\n ### HR Service Delivery\n\n  Give employees instant answers, guidance, and fast issue resolution.\n ### Talent Development",
      "score": 0.39382845,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/issues/technology/ai-performance.html",
      "title": "Decoding ROI from AI | PwC",
      "content": "So when I think about where to start, I go past efficiencies and look for the biggest, hardest problems; the most complex data; the most complex process with the most humans involved. AI is built to help in those situations. We have to build it responsibly, we have to get the workforce on board, and we have to build a system that can fundamentally think about our processes differently. That’s the revenue upside for your enterprise.\n\n### 3Capturing value from industry convergence\n\nThis factor assesses the extent to which AI enables cross-sector competition or collaboration. That could be sensing emerging value pools between sectors, responding to shifts in customer needs, or collaborating across sectors to unlock new value from ecosystem partnerships. [...] If you think about training AI models, when you scrape the data, it can easily lead to IP infringement issues, legal harm, reputational harm, financial harm. High-performing organisations really understand this at a deeper level. They are 1.7 times as likely as their peers to have a documented Responsible AI framework guiding their strategy as well as execution.\n\nRight-sized governance and risk management support AI innovation and growth. So, as companies think about deploying AI at scale and ingraining AI into the day-to-day operations, ensure the right guard rails around how the AI can be used, where it should not be used, what are the policies around it. That will unlock the value that they’re looking for. [...] So leaders deploy their resources into ideas with real commercial impact. In this way, top-performing companies stimulate AI innovation across the enterprise, setting them up to compete and win in promising new value pools.\n\n### 5 Governance and risk\n\nThe security, access controls, regulatory compliance processes, ethical frameworks, and oversight bodies needed to manage risk from AI design to deployment.\n\nAI leaders are 1.6x as likely to have a Responsible AI framework that guides AI strategy—including use case selection, design, deployment, and ongoing monitoring.\n\nWatch Kazi Islam, PwC’s Global Assurance Strategy and Growth Leader, discuss the importance of AI risk management and how to build trust in AI.\n\n### Kazi Islam\n\nHeadshot of Kazi Islam",
      "score": 0.35891515,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/ai-isnt-the-problem-your-data-might-be.html",
      "title": "AI isn't the problem; your data might be. - PwC",
      "content": "For CIOs, CDOs, and Chief Risk Officers under pressure to scale AI responsibly, this challenge may no longer be theoretical. It is operational and immediate. This is the reality for enterprises across each industry. Organizations with thousands of data feeds and hundreds of source systems face what feels like an insurmountable task— manually building trust in data, a single query at a time. Failed initiatives can mean wasted investment, but when data governance falls through the cracks, the consequences can be far more severe regulatory fines, failed initiatives, and public facing mishaps that can erode customer confidence in your brand. [...] Think of it less like a traditional data platform and more like a highly capable team of AI agents working around the clock. We provide agents dedicated to data quality and data lineage—so you always know where your data came from and how it reached you. Other agents handle governance, certification, and ontology, while tracking semantic or schema changes and their impact on code. There are even agents that allow your teams to literally talk to their data in plain language—no SQL required. Governance, policy enforcement, and auditability are embedded directly into execution workflows, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and responsible AI standards without slowing innovation. [...] That's where many AI strategies hit a wall. Not because the models aren't capable, but because the data isn't ready. This is the challenge PwC and Google Cloud are helping address an ecosystem of enterprise data agents, a set of more than 30 agents that organize, govern, and activate enterprise data for AI at scale.\n\n## The data problem could be bigger than you think\n\nPicture this: Your team spends months manually combing through databases, cross-referencing records, and tracing the source of a single transaction—often alongside their day-to-day responsibilities. Eventually, someone declares the data “clean enough.”",
      "score": 0.32514593,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Agentic commerce discoverability: What if AI agents can't find you?",
      "content": "Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience (GECX) is designed to bring conversational buying directly to brand websites. GECX uses AI to guide customers through product discovery, answer questions in real time, and move them from intent to purchase. It sits on top of a retailer’s existing commerce backbone while preserving merchant-of-record control.\n\nAgentic commerce, therefore, spans two environments simultaneously:\n\nBoth are critical. {
  "query": "Accenture agentic AI HR",
