  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2025/accenture-helps-organizations-advance-agentic-ai-with-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Accenture Helps Organizations Advance Agentic AI with Gemini ...",
      "content": "Accenture is advancing agentic AI with support for Gemini Enterprise, a new agentic platform designed to bring the full power of Google’s AI to every employee and every workflow. With Accenture’s deep experience in cloud and AI technologies, engineering capabilities, and technical expertise on Google Cloud technology, the two companies will help drive operational efficiencies and business value using Google’s latest AI innovations. [...] “With Gemini Enterprise, we are making it possible for every employee and workflow to benefit from the latest advancements in Google’s AI,” said Victor Morales, VP of GSI Partnerships, Google Cloud. “We’re combining our AI leadership with Accenture's strategic expertise to help clients solve their most complex challenges and achieve measurable outcomes.”\n\nAccenture is deepening its commitment to agentic reinvention with Google Cloud through the evolution of its joint generative AI Center of Excellence (CoE) by expanding it with agentic capabilities to support clients. Powered by Google Gemini models and now with Gemini Enterprise, the CoE will provide clients with the resources they need to scale and orchestrate multi-agent systems that deliver measurable business outcomes. [...] The companies are delivering value and enabling clients to solve complex business challenges with AI across industries, such as:\n\nTo demonstrate the rapid evolution of agentic AI and its transformative potential, Accenture recently hosted a Google Agentic AI Codefest competition to build AI agents that assist organizations across 15 industries. These agents are now available through Google Cloud Marketplace and include next-gen agents, conversational agents, and AgentEngine agents. More than 450 engineered agents built by Accenture are currently available on Google Cloud Marketplace, which will be accessible directly within Gemini Enterprise so that organizations can easily combine agents into one singular view.",
      "score": 0.85157084,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-impact-workforce-change?amp=",
      "title": "AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "Learn More\n\n### How agentic AI is reshaping financial services work\n\nAcross banking and insurance, AI is automating routine tasks, augmenting complex work, but the real shift is how human judgement is redeployed to higher-value decisions. As one insurance client put it, the goal is to “take the robot out of the human”. Agentic AI does exactly that: semi-independent, specialist agents work alongside employees like capable coworkers.\n\nThe impact is neither uniform nor inevitable. AI does not affect every task in a role and outcomes are not predetermined. Leaders make deliberate choices about what should remain human work and what should shift to AI. This is less about redesigning roles and more about designing work for outcomes and intent, not job titles.\n\nSector-by-sector impact [...] Agentic AI will reshape how value is created across the financial services workforce — not just the workforce itself. The right strategy is therefore highly organization specific.\n\n#### Considering individuals, teams and networks\n\nReinventing work means designing for individuals and for the teams and networks that create value.\n\nMost knowledge work in financial services is team based. Yet few teams are adapting effectively. Our Talent Reinventors survey found that only 19% of employees say their team experiments with AI together, and just 17% feel psychologically safe to share new ideas. [...] #### Real world examples: AI in HR\n\nOur Responsible AI Programme for HR, launched in 2016, was designed to accelerate, govern and monitor AI use across HR. We now apply AI across the full talent lifecycle — recruitment, onboarding, development, mobility, reward and colleague support.\n\nThe impact has been material: around 45% productivity gains within HR, a 30% improvement in proficiency for priority skills, a 40% increase in internal fill rates and a 35% reduction in time to fill. These outcomes empower colleagues. For example, we use AI to infer skills from work and learning experience, surface them with employees and help them identify future roles, career paths, and learning options.",
      "score": 0.8127637,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-impact-workforce-change",
      "title": "AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "Agentic AI will reshape how value is created across the financial services workforce — not just the workforce itself. The right strategy is therefore highly organization specific.\n\n#### Considering individuals, teams and networks\n\nReinventing work means designing for individuals and for the teams and networks that create value.\n\nMost knowledge work in financial services is team based. Yet few teams are adapting effectively. Our Talent Reinventors survey found that only 19% of employees say their team experiments with AI together, and just 17% feel psychologically safe to share new ideas. [...] Accenture’s Banking Top Trends 2026: Unconstrained banking is here\n\nLearn More\n\n### How agentic AI is reshaping financial services work\n\nAcross banking and insurance, AI is automating routine tasks, augmenting complex work, but the real shift is how human judgement is redeployed to higher-value decisions. As one insurance client put it, the goal is to “take the robot out of the human”. Agentic AI does exactly that: semi-independent, specialist agents work alongside employees like capable coworkers. [...] ### Conclusion\n\nIn this penultimate blog, we explored how financial services firms must reshape their workforce. Agentic AI requires us to think differently about humans and agents working together. Banks and insurers are likely to employ smaller but far more digitally and human skilled workforces, driving sustained demand for reskilling. HR must be central to this transition, helping leaders navigate change and manage the hybrid workforce.\n\nIn the final blog in this series, I will explore how leadership, culture and operating models must also evolve to sustain this transformation.\n\n# Related Posts\n\nBackground Image\n\n###### By Andy Young\n\n### AI implications for leadership, culture and operating models in financial services\n\nRead more 631 Views\n\nBackground Image\n\n###### By Andy Young",
      "score": 0.79369855,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4022657/accenture-reimagines-it-operations-with-agentic-ai.html",
      "title": "Accenture reimagines IT operations with agentic AI | CIO",
      "content": "## Topics\n\nThor Olavsrud\n\n# Accenture reimagines IT operations with agentic AI\n\n## The global consulting firm has built an AI-powered integration platform to support IT operations.\n\nAccenture\n\nOne of the challenges of IT operations at a global consulting firm like Accenture is it runs a huge and diverse portfolio of technologies to drive its digital core. Maintaining and operating that core requires continuously developing deep skillsets that are becoming rarer as every industry competes within a limited talent pool.\n\nAccenture’s answer is the Accenture Advanced Technology Agent (AATA), an agentic AI-powered integration platform that sits between the company’s human workforce and its technology platforms, and something that’s earned the company a 2025 CIO 100 Award in IT Excellence. [...] To smooth that process, AATA is an agnostic, open system with agentic architecture that enables IT teams and end users to interact with the core to resolve issues or deliver customer solutions without having to connect to an agent or file a single ticket.\n\nAccenture started working on AATA in 2023, starting with a conceptual architectural model. “Then we started to think about how people would consume the technology to derive value, so we wanted it to be largely democratized,” Courtney says. “You could have an open standards orchestration layer and a consistent prompt layer, but then enable teams to be able to either consume natively available agents, plug in existing automation or gen AI processes, or actually create custom agents if that was what required.” [...] Steven Courtney, MD of global IT, technology vision, and strategy, Accenture\n\nSteven Courtney, MD of global IT, technology vision, and strategy, Accenture\n\nAccenture\n\nSome specific focus areas for AATA included variations in AI maturity. Recognizing not every organization will be in the same place when it comes to AI, Accenture built AATA to have open architecture with the flexibility to swap models in and out, allowing it to stay model agnostic and keep pace with the market.",
      "score": 0.78650844,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/tag/artificial-intelligence-ai",
      "title": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "Title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) | Accenture Banking Blog\n###### Agentic AI and the future of work in financial services. ###### AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services. ###### The ultimate guide to banking in the cloud. ##### Accenture Banking Blog Other Blogs Banking BlogCapital Markets BlogInsurance Blog. # ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE (AI). # Artificial Intelligence (AI). ###### Accenture Banking Accenture Banking. #### Savvy banks look to AI to regain customers' trust. ##### Commercial Banking, ##### nSight,. ### Commercial Banking Top Trends for 2025 and beyond. Commercial banking is at a strategic inflection point. ##### Agentic AI, ##### Generative AI, ##### nSight,. ##### Accenture at Sibos, ##### Commercial Banking, ##### Payments,. ### Agentic payments in commerce—the future is here. ##### Agentic AI, ##### Payments Trends, ##### Sibos 2025,. ### Agentic AI and the future of work in financial services. In previous blogs in this series, I explored how leading financial services (FS) organizations are scaling AI for value and using a human-led approach, including agentic architectures that reimagine w... ##### Agentic AI, ##### Future of Workforce,. ### AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services. In my previous blogs in this series, I’ve looked at how leaders are scaling AI, how we are reinventing work using agentic AI and how to lead this change. ##### Agentic AI, ##### Future of Workforce,.",

    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-future-of-work",
      "title": "Agentic AI and the future of work in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "We developed AI agents to sit on top of the claims platform and orchestrate out to other systems, supporting the human claims handler. The AI agents support tasks such as information gathering, data quality, policy and coverage checks, policy detail extraction, claim data summarization (including the first notification of loss, customer and expert inputs, customer correspondence) and even some support to litigation, fraud and reserve management. [...] ### What is agentic architecture?\n\nAI agents are programs that handle tasks and workflows to achieve specific goals. Humans set the goals, but the agents operate more independently, adapting their strategies as needed to achieve the goal. They take inputs, reason, decide on tasks to perform, interact with other agents and tools, review their outcomes and determine the next steps required.\n\nThe agents ‘know’ a domain context in which they have been trained within an organization (e.g. on specific training data and documents). They have long-term memory and can learn from past interactions to optimize decision-making. [...] Through all these tasks the agents support the claims handler to be in charge as the ‘human in the lead’ — for instance, they can look back into source data easily. We have been able to free up 20% of their capacity, focusing their time on better decision-making (improving claims accuracy by 1%) and supporting negotiation in the most complex and valuable claims.\n\nWe’ve seen similar work from a US-based P&C insurer through to complex commercial claims operations.\n\n### The ‘so what’ for work and workforce\n\nImagine agentic AI as your co-worker, where you have team members dedicated to you who specialize in their support to you and can even bring perspectives and strengths complementary to your own.",
      "score": 0.73914057,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://insuranceblog.accenture.com/tag/agentic-ai",

The annual Accenture Tech Vision report is in its 25th year and continues to be a huge source of insight for our technological future. ##### AI, ##### insurance, ##### insurance talent,. ##### Insurance industry, ##### People & Culture,. ### 5 predictions for the insurance industry in 2026. ##### Insurance industry,. ### 3 ways to prepare the insurance workforce for the generative AI era. Now in its 25th year, this year’s report AI: A Declaration of autonomy highlights four key trends that ar... ### Agentic AI is transforming health insurance claims. ##### agentic AI, ##### Claims, ##### Generative AI, ##### insurance talent,. ### When LLMs get their bodies: Tailored robotic solutions for insurers. ##### Insurance industry,. ### Four key ways for insurers to build resilience in a shifting trade landscape. ##### insurance, ##### Resilience,. ##### Senior Managing Director – Insurance Lead, Global and EMEA. ##### Senior Managing Director – Insurance Lead, Americas. ##### Senior Managing Director - Insurance Lead, Asia Pacific. ##### Managing Director – Insurance Technology Lead, Global.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/services/cybersecurity/cyber-protection/secure-ai",
      "title": "Secure AI and Agentic AI | Accenture",
      "content": "#### Agentic AI\n\nAccenture's AI Refinery platform and a scalable agentic architecture can help organizations build and maintain secure AI agents, ensuring they operate reliably and efficiently.\n\n## Reinvention in action\n\nslideText 1  ofText  / 1\n\n### Essentials for securing AI agents in the cloud\n\nAI agents are reshaping cloud workflows, but cybersecurity must evolve too. Accenture’s Zero Trust Model offers a proactive blueprint to secure AI-powered environments.\n\nRead more\n\n### Redefining Resilience in the generative AI era\n\nFind out how to safeguard against gen AI-powered attacks and protect your AI environments.\n\nRead more\n\n### Empowering a secure autonomous AI future [...] Generative AI and Agentic AI have introduced a new era of enterprise intelligence that organizations must embrace, or risk falling behind. However, it also elevates cybersecurity risks and introduces new vulnerabilities, which requires embedding security and trust by design into any AI program. And while most CEOs see cybersecurity as a major consideration for gen AI, only 6% feel they have a robust responsible AI foundation. Accenture can help close that gap and establish resiliency across your AI lifecycle.\n\n### How we help\n\n#### Govern AI usage\n\nImplement AI security governance frameworks, establish consistent policies and training for AI usage, and design and implement review and reporting processes.\n\n#### Protect AI environments [...] ### Sean Kelly\n\nManaging Director, Global Secure AI Lead\n\n### Ryan Leininger\n\nManaging Director – Accenture Cybersecurity, Global AI Resilience Lead\n\nLinkedIn\n\n### Damon McDougald\n\nGlobal Cyber Protection Domain Lead\n\nLinkedIn\n\n## Start now\n\n### Want to explore your next steps in securing AI at scale?     {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-puts-gemini-enterprise-heart-new-agentic-taskforce-enterprise-automation/",
      "title": "Google puts Gemini Enterprise at the heart of the new agentic ...",
      "content": "### Connecting the ecosystem\n\nGoogle revealed that Gemini Enterprise now supports Bring Your Own Model Context Protocol, which can be thought of as a technical bridge that allows administrators to connect the platform to their internal tools and servers. With this, AI agents built with Gemini can now discover and use tools hosted on companies’ private servers, whenever they need them to complete a task. There’s also a new Agent Marketplace in the Agent Gallery, which allows enterprises to access specialized third-party agents developed by Google partners such as ServiceNow Inc., Oracle Corp. and Accenture Plc. [...] To address the “solo” nature of AI, Gemini Enterprise is adding new features called Projects and Canvas, which will transform it from a personal assistant into a team member that everyone can access. Projects provides a shared workspace for humans and agents to co-create, drawing on context from Google Workspace and Microsoft OneDrive. Meanwhile, Canvas is an integrated editor that enables teams to collaborate on Docs and Slides along with AI agents, eliminating the need to keep switching tabs.\n\n### Connecting the ecosystem [...] To that end, it has announced a major revamp of Gemini Enterprise, saying it intends to transition AI from an isolated productivity tool into a “secure, collaborative autonomous engine” for business. “Companies are ready to build their agentic task force, but this demands doing so within a secure and governed environment,” the company said. “This includes creating and deploying agents with their own identity, registry, and gateway so they can always be traced, monitored, and managed.”",
      "score": 0.21950515,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-invests-750m-fuel-agentic-enterprise-googlecloudnext/",
      "title": "Google Cloud invests $750M to fuel the agentic enterprise - SiliconANGLE",
      "content": "The Google Cloud Partner Network spans the full delivery chain of the agentic enterprise, from independent software providers building technology that works alongside Google Cloud, to global systems integrators such as Accenture PLC delivering agentic solutions directly to customers, as well as data providers and marketplace partners, Larson explained. The $750 million investment covers sandbox credits for workshops and pilots, demand generation to identify customers ready to begin their agentic journey, deployment vouchers and forward-deployed engineering services to bring workloads live. [...] The program is itself built on an agentic architecture, with AI agents already handling partner onboarding, training, enablement and support — including a statement-of-work analyzer that went from zero to 90% adoption among funded partners within six months, according to Larson. The pace of partner demand now sees annual release cycles accelerating to monthly cadences as partners recognize a once-in-a-generation market opportunity. [...] Vast Data raises $1B at $30B valuation as AI infrastructure demand accelerates\n\n##### LATEST STORIES\n\nMaximizing Gemini: Google Cloud makes its bid to build the operating system for enterprise AI\n\nAI - BY MARK ALBERTSON . 58 MINS AGO\n\nSpaceX partners with Cursor on AI training, floats potential $60B acquisition\n\nAI - BY MARIA DEUTSCHER . 1 HOUR AGO\n\nAWS accelerates AI agent development in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore\n\nAI - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 1 HOUR AGO\n\nTheCUBE's John Furrier and Alison Kosik conduct a day one keynote assessing the agent control plane battleground. during Google Cloud Next 2026.\n\nThe decisive layer in AI is still unclaimed: theCUBE’s Google Cloud Next day one keynote analysis\n\nAI - BY EMILE LOUW . 2 HOURS AGO",

    },

      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/22/google_enterprise/",
      "title": "Google says it has all the answers for AI agent sprawl - The Register",
      "content": "The announcements land in an increasingly competitive market for enterprise AI platforms. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, and Salesforce have all made a push into agent orchestration and management in recent months. Google's approach leans heavily on vertical integration, with the hope that designing chips, models, infrastructure, and application layers together produces better results than assembling components from different vendors.\n\nGoogle also announced a $750 million fund to support its partner ecosystem in building and deploying agentic AI, along with agreements with McKinsey, Deloitte, and other consulting firms that will receive early access to upcoming models from Google DeepMind. ®\n\nImage 19Share\n\n[](\n\n[](\n\n#### More about\n\n   Google AI\n   Google Cloud Next\n\nMore like these\n\n× [...] Within the press material for the show, several large customers provided testimonials about their own Gemini deployments. GE Appliances said it has more than 800 of Google's AI agents running across manufacturing, logistics, and supply chain operations. KPMG reported 90 percent Gemini Enterprise adoption among employees with more than 100 agents deployed in the first month. Tata Steel said it deployed over 300 specialized agents in nine months. Merck announced a partnership valued at up to $1 billion to build an agentic platform across its R&D, manufacturing, and commercial functions. [...] ## More from The Register\n\n### Special Features\n\n   All Special Features\n   RSA\n   Agentic AI\n   The Future of the Datacenter\n   AWS:Reinvent\n   SC25\n   Supercomputing Month\n   Datacenter Networking Nexus\n   The State of Storage\n\n### Vendor Voice\n\n   All Vendor Voice\n   Infinidat\n   Everpure\n   Rubrik\n   Make it real with Capgemini and AWS\n   Money Movement Hub\n   ZTE\n   Scale Kubernetes.     {
      "url": "https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/microsoft-discovery-advancing-agentic-rd-at-scale/",
      "title": "Microsoft Discovery: Advancing agentic R&D at scale",
      "content": "> _We are entering a new phase of our partnership with Microsoft, focused on scaling AI agents across research, sales and marketing to drive near-term growth. By connecting customer demand to scientific development and back to market execution, agentic AI is enabling faster cycles, sharper prioritization, and tangible impact on revenue growth and business performance.”_\n> \n> \n> —Mike Radossich, Chief Executive Officer (CEO), Syensqo\n\n### GigaTIME [...] ## The era of agentic AI for research and development\n\nAgentic AI opens a new chapter for R&D where autonomous agent teams, guided by human expertise, perform the core research and engineering tasks in a redefined agentic loop. Specialized agents can reason on top of vast amounts of organizational and public-domain knowledge, create hypotheses on an expanded search space, test and validate those hypotheses at scale, analyze the results, and feed conclusions into iterative loops. Empowering science and engineering experts with agentic AI has the potential to reshape the future of science and engineering, enabling organizations to lead boldly in the new Frontier R&D era. [...] ## A growing ecosystem\n\nMicrosoft Discovery works with an expanding ecosystem of partners offering integrated tools and specialized expertise.\n\nImage 5: Logo lockups of partners that work with Microsoft Discovery.\n## Expanding what is possible for R&D\n\nExpanding the preview marks an important step in making agentic AI available to a broader set of R&D organizations. Microsoft Discovery reflects our belief that the next generation of scientific progress can come from systems that combine human expertise with AI that can reason, plan, and act at scale.\n\nWe look forward to partnering with organizations that want to rethink how discovery happens and to help shape the future of enterprise R&D.",
      "score": 0.2552304,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/microsoft/",
      "title": "Microsoft – CIO",
      "content": "By acquiring the Swedish artificial intelligence company and integrating its technology into its existing people management software, Workday aims to \"create the work experience of the future,\" a scenario of human employees empowered as never before by AI agents.\n\n### Microsoft and Workday collaborate to manage agentic AI workers\n\nWith an ID from Microsoft and a records in Workday, AI agents will be able to securely interoperate with each other and with their human coworkers.\n\n### World’s largest pulp manufacturer streamlined SAP S/4HANA integration for better outcomes\n\n### Welcome to the new world of risk: Microsoft cuts off services to energy company without notice [...] ## Topics\n\n# Microsoft\n\nMicrosoft | News, how-tos, features, reviews, and videos\n\n## Latest from today\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft facing CMA probe of its business software portfolio\n\nThe regulator will commence its investigation in May, examining concerns emerging from its cloud market probe.\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft shuffles more of its senior AI leadership\n\nImage\n\n### FTC digs deeper into Microsoft’s bundling and licensing practices\n\n### What hyperscalers’ hyper-spending on data centers tells CIOs\n\n### Microsoft’s bet on AI causes jitters in the market and the enterprise\n\n### AI market correction: CIOs step away from the hype and vendors adjust\n\n### Microsoft has yet to ignite enthusiasm for agentic AI\n\n### SAP touts Microsoft disaster recovery plan for Europe; Analysts doubt it will work [...] ### 5 top business use cases for AI agents\n\nAI agents are poised to transform the enterprise landscape, from automating mundane tasks to driving customer service and innovation. But having strong guardrails in place will be key to success.\n\n### Microsoft is set to hike its Power BI prices — will buyers jump ship or just take the hit?\n\nThe increases are set to take effect April 1, and seem to be a strategic push by Microsoft to get customers to move to its other platforms.\n\n### Microsoft joins the European cloud group that sued it for antitrust\n\nThe world’s largest software company paid a mere €54,000 to join CISPE as a non-voting member, but Amazon still voted to block Microsoft from joining at all.",
      "score": 0.19228172,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "content": "#### Start your proof of concept with $300 in free credit\n\n Develop with our latest Generative AI models and tools.\n Get free usage of 20+ popular products, including Compute Engine and AI APIs.\n No automatic charges, no commitment.\n\nView free product offers\n\n#### Keep exploring with 20+ always-free products.\n\nAccess 20+ free products for common use cases, including AI APIs, VMs, data warehouses, and more.\n\n## Documentation resources\n\nFind quickstarts and guides, review key references, and get help with common issues.\n\n### Get started\n\n Data Agent Kit extension for VS Code overview\n Install the extension\n Set up and configure settings\n Find and explore data\n Release notes\n\n### Connect to your data [...] ### Connect to your data\n\n Data Agent Kit for CLIs: Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI\n Use MCP servers\n Transform data\n Build data pipelines\n Train AI and ML models\n\n## Related videos\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. It lets you work with your Google Cloud resources to manage your data assets, run queries, and deploy data pipelines directly from your preferred IDE.\n\nGet started for free\n\n#### Start your proof of concept with $300 in free credit",

      "content": "Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Workflows documentation\n\nYou can use Workflows to execute services in an order you define: a workflow.\nCombine the power of Google Cloud services, custom services hosted on Cloud Run functions or\nCloud Run, and any HTTP-based API to create flexible serverless applications.\nWorkflows requires no infrastructure management and scales seamlessly with demand,\nincluding scaling down to zero.\n\nGo to the Workflows product page for more.\n\n#### Start your proof of concept with $300 in free credit\n\n#### Keep exploring with 20+ always-free products.\n\nAccess 20+ free products for common use cases, including AI APIs, VMs, data warehouses,\nand more.\n\n## Documentation resources\n\n### Get started\n\nCreate and deploy a workflow using the console [...] Create and deploy a workflow using the console\n\nCreate and deploy a workflow using the gcloud CLI\n\nCreate and deploy a workflow using Terraform\n\nExecute a workflow using a client library\n\n### Reference\n\nSyntax reference\n\nStandard library reference\n\nExecutions REST API\n\nConnectors reference\n\n### Resources\n\nRelease notes\n\nQuotas and limits\n\nPricing\n\nGet support\n\n## Related resources\n\n### Run a batch translation using the Cloud Translation connector\n\nLearn how to use the Cloud Translation API connector to translate files to other\nlanguages in asynchronous batch mode.\n\n### Use Workflows with Cloud Run and Cloud Functions\n\nLearn how to use Workflows to link two public HTTP services (using Cloud Functions), an\nexternal REST API, and a private Cloud Run service together.     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-au/managing-ai-powered-future-of-work.html",
      "title": "The Workday Agent System of Record Is Now Generally Available",
      "content": "With a unique understanding of how AI agents interact with business processes, tasks, and reporting data, ASOR brings insights to process efficiency, ROI, and total business impact. This helps leaders measure and assess the value that AI agents provide, so they can refocus employees on the highest value work.\n\n“The real value of Workday’s Agent System of Record is the ability to capture analytics across an ecosystem of agents,” said Claudio Valera, PwC global and U.S. Workday alliance leader. “By bringing insights from thousands of use cases into a single system, you’re able to clearly see how your human-machine teams are performing. This visibility enables teams to innovate more effectively and scale AI with confidence.”\n\n## Built for Accountability and Governance [...] Jo-Anne Ruhl\n kelly-trindel-headshot-workday-logo \n\n  Artificial Intelligence Human Resources\n\n  ### Workday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\n  Our North Star remains rooted in our AI ethics principles and a robust responsible AI framework.\n\n   \n\n  Kelly Trindel\n Three people working on laptop together \n\n  Artificial Intelligence Human Resources\n\n  ### Designing AI-Ready Roles: The Augmented Strategist Blueprint\n\n  While many organizations struggle to scale AI, one group of high-maturity users—the augmented strategists—are building a path to ROI by designing roles intentionally for AI-powered business.\n\n   \n\n  Julie Jares [...] “Our HR leadership sees that the future of our workforce is human plus agent, and in order to make sense of that, we need all of those analytics in one spot. There is just so much value in having that centralized ASOR repository of the agents at a foundational level. And then the real value comes in from logging all of the telemetry for every one of those calls into that same repository that we can start mining for insights,\" adds a director of business systems architecture at a major health insurance company.\n\n> \"The real value of Workday’s Agent System of Record is the ability to capture analytics across an ecosystem of agents, bringing insights from thousands of use cases into a single system.\"\n>\n>  Claudio Valera PwC Global and U.S. Workday Alliance Leader",
      "score": 0.58408195,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Blog Homepage | Workday",
      "content": "Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Delivers Next Wave of Agentic AI to Power the New Work Day\n\n  With hundreds of new AI capabilities and agents, we’re helping HR, Finance, IT, and Legal teams trade mundane, repetitive tasks for the work that really matters.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n man with headphones and woman looking at computer \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## The Business Value of Your Developer Ecosystem\n\n  The true value of a developer ecosystem isn't captured on a revenue statement—it's in the compound effect of community, innovation, and network strength that accrues to your platform over time.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores\n  \n\n  Workday News and Culture\n\n  ## Workday Recognized as One of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® [...] ## Innovation Without Compromise\n\n  In these two episodes of The Innovation Exchange podcast, Workday’s Mark Woollen speaks with leaders from PwC and Relish about how partner-built innovations are helping organizations move faster without sacrificing trust, compliance, or control.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n two-people-smiling-at-laptop-devcon-logo-with-gradient \n\n  Application Development   Technology Innovation\n\n  ## Workday DevCon 2026: Going Agentic, Building the Future\n\n  At Workday DevCon, developers solve real problems and build solutions that matter. If you’re not already part of the action, 2026 is the year to jump in—either live or online.\n\n   \n\n  Nick Moores [...] Language Selection\n\n United States (English)\")\n Australia (English)\")\n Belgium (English)\")\n Canada (English)\")\n Denmark (English)\")\n Deutschland (Deutsch)\")\n España (Español)\")\n Finland (English)\")\n France (Français)\")\n Hong Kong (English)\")\n Indonesia (English)\")\n Ireland (English)\")\n Italia (Italiano)\")\n 日本 (日本語)\")\n 대한민국 (한국어)\")\n Luxembourg (English)\")\n Malaysia (English)\")\n México (Español)\")\n Nederland (Nederlands)\")\n New Zealand (English)\")\n Norway (English)\")\n Österreich (Deutsch)\")\n Schweiz (Deutsch)\")\n Singapore (English)\")\n South Africa (English)\")\n Sweden (English)\")\n Taiwan (English)\")\n Thailand (English)\")\n United Kingdom (English)\")\n\n a group of people walking down the stairs \n\n  Artificial Intelligence   Technology Innovation",

    {
      "url": "https://events.sap.com/gb-hr-connect-london-2026/en_us/home.html",
      "title": "SAP HR Connect London 2026",
      "content": "###\n\n### \n\nDoddington 1 - Joule and ESM with PwC\n\nCombining SAP SuccessFactors ESM with Joule’s AI-powered assistance, to transform HR service delivery. Hear a real stroy of streamlining requests, automating routine tasks, and giving employees instant, intuitive support. The integrated model improved efficiency, reduced manual workload, and delivered a more consistent global experience, enabling HR to focus on strategic, value‑driven work.\n\nGrand Hall 1 - Payroll Modernisation - Building a Case for Change. Nick Francis, Payroll Manager, Decathlon with Gavdi\n\nDoddington 2 - A Unified Skills, Development and Careers Goal: How RS Group Built for Scale and Employee Experience with Skillsoft [...] ###\n\n### \n\nHow leading organisations are moving from transformation ambition to real business value. Panellists discuss why they are rethinking operating models and workforce strategies, how they’re standardising and simplifying at scale, and how technology, data, and skills are enabling practical, organisation‑wide outcomes. The session focuses on design intent, global operating models, skills‑based transformation, and embedding value through continuous improvement, AI, and integrated HR experiences\n\nMaryann Abbajay, Chief Revenue Officer , Steve Raymond – National Grid, Gail Kinahan – Standard Chartered, PageGroup and PwC\n\n###\n\n### \n\nDoddington 1 - Joule and ESM with PwC [...] A digital adoption strategy is critical to realising the full value of your SAP investment and navigating the evolution of agentic AI. WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM empowers employees and managers to complete HR workflows correctly and confidently through personalised guidance, automation, AI assistance and learning in the flow of work.       "content": "Zhipu’s GLM-Image demonstrates viability of domestic alternatives amid US export restrictions\n\n### Anthropic expands Claude Code beyond developer tasks with Cowork\n\nAnalysts predict that the new assistant will gain traction in knowledge-driven roles, particularly in environments where clear guardrails and governance can be established.\n\n### Java 27 gets its first feature\n\nJDK 27, due in September, kicks off with a post-quantum hybrid key exchange proposal to bolster the security of network communications.\n\n### Google’s Universal Commerce Protocol aims to simplify life for shopping bots… and devs\n\nAgentic AI is struggling with the complexity and variety of real-world commerce, so Google and others are helping enterprises standardize interactions with online shopping bots. [...] ## Topics\n\n# News\n\nThe news developers need to know\n\n## Latest from today\n\nImage\n\n### Claude Mythos signals a new era in AI-driven security, finding 271 flaws in Firefox\n\nThe AI exposed hundreds of bugs in Mozilla’s web browser, raising hopes around defensive advantage, alongside fears of dual-use risk.\n\nImage\n\n### Malicious pgserve, automagik developer tools found in npm registry\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft issues out-of-band patch for critical security flaw in update to ASP.NET Core\n\n### SpaceX secures option to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B\n\n### Snowflake offers help to users and builders of AI agents\n\n### Amazon’s $5B Anthropic bet is really about compute, not just cash\n\n### GitHub pauses new Copilot sign-ups as agentic AI strains infrastructure [...] ### Hackers exploit Vercel’s trust in AI integration\n\n### Oracle delivers semantic search without LLMs\n\n### Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max-Thinking expands enterprise AI model choices\n\nThe company claims the model demonstrates performance comparable to GPT-5.2-Thinking, Claude-Opus-4.5, and Gemini 3 Pro.\n\n### Unplugged holes in the npm and yarn package managers could let attackers bypass defenses against Shai-Hulud\n\nA researcher at Koi Security says the two key platforms have not plugged the vulnerabilities enabling the worm attacks, and ‘the JavaScript ecosystem deserves better.’\n\n### Descope introduces Agentic Identity Hub 2.0 for managing AI agents",

    {
      "url": "https://www.computerworld.com/article/4161990/gemini-enterprise-update-brings-ai-agents-into-collaborative-workflows.html",
      "title": "Gemini Enterprise update brings AI agents into collaborative workflows",
      "content": "Image\n\n### The smartest ways to sync your Android and computer clipboards\n\nImage\n\n### Surface Prices Surge, IBM DEI Settlement, Apple Business | Ep. 73\n\nImage\n\n### Anthropic Ruling, AI ROI Failures, Hacker Identified Globally | Ep. 71\n\nImage\n\n### Adobe Exploit, DARPA AI Language, Intel Terafab Push | Ep. 72\n\nImage\n\n### Surface Prices Surge, IBM DEI Settlement, Apple Business | Ep. 73\n\nImage\n\n### Anthropic Ruling, AI ROI Failures, Hacker Identified Globally | Ep. 71\n\nImage\n\n### Adobe Exploit, DARPA AI Language, Intel Terafab Push | Ep. 72\n\nImage\n\n### About\n\n### Policies\n\n### More\n\n### Our Network [...] “Implementing agentic processes is seen by most vendors as the next big opportunity to drive adoption of AI technologies in enterprise environments,” said Ed Anderson, research vice president and distinguished analyst at Gartner. “There will be a lot of competition in this area.”\n\nAccording to Leone, Google’s edge lies in a “full-stack ownership claim few other vendors can make as cleanly, across infrastructure, models, data, security, and end-user apps,” he said. Tools such as Agent Identity, Registry and Gateway — as well as security features such as Model Armor and MCP and A2A protocols to connect agents — will help provide IT with visibility and control when running thousands of agents across business units. [...] Underpinning the Gemini Enterprise app is the new Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform that’s billed as the platform to “build, scale, govern, and optimize agents,” said, Michael Gerstenhaber, vice president of product management, Cloud AI, Google Cloud.\n\n## IT gets new agentic AI management tools, too\n\nAs part the Agent Platform, Google introduced new tools at Cloud Next to help IT teams manage agents build and deployed in Gemini Enterprise. Among these is Agent Identity, which assigns a unique cryptographic ID to each agent operating in Gemini Enterprise.",

      "content": "“When AI is moving at such a fast pace, no one company like Apple can really move fast enough to keep up,” Gold said.\n\nThe Google partnership is a solid step in that direction, as       "url": "https://www.reuters.com/technology/vast-data-valued-30-billion-latest-funding-round-2026-04-22/",
      "title": "Nvidia-backed VAST Data valued at $30 billion in latest funding round",
      "content": "The ‌company, ⁠which counts Elon Musk's xAI and CoreWeave (CRWV.O), opens new tab and the U.S. Air Force, among others, as its customers, was valued at $9.1 billion in 2023.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue\n\n\"The VAST platform is a key enabling technology for next gen ​AI infrastructure initiatives - ​providing a ⁠modern, flexible data architecture for Gen AI applications and agentic workflows,\" said Larry Feinsmith, managing director and ​head of Global Tech Strategy, Innovation and Partnerships ​at JPMorgan ⁠Chase.\n\nPrimary proceeds from the latest raise will be used to expand VAST Data's global footprint, strengthen partnerships and pursue strategic transactions to broaden ⁠its ​technology platform. [...] The company in November signed a $1.17 ​billion commercial agreement with cloud provider CoreWeave, extending their existing partnership.\n\nReporting by Pritam Biswas and ​Prakhar Srivastava in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber and Shinjini Ganguli\n\nOur Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab\n\n   Suggested Topics:\n   Technology\n\n    \n    \n    \n    \n    \n\nPurchase Licensing Rights\n\n## Read Next\n\n   44 mins agoBusiness categoryGoogle puts AI agents at heart of its enterprise money-making push , opens new tab\n\n### Stay Informed\n\n   Download the App (iOS), opens new tab\n   Download the App (Android), opens new tab\n   Newsletters\n   Subscribe\n\n### Information you can trust [...] # Nvidia-backed VAST Data valued at $30 billion in latest funding round | Reuters\n\nSkip to main content\n\nReport AdImage 1\n\nExclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv\n\n - VAST Data said on Wednesday it was valued at $30 billion in its latest funding round, growing more than ​threefold since 2023, helped by strong interest in companies ‌that build the infrastructure needed for artificial intelligence.\n\nThe New York-based company, which is backed by Nvidia, said the Series F round fetched it about $1 billion ​in primary and secondary capital, and was led by Drive ​Capital and Access Industries.\n\nLearn about the latest breakthroughs in AI and tech with the Reuters Artificial Intelligencer newsletter. Sign up here.",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/ch-en/insights/expert-perspectives/from-reactive-to-resolution-how-agentic-ai-is-redefining-customer-and-employee-experience",
      "title": "Agentic AI transforms CX and PX - Capgemini Switzerland",
      "content": "According to Capgemini Research Institute (CRI) report, agentic AI could generate up to $450 billion in economic value by 2028, transforming how organizations operate across industries.  \n  \nCapgemini, in partnership with ServiceNow, is changing that – by harnessing the power of agentic AI to reimagine both customer experience (CX) and people experience (PX). Unlike traditional AI-assist tools that merely suggest next steps, agentic AI is autonomous, goal-driven, and capable of reasoning, planning, and acting across systems to deliver outcomes – not just recommendations. [...] ##### Why Capgemini and ServiceNow?\n\nCapgemini is a globally recognized ServiceNow Elite Partner, offering deep expertise across IT Service Management (ITSM), Human Resources (HR), and Customer Experience (CX). With over 600 successful ServiceNow engagements and a workforce holding more than 12,000 certifications and accreditations, we deliver the scale, experience, and innovation required to implement agentic AI solutions that drive real business outcomes. [...] #### Moments that matter: Agentic AI in people experience\n\nMany organizations still rely on fragmented systems and manual processes that slow productivity and frustrate employees.  \n  \nCapgemini’s PX with ServiceNow transforms the employee journey by delivering moments that matter – powered by agentic AI. These are the pivotal points in an employee’s working life where experience has the greatest impact: onboarding, development, day-to-day work, getting support, and offboarding. By orchestrating these moments with intelligent automation, organizations can create seamless, personalized experiences that boost satisfaction, productivity, and retention.\n\nKey capabilities include the following:",
      "score": 0.99996924,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/sg-en/news/capgemini-accelerates-enterprise-adoption-of-agentic-ai-for-industries-with-nvidia",
      "title": "Capgemini accelerates enterprise adoption of agentic AI for industries with NVIDIA - Capgemini Singapore",
      "content": "“Agentic AI is changing the way we live and work. There is vast potential for AI agents to drive innovation,” said Chris Penrose, Global Head of Business Development for Telco, NVIDIA. “Capgemini has a deep understanding of the complex challenges facing enterprises and the industry-specific agentic AI use cases that can unlock significant business value. By leveraging NVIDIA NIM, together we can accelerate deployment of AI agents that enhance productivity and revolutionize the way they operate, whilst addressing critical concerns like trust, safety, security and compliance.”\n\nTogether with NVIDIA, Capgemini is building over 100 bespoke AI agent-driven solutions tailored to various industry use cases, including: [...] Paris, March 19, 2025 –Capgeminitoday announced the introduction of customized agentic solutions designed in collaboration with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI adoption. Capgemini will deliver end-to-end AI services tailored to meet the diverse needs of specific industries when implementing AI agents, from healthcare and financial services to manufacturing and telco. By leveraging the power of NVIDIA NIM and a dedicated agentic gallery, Capgemini will be able to streamline deployment and reduce complexity for enterprise clients looking to derive actionable insights to achieve agentic-driven business transformation. [...] “With the AI Factory, we are creating a secure and sustainable foundation for AI innovation in Norway,” said Jannicke Hilland, EVP and Head of Telenor Infrastructure. “Capgemini has played a crucial role in developing this service, working closely with us to build a platform that allows businesses to harness AI while maintaining full control over their data. Together, we are ensuring that organizations have access to cutting-edge AI solutions without compromising security or sustainability.”",
      "score": 0.999966,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/no-no/news/press-releases/capgemini-accelerates-enterprise-adoption-of-agentic-ai-for-industries-with-nvidia",
      "title": "Capgemini accelerates enterprise adoption of agentic AI for industries with NVIDIA - Capgemini Norway",
      "content": "“Agentic AI is changing the way we live and work. There is vast potential for AI agents to drive innovation,” said Chris Penrose, Global Head of Business Development for Telco, NVIDIA. “Capgemini has a deep understanding of the complex challenges facing enterprises and the industry-specific agentic AI use cases that can unlock significant business value. By leveraging NVIDIA NIM, together we can accelerate deployment of AI agents that enhance productivity and revolutionize the way they operate, whilst addressing critical concerns like trust, safety, security and compliance.”\n\nTogether with NVIDIA, Capgemini is building over 100 bespoke AI agent-driven solutions tailored to various industry use cases, including: [...] “This new collaboration with NVIDIA marks a pivotal step forward in our commitment to bringing cutting-edge AI-powered technology solutions to our clients for accelerated value creation,” said Roshan Gya, Capgemini Invent CEO and Group Executive Board member at Capgemini. “By leveraging the power of the NVIDIA AI Stack, Capgemini will help clients expedite their agentic AI journey from strategy to full deployment, enabling them to solve complex business challenges and innovate at scale. NVIDIA’s robust platform provides the necessary infrastructure and tools to make this acceleration possible. Our work with Telenor on its AI Factory showcases how we can help an enterprise to scale generative and agentic AI to gain competitive advantage and realize business value.”\n\n## Related news [...] Paris, March 19, 2025 –Capgeminitoday announced the introduction of customized agentic solutions designed in collaboration with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI adoption. Capgemini will deliver end-to-end AI services tailored to meet the diverse needs of specific industries when implementing AI agents, from healthcare and financial services to manufacturing and telco. By leveraging the power of NVIDIA NIM and a dedicated agentic gallery, Capgemini will be able to streamline deployment and reduce complexity for enterprise clients looking to derive actionable insights to achieve agentic-driven business transformation.",
      "score": 0.99996054,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/se-en/solutions/agentic-ai-for-enterprise-by-capgemini",
      "title": "Agentic AI for Enterprise by Capgemini - Capgemini Sweden",
      "content": "### How we can help\n\nAI agents will transform the way businesses operate, leading to unprecedented levels of productivity, scalable efficiency and innovation.\n\nAccording to our research, most organizations agree that AI agents promise smoother automation and enhanced productivity: AI agents will help drive higher levels of automation in our workflows (71%) or significantly improve customer service, leading to improved satisfaction (64%). Still organizations meet key challenges such as integration complexity, accountability, governance, and ethical concerns.\n\nWe help organizations navigate the complexities of implementing agentic AI solutions, bringing robust governance frameworks that allow compliance, scalability, and consistent performance. We also address strategic objectives such as: [...] Our offer includes three possible implementation routes: Off-the-shelf agents for common tasks, custom AI agents tailored to specific processes, and embedded agents integrated into existing platforms. This flexibility ensures that our AI agents fulfill the unique requirements of your organization for optimal performance and scalability. In addition, our dedicated agentic gallery simplifies the procedure of developing AI agents from the ground up for each business process, resulting in significant time savings and cost reductions.\n\nPartnering with NVIDIA, Capgemini is building over 100 bespoke AI agent-driven solutions tailored to various industry use cases, from life sciences, telco or financial services to manufacturing and retail. [...] #### Rapid prototyping and deployment\n\nAccelerating AI agent rollouts with pre-configured workflows and optimized infrastructure, reducing time-to-market.\n\n#### Seamless integration\n\nCombining AI agent capabilities with existing business applications to unlock new levels of process automation, efficiency and data-driven decision-making.\n\n#### Scalability and governance\n\nThe dedicated agentic capabilities of Capgemini RAISE, including governance, real-time monitoring and orchestration, enables unified control of agentic solutions with tangible results.",
      "score": 0.9999502,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/in-en/solutions/agentic-ai-for-enterprise-by-capgemini",
      "title": "Agentic AI for Enterprise by Capgemini - Capgemini India",
      "content": "Title: Agentic AI for Enterprise by Capgemini - Capgemini India\n# Agentic AI for Enterprise by Capgemini. ### Empower your enterprise with agentic AI-driven transformation. Scaling AI agents is not a one-time project—it’s a structured journey. > *We offer bespoke AI solutions tailored to your unique business needs, ensuring seamless integration of AI agents as part of your team in sync with your company-specific processes.*. Pioneering Gen AI factory in the agentic age. AI agents will transform the way businesses operate, leading to unprecedented levels of productivity, scalable efficiency and innovation. According to our research, most organizations agree that AI agents promise smoother automation and enhanced productivity: AI agents will help drive higher levels of automation in our workflows (71%) or significantly improve customer service, leading to improved satisfaction (64%). We help organizations navigate the complexities of implementing agentic AI solutions, bringing robust governance frameworks that allow compliance, scalability, and consistent performance. Combining AI agent capabilities with existing business applications to unlock new levels of process automation, efficiency and data-driven decision-making. This flexibility ensures that our AI agents fulfill the unique requirements of your organization for optimal performance and scalability. Partnering with NVIDIA, Capgemini is building over 100 bespoke AI agent-driven solutions tailored to various industry use cases, from life sciences, telco or financial services to manufacturing and retail. > *The future of enterprise operations lies in the seamless integration of AI agents working together with humans and under their control*. ### Enabling evidence-based education decision-making with agentic AI. Capgemini teams up with UNESCO and AWS for the 7th edition of the Global Data Science Challenge to develop an agentic Gen AI system that can empower countries to make informed, evidence-based policy decisions to enhance foundational learning outcomes. ### The next industrial revolution – multi-agent systems and small Gen AI models are transforming factories. ### From innovation to transformation: How AI agents are shaping the future of work. ### Activating Gen AI at scale to transform financial services: Insights and best practices from Capgemini. AI, Analytics, Agents Global Leader. Agentic AI for Enterprise Offer Leader. Before joining Capgemini, she was Director of Data Science and Machine Learning at Adidas in Amsterdam, leading a global team focused on AI solutions for personalization, demand forecasting, and price optimization.",

    }

      "title": "Welcome to Google Cloud Next26",
      "content": "Agentic Taskforceincludes new AI capabilities across Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience and Google Workspace.\n Customer Success across our entire portfolio. [...] Agentic Data Cloud introduces a new, AI-native architecture that allows your data to be utilized at the speed and scale required by agentic AI. We are announcing Knowledge Catalog to ground agents in trusted business context across your entire data estate, a Data Agent Kit for Gemini-powered data science authoring, and a cross-cloud, AI-native Lakehouse that gives you seamless access to all of your data, regardless of where it’s located, unleashing a borderless foundation for autonomous action. [...] Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is our new, comprehensive platform to build, scale, govern, and optimize agents. Built on top of our leading infrastructure, and deeply integrated with our data and security capabilities, it is the foundation for the Agentic Enterprise. It brings together the best of Vertex AI with transformational new features, including Agent Studio, Agent-to-Agent Orchestration, Agent Registry, Agent Identity, Agent Gateway, Agent Observability, and much, much more. This empowers your technical teams with a comprehensive, unified platform to manage every aspect of your agents, and deliver them through the Gemini Enterprise app, the front door for AI for every employee.",
      "score": 0.4403014,

    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.infosys.com/iki/techcompass/personal-journey-evolution-user-interfaces.html",
      "title": "From Terminals to Agents: A Personal Journey Through the Evolution of User Interfaces",
      "content": "By 2025, the focus shifted to Agentic AI, which acts on behalf of users rather than relying on direct clicks. Agentic Interfaces interpret user intent, analyze context, and choose suitable actions rather than just following commands.\n\nFor example, consider a banking app. Instead of navigating through a series of menus to transfer money, a user can now express their intention like this:\n\n\"Send $500 to Emma next Friday, but only if my account maintains a balance of at least $2,000.\"\n\nThe agent promptly checks the user's account balance, schedules the transfer, and confirms the action by saying, \"I've scheduled your transfer. Your account balance will remain above $2,000. Would you like me to send a reminder before the transfer is processed?\" [...] Reflecting on my career, I see a clear progression: from text terminals requiring precision, to graphical interfaces that broadened access, to web-based systems connecting experiences, and now to Agentic AI acting on our behalf.\n\nThis transition is defined not only by technological capability, but also by its scale and speed. [...] Infosys TechCompass \n\n Technology trends;)\n Techno-functional trends;)\n\n Email Subscriptions\n\n Data\n Artificial Intelligence\n Cloud\n Experience\n API, Microservices\n Low Code, No Code\n Cybersecurity\n IT BizOps\n DevSecOps\n Open source\n\n 5G\n Education\n Digital Supply Chain\n\n## From Terminals to Agents: A Personal Journey Through the Evolution of User Interfaces\n\nTechnological advancements have transformed the way humans interact with computers, yet one fundamental principle remains unchanged: the interface defines the nature of that relationship.\n\nOver my career, I have witnessed this relationship's transformation—from Unix command-line terminals to sophisticated, intention-driven agentic interfaces acting on our behalf.",
      "score": 0.3273677,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.tcs.com/who-we-are/events/tcs-at-ssow-conference-2026",
      "title": "TCS at SSOW Conference 2026: AI-led Autonomous GBS Showcase",
      "content": "Title: TCS at SSOW Conference 2026: AI-led Autonomous GBS Showcase\nAt the event, TCS will showcase its abilities and discuss ai-powered supply chain and the evolution towards autonomous GBS in procurement operations and more. * At SSOW 2026, TCS will demonstrate its capabilities that help build artificial intelligence (AI)‑led Autonomous Global Business Services (GBS) and showcase how agentic AI, Intelligent Choice Architecture (ICA), and Connected Organisation Intelligence™ (COI) enable perpetually adaptive enterprises. * At the TCS booth, attendees will experience next‑gen autonomous finance, supply chain, human resources (HR), and customer experience (CX) solutions in action, demonstrating how agentic AI drives real‑time intelligence, resilience, and enterprise‑wide adaptability. The panel discussion will explore how AI‑led autonomous operations, enabled through mature global business services (GBS), are redefining supply chains from reactive, siloed firefighting to foresight‑driven, enterprise‑wide orchestration. He oversees one of TCS’ largest service practices that helps customers leverage cognitive technologies to transform the operations stack comprising business processes and IT infrastructure, making their operations more intelligent, agile, scalable, resilient, and adaptable. He oversees one of TCS’ largest service practices that helps customers leverage cognitive technologies to transform the operations stack comprising business processes and IT infrastructure, making their operations more intelligent, agile, scalable, resilient, and adaptable. Ajith Thomas is the Global Head of Supply Chain and Sourcing & Procurement for Enterprise Cognitive Business Operations at Tata Consultancy Services. Ajith Thomas is the Global Head of Supply Chain and Sourcing & Procurement for Enterprise Cognitive Business Operations at Tata Consultancy Services. This recognition reflects TCS’ strong commitment to helping organisations build future-ready operations, accelerate strategic priorities, and unlock sustained business value through advanced digital, AI-led, and industry-focussed solutions. Ashok Pai, senior vice president and global head, Enterprise Cognitive Business Operations, TCS, will be participating in The G6 debate titled ‘The outsourcing model in 2030: In the AI age, does BPO fit?’ He will be joined by industry leaders to examine how AI is reshaping the future of outsourcing. Ashok will discuss TCS’ AI‑led Autonomous GBS vision, explaining how agentic AI and COI enable self‑orchestrating operations that seamlessly sense change, make decisions, and execute actions across enterprise functions like finance, supply chain, HR and customer experience (CX).",

    {
      "url": "https://www.tcs.com/what-we-do/industries/insurance/solution/tcs-agentic-claims-processing",
      "title": "TCS Agentic AI Insurance Decisioning: Reimagining Claims Management",
      "content": "TCS Agentic AI Insurance Decisioning supports the automation of workers compensation, disability, auto and property claims by leveraging specialized AI agents.\n\nThese agents extract and interpret information from various data sources such as doctor’s notes, employment letters, and claim forms. By reasoning across these inputs, they assess compensability with high accuracy. The solution uses fine-tuned language models to evaluate compliance aligned with state-specific regulations, NAIC guidelines, and legal precedents ensuring that decisions are fair, consistent, and defensible.\n\nAuto adjudication pipeline   \n The auto adjudication pipeline enables end-to-end claims processing through modular AI agents: [...] ## Overview\n\nTCS Agentic AI Insurance Decisioning is an AI-native solution that reimagines insurance claims management as an adaptive, agent-driven process. By embedding intelligence across the value chain, it improves efficiency, reduces turnaround time, and enhances customer experience. The solution, built on agentic AI, manages complex workflows, supports expert-level decision-making, and integrates seamlessly with enterprise systems. It moves beyond traditional RPA and rules-based automation to deliver context-aware claims processing.\n\n## Solution [...] Human-in-the-loop by design  \n Blending automation with oversight, the solution allows claims professionals to review, override, or co-pilot decisions through an intuitive interface. The system learns from each interaction—continuously refining agent performance and trustworthiness.\n\nEnterprise-scale and cloud native  \n Built on a scalable, cloud-native foundation, the solution can handle high-volume claims across geographies with ease. TCS ensures enterprise-grade security, performance, and reliability—backed by global delivery, managed services, and continuous support.\n\n## Claims intelligence in practice\n\nWorkers compensation, Auto, Property and disability lines of business",

      "content": "Title: TCS Helps Bekaert Achieve Seamless HR System Transformation\nAt TCS, we don’t just help businesses transform. 24 Mar 2026 TCS Named a Leader in IDC MarketScape for Asia/Pacific Professional and Managed Services for Google Cloud. 20 Mar 2026 TCS a Leader in Payments Business Process Services: Everest Group. 12 Mar 2026 TCS a Leader in Customer Experience Services in Insurance Operations. ### Find the latest news about TCS. 20 Mar 2026 TCS and Swissport extend strategic partnership to accelerate AI-led transformation. 19 Mar 2026 Amadeus and TCS announce global strategic partnership to accelerate modern airline retailing. Learn how TCS leveraged Salesforce Experience Cloud and Salesforce CMS to help Linde implement a secure digital experience for better CX, efficiency, and growth. Join TCS at NVIDIA GTC 2026 and explore the next wave of AI innovation spanning physical and agentic AI that help enterprises learn, decide, and act at speed. * Bekaert wanted to centralize its global HR systems into a single cloud-based solution. * TCS standardizes Bekaert’s HR processes with a global, cloud-based information system. Challenge Solution Business Benefits What the client says what made it happen. In keeping with Bekaert's philosophy of ‘Better Together’, the firm’s HR division partnered with TCS to step up skills, build new capabilities, and nurture a leadership culture. To realize its HR transformation goals, Bekaert implemented the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central solution, a cloud-based information system. As a strategic partner, TCS designed and deployed data migration from SAP’s enterprise resource planning (SAP ERP) system and other data sources into a consistent global data layer on SuccessFactors Employee Central. TCS helped Bekaert reduce the overheads of transactional tasks by unifying, automating, and digitalizing global HR systems. Thanks to TCS, we managed to deliver up to the HR business expectations. With autonomous vehicles set to become the future of mobility, read how TCS’ solution facilitates AV development using cutting-edge technologies like AI & ML.     {
      "url": "https://www.tcs.com/who-we-are/newsroom/press-release/tcs-launches-genai-agentic-ai-powered-tcs-mastercraft-help-enterprises-swiftly-modernize-legacy-systems",
      "title": "TCS Launches GenAI and Agentic AI-Powered TCS MasterCraft™ to Help Enterprises Swiftly Modernize Legacy Systems",
      "content": "Rooted in the heritage of the Tata Group, TCS is focused on creating long term value for its clients, its investors, its employees, and the community at large. With a highly skilled workforce of over 607,979 consultants in 55 countries and 180 service delivery centres across the world, the company has been recognized as a top employer in six continents. With the ability to rapidly apply and scale new technologies, the company has built long term partnerships with its clients – helping them emerge as perpetually adaptive enterprises. Many of these relationships have endured into decades and navigated every technology cycle, from mainframes in the 1970s to Artificial Intelligence today. [...] PRESS RELEASE\n\nNEWYORK | MUMBAI, MAY 06, 2025: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS) a leading global IT services, consulting, and business solutions organization, has released the latest version of TCS MasterCraft™ augmented with GenAI and Agentic AI. This solution automates the process of modernizing legacy applications, thereby significantly reducing the cost and time required for manual conversions. The GenAI and Agentic AI enhancement will allow organizations to mine business logic faster and more accurately. The automation driven by the latest version of TCS MasterCraft can save over 70% in costs and achieve results twice as fast as traditional interventions. [...] Since 2012, TCS MasterCraft has been helping customers transform their legacy systems into modern, scalable, and cloud-native applications, ensuring that they can continuously evolve and keep up with changing business needs. Over the last decade, the offering has adapted to new technological advances and incorporated disruptive tech to offer the best-in-class solutions to enterprises across industries. Now, a layer of artificial intelligence boosts this automation-driven modernization solution. The Agentic AI augmentation brings together the vast TCS knowledge base, best practices and purposive agents to solve challenges that are unique to different types of complex modernization programs.",

      "url": "https://www.tcs.com/content/tcs/global/en/who-we-are/newsroom/press-release/tcs-launches-genai-agentic-ai-powered-tcs-mastercraft-help-enterprises-swiftly-modernize-legacy-systems",
      "title": "TCS Launches GenAI and Agentic AI-Powered TCS MasterCraft™ to Help Enterprises Swiftly Modernize Legacy Systems",
      "content": "Title: TCS Launches GenAI and Agentic AI-Powered TCS MasterCraft™ to Help Enterprises Swiftly Modernize Legacy Systems\n09 Mar 2026 TCS Named a Leader in Artificial Intelligence and Generative AI Services by Everest Group. ### Find the latest news about TCS. 18 Mar 2026 TCS and Pearson partner to accelerate AI-powered learning for global industries. 09 Mar 2026 TCS launches Gemini Experience Center in the US to help accelerate AI-powered manufacturing. Find the latest news about TCS;)- Press Releases. # TCS Launches GenAI and Agentic AI-Powered TCS MasterCraft™ to Help Enterprises Swiftly Modernize Legacy Systems. *The Gen AI and Agentic AI powered TCS MasterCraft™ reduces modernization cost by over 70% and achieve results twice as fast as traditional interventions*. **NEWYORK | MUMBAI, MAY 06, 2025:** Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS) a leading global IT services, consulting, and business solutions organization, has released the latest version of TCS MasterCraft™ augmented with GenAI and Agentic AI. A solution like TCS MasterCraft™ powered by GenAI and Agentic-AI reduces the risk of obsolescence and ensures sustainable success in the market. TCS is also using the latest Gen AI and Agentic AI-powered TCS MasterCraft to migrate over 50 million lines of legacy COBOL code to Java, a modern language, **for a global financial services** **major** in the US. TCS’ comprehensive AI and automation suite, TCS MasterCraft, supports enterprises in modernizing mainframe applications, facilitating monolithic application rearchitecting to microservices, and optimizing mainframe workloads. **Ashvini Saxena, VP and Head, TCS Components Engineering Group and Digital Software and Solutions** said, “*Enterprises have large applications in multiple technologies and architectures over decades. Since 2012, TCS MasterCraft has been helping customers transform their legacy systems into modern, scalable, and cloud-native applications, ensuring that they can continuously evolve and keep up with changing business needs. The Agentic AI augmentation brings together the vast TCS knowledge base, best practices and purposive agents to solve challenges that are unique to different types of complex modernization programs. The new version of TCS MasterCraft is part of TCS’ broader focus on enabling AI-led solutions that can simplify and optimize complex business processes. TCS Expands Strategic Partnership with Zscaler to Redefine Enterprise Workspace Innovation with New AI-powered Solution. TCS and ServiceNow partner to accelerate large-scale AI adoption for enterprises.",
      "score": 0.6780931,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.tcs.com/what-we-do/industries/manufacturing/solution/tcs-manufacturing-ai-agentic-futures",
      "title": "TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures",
      "content": "Title: TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures\n14 Apr 2026 Harnessing the Power of AI at TCS Life Sciences Forum 2026 New York. 16 Mar 2026 Experience the Future of AI with TCS at NVIDIA GTC 2026. 11 Feb 2026 TCS and Honeywell Collaborate to Enhance Autonomous Operations for Buildings and Industries with AI. # TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures. Share TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures. As the manufacturing value chain faces disruptions, know how TCS’ framework drives innovation and AI technology in manufacturing for better agility and growth. Experience the Future of AI with TCS at NVIDIA GTC 2026. Join TCS at NVIDIA GTC 2026 and explore the next wave of AI innovation spanning physical and agentic AI that help enterprises learn, decide, and act at speed. * The offering combines domain expertise with cutting-edge AI to support everything from rapid agent creation to real-time orchestration and simulation. * TCS offers more than technology—we bring a comprehensive strategy, robust frameworks, and deep manufacturing expertise to help clients build truly agentic enterprises. Manufacturing is entering a new era—one defined not just by automation, but by intelligent collaboration between humans and AI agents. TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures enables this transformation through a powerful, future-facing framework. It empowers enterprises to deploy autonomous, role-aware AI agents that understand context, make decisions, and act across the value chain. ## TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures. TCS provides a strategic roadmap to help manufacturers transition to an agentic enterprise—starting with AI readiness and culminating in ROI realization. It combines domain expertise with cutting-edge AI to support everything from rapid agent creation to real-time orchestration and simulation. * 100+ vertical and horizontal AI agents across manufacturing functions. TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures delivers both strategic and operational value—helping enterprises move from isolated AI use cases to intelligent, interconnected systems. TCS offers more than technology—we bring a comprehensive strategy, robust frameworks, and deep manufacturing expertise to help clients build truly agentic enterprises. With TCS, manufacturers gain not only AI-powered capabilities but also the confidence to scale them securely, ethically, and effectively. Generative AI and IIoT – Powering the Future of Manufacturing. Bridging the knowledge gap with generative AI in manufacturing.",

{
  "query": "McKinsey agentic AI HR",
